shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.23 (3.23 to 3.24)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.24 (23-24): If I were to cease performing My prescribed duties, all these worlds would fall into disorder. I would then become the cause of the chaos that would take place, thereby destroying the peace of all beings.
shlokaList.name: If I were to cease performing My prescribed duties, all these worlds would fall into disorder. I would then become the cause of the chaos that would take place, thereby destroying the peace of all beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryām karma ched aham
sankarasya cha kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.24 (3.23 to 3.24)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.25: O scion of Bharata! As an ignorant person acts with attachment to the results of their actions, a wise person should similarly act but without being attached to the results, solely for the purpose of leading people on the right path.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata! As an ignorant person acts with attachment to the results of their actions, a wise person should similarly act but without being attached to the results, solely for the purpose of leading people on the right path.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
saktāḥ karmaṇyavidvānso yathā kurvanti bhārata
kuryād vidvāns tathāsaktaśh chikīrṣhur loka-saṅgraham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.25

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.26: A wise individual should not disturb the minds of those attached to the results of their actions by asking them to stop work. Instead, they should inspire them to perform their duties diligently by performing their own duties perfectly.
shlokaList.name: A wise individual should not disturb the minds of those attached to the results of their actions by asking them to stop work. Instead, they should inspire them to perform their duties diligently by performing their own duties perfectly.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na buddhi-bhedam janayed ajñānām karma-saṅginām
joṣhayet sarva-karmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācharan
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.26

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.27: All activities are performed by the three qualities of material nature. But one illusioned by ego thinks oneself as the only doer.
shlokaList.name: All activities are performed by the three qualities of material nature. But one illusioned by ego thinks oneself as the only doer.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prakṛiteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśhaḥ
ahankāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.27

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.28: But an enlightened person, O mighty-armed one, understands that it’s only the senses which are engaged with the sense objects, and both are products of the gunas. Thus, understanding that it’s only the gunas which are active, they do not become attached.
shlokaList.name: But an enlightened person, O mighty-armed one, understands that it’s only the senses which are engaged with the sense objects, and both are products of the gunas. Thus, understanding that it’s only the gunas which are active, they do not become attached.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tattva-vit tu mahā-bāho guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ
guṇā guṇeṣhu vartanta iti matvā na sajjate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.28

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.29: Those deluded by the three gunas become attached to actions influenced by these qualities. But those with true knowledge should not disturb those who do not have the right understanding.
shlokaList.name: Those deluded by the three gunas become attached to actions influenced by these qualities. But those with true knowledge should not disturb those who do not have the right understanding.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prakṛiter guṇa-sammūḍhāḥ sajjante guṇa-karmasu
tān akṛitsna-vido mandān kṛitsna-vin na vichālayet
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.29

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.30: Surrendering all your actions unto Me, with your mind fixed on the knowledge of the self, free from desires and possessiveness, dispel the fever of confusion and engage in battle.
shlokaList.name: Surrendering all your actions unto Me, with your mind fixed on the knowledge of the self, free from desires and possessiveness, dispel the fever of confusion and engage in battle.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasā
nirāśhīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.30

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.31: Those who follow this teaching of Mine with utmost reverence, devoid of envy, become liberated from the bondage of action.
shlokaList.name: Those who follow this teaching of Mine with utmost reverence, devoid of envy, become liberated from the bondage of action.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye me matam idam nityam anutiṣhṭhanti mānavāḥ
śhraddhāvanto ’nasūyanto muchyante te ’pi karmabhiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.31

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.32: However, those who out of envy disregard My teachings and do not follow them, know that they are devoid of all true knowledge and they are ruined.
shlokaList.name: However, those who out of envy disregard My teachings and do not follow them, know that they are devoid of all true knowledge and they are ruined.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye tvetad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣhṭhanti me matam
sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāns tān viddhi naṣhṭān achetasaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.32

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.33: Even the wise man acts according to his inherent nature. All living beings are driven by the qualities that they have acquired from material nature. Then what can one achieve by repression?
shlokaList.name: Even the wise man acts according to his inherent nature. All living beings are driven by the qualities that they have acquired from material nature. Then what can one achieve by repression?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sadṛiśham cheṣhṭate svasyāḥ prakṛiter jñānavān api
prakṛitim yānti bhūtāni nigrahaḥ kim kariṣhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.33

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.34: The senses naturally incline towards their respective objects with feelings of attachment and aversion. Therefore, one should not be enslaved by the senses as they obstruct the path to supreme wisdom.
shlokaList.name: The senses naturally incline towards their respective objects with feelings of attachment and aversion. Therefore, one should not be enslaved by the senses as they obstruct the path to supreme wisdom.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
indriyasyendriyasyārthe rāga-dveṣhau vyavasthitau
tayor na vaśham āgachchhet tau hyasya paripanthinau
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.34

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.35: It is better to perform one`s own duty imperfectly than to perform another’s duty perfectly. It is better to die in the line of one`s own duty because performing the duty of others is fraught with danger.
shlokaList.name: It is better to perform one`s own duty imperfectly than to perform another’s duty perfectly. It is better to die in the line of one`s own duty because performing the duty of others is fraught with danger.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhreyān swa-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣhṭhitāt
swa-dharme nidhanam śhreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.35

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.36: Arjuna said - O descendant of Vrshni! What is it that impels a person to commit sinful acts even against their will as if driven by some force?
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O descendant of Vrshni! What is it that impels a person to commit sinful acts even against their will as if driven by some force?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
atha kena prayukto ’yam pāpam charati pūruṣhaḥ
anichchhann api vārṣhṇeya balād iva niyojitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.36

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.37: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - It is lust or desire only, which arises from rajo guna and later transforms into anger. This lust is insatiable and very sinful. Know it to be the worst enemy of humanity.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - It is lust or desire only, which arises from rajo guna and later transforms into anger. This lust is insatiable and very sinful. Know it to be the worst enemy of humanity.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
kāma eṣha krodha eṣha rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ
mahāśhano mahā-pāpmā viddhyenam iha vairiṇam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.37

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.38: Just as fire is covered by smoke, a mirror is covered by dust and an embryo is covered by the womb, similarly everything is enveloped by lust.
shlokaList.name: Just as fire is covered by smoke, a mirror is covered by dust and an embryo is covered by the womb, similarly everything is enveloped by lust.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dhūmenāvriyate vahnir yathādarśho malena cha
yatholbenāvṛito garbhas tathā tenedam āvṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.38

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.39: O Kaunteya! Even the wisdom of a wise person is obscured by this eternal enemy, which manifests as lust and which is insatiable and burns like fire.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! Even the wisdom of a wise person is obscured by this eternal enemy, which manifests as lust and which is insatiable and burns like fire.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
āvṛitam jñānam etena jñānino nitya-vairiṇā
kāma-rūpeṇa kaunteya duṣhpūreṇānalena cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.39

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.40: Lust resides in the senses, mind and intellect. Through them lust clouds knowledge and bewilders the living beings.
shlokaList.name: Lust resides in the senses, mind and intellect. Through them lust clouds knowledge and bewilders the living beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
indriyāṇi mano buddhir asyādhiṣhṭhānam uchyate
etair vimohayatyeṣha jñānam āvṛitya dehinam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.40

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.41: Therefore, O best amongst the Bharatas, first restrain your senses, and then conquer this lust, which is the root cause of sin and is the destroyer of wisdom and realization.
shlokaList.name: Therefore, O best amongst the Bharatas, first restrain your senses, and then conquer this lust, which is the root cause of sin and is the destroyer of wisdom and realization.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tasmāt tvam indriyāṇyādau niyamya bharatarṣhabha
pāpmānam prajahi hyenam jñāna-vijñāna-nāśhanam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.41

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.42: The senses are superior to the body, the mind is superior to the senses, the intellect is superior to the mind, and the self (he) is superior to the intellect.
shlokaList.name: The senses are superior to the body, the mind is superior to the senses, the intellect is superior to the mind, and the self (he) is superior to the intellect.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
indriyāṇi parāṇyāhur indriyebhyaḥ param manaḥ
manasas tu parā buddhir yo buddheḥ paratas tu saḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.42

shlokaList.description: Shloka 3.43: Thus, understanding the self to be superior to intellect and controlling the mind by discerning intelligence, O mighty-armed one, conquer this formidable enemy in the form of lust.
shlokaList.name: Thus, understanding the self to be superior to intellect and controlling the mind by discerning intelligence, O mighty-armed one, conquer this formidable enemy in the form of lust.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
evam buddheḥ param buddhvā sanstabhyātmānam ātmanā
jahi śhatrum mahā-bāho kāma-rūpam durāsadam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 3.43

chapterList.description: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge
chapterList.name: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.1: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - This eternal knowledge of Yoga was imparted by Me to the Sun-God, Vivasvan, who then conveyed it to Manu and Manu in turn imparted it to Iksvaku.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - This eternal knowledge of Yoga was imparted by Me to the Sun-God, Vivasvan, who then conveyed it to Manu and Manu in turn imparted it to Iksvaku.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
imam vivasvate yogam proktavān aham avyayam
vivasvān manave prāha manur ikṣhvākave ’bravīt
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.2: O Parantapa, this great knowledge of Yoga has been handed down through generations to the saintly kings. But over time this knowledge was lost to the world.
shlokaList.name: O Parantapa, this great knowledge of Yoga has been handed down through generations to the saintly kings. But over time this knowledge was lost to the world.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
evam paramparā-prāptam imam rājarṣhayo viduḥ
sa kāleneha mahatā yogo naṣhṭaḥ parantapa
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.3: Now, once again, I am revealing that same ancient knowledge of Yoga to you because you are My devotee and My friend. This knowledge is the greatest of all secrets.
shlokaList.name: Now, once again, I am revealing that same ancient knowledge of Yoga to you because you are My devotee and My friend. This knowledge is the greatest of all secrets.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sa evāyam mayā te ’dya yogaḥ proktaḥ purātanaḥ
bhakto ’si me sakhā cheti rahasyam hyetad uttamam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.3

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.4: Arjuna said - Your birth is much after Vivasvan’s birth. Then how should I understand that you taught this Yoga to him in the beginning?
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - Your birth is much after Vivasvan’s birth. Then how should I understand that you taught this Yoga to him in the beginning?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
aparam bhavato janma param janma vivasvataḥ
katham etad vijānīyām tvam ādau proktavān iti
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.4

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.5: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Arjuna! I and You have undergone many, many births. While I remember all of them, you do not, O Parantapa!
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Arjuna! I and You have undergone many, many births. While I remember all of them, you do not, O Parantapa!
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
bahūni me vyatītāni janmāni tava chārjuna
tānyaham veda sarvāṇi na tvam vettha parantapa
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.5

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.6: Although I am birthless and have an imperishable body and I am also the Lord of all living beings, yet I manifest Myself through My divine potency.
shlokaList.name: Although I am birthless and have an imperishable body and I am also the Lord of all living beings, yet I manifest Myself through My divine potency.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ajo ’pi sannavyayātmā bhūtānām īśhvaro ’pi san
prakṛitim svām adhiṣhṭhāya sambhavāmyātma-māyayā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.7: O scion of Bharata! Whenever there is a decline in dharma and a predominance of adharma, at that time I manifest myself.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata! Whenever there is a decline in dharma and a predominance of adharma, at that time I manifest myself.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānam sṛijāmyaham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.8: For the protection of the virtuous, the destruction of the wicked and the establishment of dharma, I appear again and again in every yuga.
shlokaList.name: For the protection of the virtuous, the destruction of the wicked and the establishment of dharma, I appear again and again in every yuga.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
paritrāṇāya sādhūnām vināśhāya cha duṣhkṛitām
dharma-sansthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.9: O Arjuna! Whoever truly understands the divine nature of My birth and activities does not take birth again in this material world after leaving the body, but instead attains Me.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna! Whoever truly understands the divine nature of My birth and activities does not take birth again in this material world after leaving the body, but instead attains Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
janma karma cha me divyam evam yo vetti tattvataḥ
tyaktvā deham punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.10: Freed from attachment, fear and anger, being fully absorbed in Me and taking refuge in Me, many, many people in the past have attained My supreme nature after being purified by this austerity of knowledge.
shlokaList.name: Freed from attachment, fear and anger, being fully absorbed in Me and taking refuge in Me, many, many people in the past have attained My supreme nature after being purified by this austerity of knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhā man-mayā mām upāśhritāḥ
bahavo jñāna-tapasā pūtā mad-bhāvam āgatāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.11: O Partha! In whatever way people approach Me, I reward them accordingly. Everyone follows My path in all respects.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! In whatever way people approach Me, I reward them accordingly. Everyone follows My path in all respects.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye yathā mām prapadyante tāns tathaiva bhajāmyaham
mama vartmānuvartante manuṣhyāḥ pārtha sarvaśhaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.11

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.12: In this world, people desiring success in their endeavors, worship various celestial beings. And thus, people get quick results in their endeavors.
shlokaList.name: In this world, people desiring success in their endeavors, worship various celestial beings. And thus, people get quick results in their endeavors.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kāṅkṣhantaḥ karmaṇām siddhim yajanta iha devatāḥ
kṣhipram hi mānuṣhe loke siddhir bhavati karmajā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.12

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.13: I have created four divisions in human society according to their qualities and activities. And although I have created this system, know that I am still the non-doer and unchangeable.
shlokaList.name: I have created four divisions in human society according to their qualities and activities. And although I have created this system, know that I am still the non-doer and unchangeable.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
chātur-varṇyam mayā sṛiṣhṭam guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśhaḥ
tasya kartāram api mām viddhyakartāram avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.13

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.14: Neither do actions bind Me, nor do I have any desire for the fruits of action. One who understands this truth about Me is also not bound by the results of their actions.
shlokaList.name: Neither do actions bind Me, nor do I have any desire for the fruits of action. One who understands this truth about Me is also not bound by the results of their actions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na mām karmāṇi limpanti na me karma-phale spṛihā
iti mām yo ’bhijānāti karmabhir na sa badhyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.14

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.15: In the past people seeking liberation performed their actions with this understanding. Therefore you should also perform your duties following their example.
shlokaList.name: In the past people seeking liberation performed their actions with this understanding. Therefore you should also perform your duties following their example.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
evam jñātvā kṛitam karma pūrvair api mumukṣhubhiḥ
kuru karmaiva tasmāttvam pūrvaiḥ pūrvataram kṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.16 (16-18): Even the wise are often perplexed in discerning what constitutes action and what constitutes inaction. Now I will clarify what true action is; understanding which you will be liberated from all suffering.
shlokaList.name: Even the wise are often perplexed in discerning what constitutes action and what constitutes inaction. Now I will clarify what true action is; understanding which you will be liberated from all suffering.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kim karma kim akarmeti kavayo ’pyatra mohitāḥ
tat te karma pravakṣhyāmi yaj jñātvā mokṣhyase ’śhubhāt
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.16 (4.16 to 4.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.17 (16-18): It is essential to understand what action is, what sinful action is and what inaction is. Indeed, knowledge about them is very difficult to understand.
shlokaList.name: It is essential to understand what action is, what sinful action is and what inaction is. Indeed, knowledge about them is very difficult to understand.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
karmaṇo hyapi boddhavyam boddhavyam cha vikarmaṇaḥ
akarmaṇaśh cha boddhavyam gahanā karmaṇo gatiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.17 (4.16 to 4.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.18 (16-18): One who sees inaction in action and action in inaction, is truly intelligent among humans. He is not affected by the results, even though such a person is engaged in a variety of work.
shlokaList.name: One who sees inaction in action and action in inaction, is truly intelligent among humans. He is not affected by the results, even though such a person is engaged in a variety of work.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
karmaṇyakarma yaḥ paśhyed akarmaṇi cha karma yaḥ
sa buddhimān manuṣhyeṣhu sa yuktaḥ kṛitsna-karma-kṛit
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.18 (4.16 to 4.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.19: He whose every action is free from any selfish desire and who has burnt the reactions of their actions in the fire of knowledge, is known as a wise man by the enlightened beings.
shlokaList.name: He whose every action is free from any selfish desire and who has burnt the reactions of their actions in the fire of knowledge, is known as a wise man by the enlightened beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yasya sarve samārambhāḥ kāma-saṅkalpa-varjitāḥ
jñānāgni-dagdha-karmāṇam tam āhuḥ paṇḍitam budhāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.19

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.20: Renouncing attachment to the fruits of actions, such a person always remains content and seeks no external support. And even while engaged in all kinds of action, they actually do nothing.
shlokaList.name: Renouncing attachment to the fruits of actions, such a person always remains content and seeks no external support. And even while engaged in all kinds of action, they actually do nothing.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tyaktvā karma-phalāsaṅgam nitya-tṛipto nirāśhrayaḥ
karmaṇyabhipravṛitto ’pi naiva kiñchit karoti saḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.20

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.21: When a man performs actions without any desire for the results, with a controlled mind, giving up all sense of possessiveness, acting only to maintain the body, he incurs no sin.
shlokaList.name: When a man performs actions without any desire for the results, with a controlled mind, giving up all sense of possessiveness, acting only to maintain the body, he incurs no sin.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nirāśhīr yata-chittātmā tyakta-sarva-parigrahaḥ
śhārīram kevalam karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.21

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.22: Being content with whatever spontaneously comes their way, free from envy and beyond life’s dualities, such people are equipoised in success and failure and are not bound by their actions.
shlokaList.name: Being content with whatever spontaneously comes their way, free from envy and beyond life’s dualities, such people are equipoised in success and failure and are not bound by their actions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yadṛichchhā-lābha-santuṣhṭo dvandvātīto vimatsaraḥ
samaḥ siddhāvasiddhau cha kṛitvāpi na nibadhyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.22

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.23 (23-24): He who is free from attachments, has a mind established in knowledge and performs duties as an act of sacrifice, he is freed from all reactions of work. Such a person’s actions begin and end in the Supreme.
shlokaList.name: He who is free from attachments, has a mind established in knowledge and performs duties as an act of sacrifice, he is freed from all reactions of work. Such a person’s actions begin and end in the Supreme.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
gata-saṅgasya muktasya jñānāvasthita-chetasaḥ
yajñāyācharataḥ karma samagram pravilīyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.23 (4.23 to 4.24)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.24 (23-24): In the sacrifices that they perform (yajna), the offering is Brahman, the clarified butter is Brahman, the fire is Brahman, and it is offered by a person who is also of the nature of Brahman. Such a person who while working, is absorbed in Brahman, reaches Brahman.
shlokaList.name: In the sacrifices that they perform (yajna), the offering is Brahman, the clarified butter is Brahman, the fire is Brahman, and it is offered by a person who is also of the nature of Brahman. Such a person who while working, is absorbed in Brahman, reaches Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
brahmārpaṇam brahma havir brahmāgnau brahmaṇā hutam
brahmaiva tena gantavyam brahma-karma-samādhinā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.24 (4.23 to 4.24)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.25 (25-27): Some Yogis perfectly worship celestial deities through sacrificial offerings, while others offer sacrifice into the fire of eternal Brahman.
shlokaList.name: Some Yogis perfectly worship celestial deities through sacrificial offerings, while others offer sacrifice into the fire of eternal Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
daivam evāpare yajñam yoginaḥ paryupāsate
brahmāgnāvapare yajñam yajñenaivopajuhvati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.25 (4.25 to 4.27)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.26 (25-27): Some offer their senses like hearing into the fire of self-control, while others, offer sound and other sense objects into the fire of the senses.
shlokaList.name: Some offer their senses like hearing into the fire of self-control, while others, offer sound and other sense objects into the fire of the senses.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrotrādīnīndriyāṇyanye sanyamāgniṣhu juhvati
śhabdādīn viṣhayānanya indriyāgniṣhu juhvati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.26 (4.25 to 4.27)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.27 (25-27): Some, who are illuminated by knowledge, offer the function of all their senses and their life air into the fire of self-control.
shlokaList.name: Some, who are illuminated by knowledge, offer the function of all their senses and their life air into the fire of self-control.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarvāṇīndriya-karmāṇi prāṇa-karmāṇi chāpare
ātma-sanyama-yogāgnau juhvati jñāna-dīpite
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.27 (4.25 to 4.27)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.28 (28-29): Some achieve enlightenment by offering their wealth & possessions in sacrifice, some by performing austerities as an act of sacrifice, some by practicing yoga of self-discipline and some others by studying the scriptures and Vedas, under strict vows.
shlokaList.name: Some achieve enlightenment by offering their wealth & possessions in sacrifice, some by performing austerities as an act of sacrifice, some by practicing yoga of self-discipline and some others by studying the scriptures and Vedas, under strict vows.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dravya-yajñās tapo-yajñā yoga-yajñās tathāpare
swādhyāya-jñāna-yajñāśh cha yatayaḥ sanśhita-vratāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.28 (4.28 to 4.29)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.29 (28-29): Others are drawn to the practice of breath control to achieve a state of trance. They focus on offering the incoming breath into the outgoing and outgoing breath into the incoming. Thus, checking the inward and outward movement of breath, they ultimately achieve trance, ceasing all breathing. Others regulate their diet and offer their life breaths into itself as a sacrifice.
shlokaList.name: Others are drawn to the practice of breath control to achieve a state of trance. They focus on offering the incoming breath into the outgoing and outgoing breath into the incoming. Thus, checking the inward and outward movement of breath, they ultimately achieve trance, ceasing all breathing. Others regulate their diet and offer their life breaths into itself as a sacrifice.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
apāne juhvati prāṇam prāṇe ’pānam tathāpare
prāṇāpāna-gatī ruddhvā prāṇāyāma-parāyaṇāḥ
apare niyatāhārāḥ prāṇān prāṇeṣhu juhvati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.29 (4.28 to 4.29)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.30: For those who are aware of this science of sacrifice, these sacrifices purify them of all sinful reactions. And because they partake of the nectarean remnants of these sacrifices, they attain the Supreme Brahman.
shlokaList.name: For those who are aware of this science of sacrifice, these sacrifices purify them of all sinful reactions. And because they partake of the nectarean remnants of these sacrifices, they attain the Supreme Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarve ’pyete yajña-vido yajña-kṣhapita-kalmaṣhāḥ
yajña-śiṣṭāmṛta-bhujo yānti brahma sanātanam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.30

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.31: O best of the Kurus, for those who do not perform sacrifices, there is no happiness in this world or in this life, then what to speak of the next?
shlokaList.name: O best of the Kurus, for those who do not perform sacrifices, there is no happiness in this world or in this life, then what to speak of the next?
shlokaList.sanskritText: nāyaṁ loko ’styayajñasya kuto ’nyaḥ kuru-sattama
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.31

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.32: All these various sacrifices are described in the Vedas. They are all born of different types of work. Knowing this, you will be liberated from all material bondage.
shlokaList.name: All these various sacrifices are described in the Vedas. They are all born of different types of work. Knowing this, you will be liberated from all material bondage.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
evam bahu-vidhā yajñā vitatā brahmaṇo mukhe
karma-jān viddhi tān sarvān evam jñātvā vimokṣhyase
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.32

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.33: O Parantapa, sacrifice performed with knowledge is superior to just sacrifice of material wealth and possessions. O Partha, ultimately all acts of sacrifices culminate in knowledge.
shlokaList.name: O Parantapa, sacrifice performed with knowledge is superior to just sacrifice of material wealth and possessions. O Partha, ultimately all acts of sacrifices culminate in knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhreyān dravya-mayād yajñāj jñāna-yajñaḥ parantapa
sarvam karmākhilam pārtha jñāne parisamāpyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.33

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.34: Approach a knowledgeable teacher, inquire from him with humility and offer service unto them. The wise seers of truth will impart that knowledge unto you because they have realized the truth.
shlokaList.name: Approach a knowledgeable teacher, inquire from him with humility and offer service unto them. The wise seers of truth will impart that knowledge unto you because they have realized the truth.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśhnena sevayā
upadekṣhyanti te jñānam jñāninas tattva-darśhinaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.34

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.35: Having realized the eternal truth, you will never fall into illusion again, O son of Pandu. Having achieved this knowledge, you will see all living beings as part of the Supreme and situated within Me.
shlokaList.name: Having realized the eternal truth, you will never fall into illusion again, O son of Pandu. Having achieved this knowledge, you will see all living beings as part of the Supreme and situated within Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yaj jñātvā na punar moham evam yāsyasi pāṇḍava
yena bhūtānyaśheṣheṇa drakṣhyasyātmanyatho mayi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.35

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.36 (36-37): Even if you are considered the most sinful amongst all sinners, you can still cross over the ocean of material suffering in the boat of divine knowledge.
shlokaList.name: Even if you are considered the most sinful amongst all sinners, you can still cross over the ocean of material suffering in the boat of divine knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
api ched asi pāpebhyaḥ sarvebhyaḥ pāpa-kṛit-tamaḥ
sarvam jñāna-plavenaiva vṛijinam santariṣhyasi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.36 (4.36 to 4.37)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.37 (36-37): O Arjuna, just as a blazing fire reduces wood to ashes, similarly the fire of knowledge burns all reactions of one’s actions to ashes.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna, just as a blazing fire reduces wood to ashes, similarly the fire of knowledge burns all reactions of one’s actions to ashes.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yathaidhānsi samiddho ’gnir bhasma-sāt kurute ’rjuna
jñānāgniḥ sarva-karmāṇi bhasma-sāt kurute tathā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.37 (4.36 to 4.37)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.38: There is nothing as pure as divine knowledge in this world. One who is established in the perfect stage of yoga, relishes this knowledge within themselves in due course of time.
shlokaList.name: There is nothing as pure as divine knowledge in this world. One who is established in the perfect stage of yoga, relishes this knowledge within themselves in due course of time.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na hi jñānena sadṛiśham pavitramiha vidyate
tatsvayam yogasansiddhaḥ kālenātmani vindati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.38

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.39: One who has faith in this divine knowledge and being completely dedicated to it controls his mind and senses, he becomes enlightened. And after attaining this divine knowledge one very soon attains supreme peace.
shlokaList.name: One who has faith in this divine knowledge and being completely dedicated to it controls his mind and senses, he becomes enlightened. And after attaining this divine knowledge one very soon attains supreme peace.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhraddhāvānllabhate jñānam tat-paraḥ sanyatendriyaḥ
jñānam labdhvā parām śhāntim achireṇādhigachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.39

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.40: Those who are ignorant, or lack faith, or are doubtful, they eventually perish. For those who are doubtful, there is no happiness in this world or the next.
shlokaList.name: Those who are ignorant, or lack faith, or are doubtful, they eventually perish. For those who are doubtful, there is no happiness in this world or the next.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ajñaśh chāśhraddadhānaśh cha sanśhayātmā vinaśhyati
nāyam loko ’sti na paro na sukham sanśhayātmanaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.40

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.41 (41-42): O Dhananjaya! One who has renounced the fruits of one’s work, whose doubts are dispelled by divine knowledge, and who is situated in the knowledge of the self, is not bound by the results of one’s actions.
shlokaList.name: O Dhananjaya! One who has renounced the fruits of one’s work, whose doubts are dispelled by divine knowledge, and who is situated in the knowledge of the self, is not bound by the results of one’s actions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yoga-sannyasta-karmāṇam jñāna-sañchhinna-sanśhayam
ātmavantam na karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañjaya
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.41 (4.41 to 4.42)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 4.42 (41-42): O scion of Bharata! Therefore, dispel all confusion from your heart born of ignorance, with the sword of divine knowledge. Establish yourself in yoga and stand up to fight.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata! Therefore, dispel all confusion from your heart born of ignorance, with the sword of divine knowledge. Establish yourself in yoga and stand up to fight.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tasmād ajñāna-sambhūtam hṛit-stham jñānāsinātmanaḥ
chhittvainam sanśhayam yogam ātiṣhṭhottiṣhṭha bhārata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 4.42 (4.41 to 4.42)

chapterList.description: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation
chapterList.name: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.1: Arjuna said - O Krishna! You have praised the path of sannyasa, renunciation of action and also the path of Karma Yoga the path of action without attachment to the results. But between these two, which one is superior? Please tell me definitively.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Krishna! You have praised the path of sannyasa, renunciation of action and also the path of Karma Yoga the path of action without attachment to the results. But between these two, which one is superior? Please tell me definitively.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
sannyāsam karmaṇām kṛiṣhṇa punar yogam cha śhansasi
yach chhreya etayor ekam tan me brūhi su-niśhchitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.2: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Sannyasa, renunciation of action and Karma Yoga, action without attachment to results, both lead to the path of liberation. However, between the two, the path of Karma Yoga is better than Sannyasa.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Sannyasa, renunciation of action and Karma Yoga, action without attachment to results, both lead to the path of liberation. However, between the two, the path of Karma Yoga is better than Sannyasa.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
sannyāsaḥ karma-yogaśh cha niḥśhreyasa-karāvubhau
tayos tu karma-sannyāsāt karma-yogo viśhiṣhyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.3: O mighty-armed Arjuna, one who neither hates nor desires the results of actions should be considered as always renounced. He is unaffected by all dualities, and easily achieves liberation from bondage.
shlokaList.name: O mighty-armed Arjuna, one who neither hates nor desires the results of actions should be considered as always renounced. He is unaffected by all dualities, and easily achieves liberation from bondage.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jñeyaḥ sa nitya-sannyāsī yo na dveṣhṭi na kāṅkṣhati
nirdvandvo hi mahā-bāho sukham bandhāt pramuchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.3

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.4 (4-5): Those with limited understanding claim that Sankhya (the path of knowledge & renunciation) and Karma Yoga (the path of action) are different. But those who are wise declare that by applying completely to one of the paths, one can achieve the results of both.
shlokaList.name: Those with limited understanding claim that Sankhya (the path of knowledge & renunciation) and Karma Yoga (the path of action) are different. But those who are wise declare that by applying completely to one of the paths, one can achieve the results of both.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sānkhya-yogau pṛithag bālāḥ pravadanti na paṇḍitāḥ
ekamapyāsthitaḥ samyag ubhayor vindate phalam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.4 (5.4 to 5.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.5 (4-5): Whatever is achieved by following the path of Sankhya, that can also be gained by following the path of Karma Yoga. One who sees Sankhya and Karma Yoga as one, he actually sees everything as it is.
shlokaList.name: Whatever is achieved by following the path of Sankhya, that can also be gained by following the path of Karma Yoga. One who sees Sankhya and Karma Yoga as one, he actually sees everything as it is.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yat sānkhyaiḥ prāpyate sthānam tad yogair api gamyate
ekam sānkhyam cha yogam cha yaḥ paśhyati sa paśhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.5 (5.4 to 5.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.6: But, O mighty-armed Arjuna, true renunciation (sannyasa) is difficult to achieve without Karma Yoga. A thoughtful person, who practices Karma Yoga, can quickly attain the Supreme.
shlokaList.name: But, O mighty-armed Arjuna, true renunciation (sannyasa) is difficult to achieve without Karma Yoga. A thoughtful person, who practices Karma Yoga, can quickly attain the Supreme.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sannyāsas tu mahā-bāho duḥkham āptum ayogataḥ
yoga-yukto munir brahma na chireṇādhigachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.7: One who remains steadfast in the path of Karma Yoga is pure within, is self-controlled, has mastery over the mind and senses and sees all beings as equal to oneself, is never bound by actions despite engaging in them.
shlokaList.name: One who remains steadfast in the path of Karma Yoga is pure within, is self-controlled, has mastery over the mind and senses and sees all beings as equal to oneself, is never bound by actions despite engaging in them.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yoga-yukto viśhuddhātmā vijitātmā jitendriyaḥ
sarva-bhūtātma-bhūtātmā kurvann api na lipyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.8 (8-9): A person in divine consciousness, one who knows the truth, even while engaged in activities such as seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping and breathing, always realizes within himself that he is actually not the doer of these actions at all.
shlokaList.name: A person in divine consciousness, one who knows the truth, even while engaged in activities such as seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping and breathing, always realizes within himself that he is actually not the doer of these actions at all.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
naiva kiñchit karomīti yukto manyeta tattva-vit
paśhyañ śhṛiṇvan spṛiśhañjighrann aśhnangachchhan svapañśhvasan
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.8 (5.8 to 5.9)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.9 (8-9): While speaking, excreting, accepting, opening, and closing the eyes, a person who understands the truth, understands that the senses are interacting with sense objects.
shlokaList.name: While speaking, excreting, accepting, opening, and closing the eyes, a person who understands the truth, understands that the senses are interacting with sense objects.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pralapan visṛijan gṛihṇann unmiṣhan nimiṣhann api
indriyāṇīndriyārtheṣhu vartanta iti dhārayan
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.9 (5.8 to 5.9)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.10: A person who performs actions without attachment and offers the results of one’s work unto the Supreme, remains untouched by any sinful reaction, just like a lotus leaf remains untouched by water.
shlokaList.name: A person who performs actions without attachment and offers the results of one’s work unto the Supreme, remains untouched by any sinful reaction, just like a lotus leaf remains untouched by water.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
brahmaṇyādhāya karmāṇi saṅgam tyaktvā karoti yaḥ
lipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.11: Karma yogis perform actions using their body, mind, intellect and senses, renouncing attachment to the fruits of their actions, solely for self-purification.
shlokaList.name: Karma yogis perform actions using their body, mind, intellect and senses, renouncing attachment to the fruits of their actions, solely for self-purification.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kāyena manasā buddhyā kevalair indriyair api
yoginaḥ karma kurvanti saṅgam tyaktvātma-śhuddhaye
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.11

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.12: By letting go of attachment to the fruits of actions, a yogi attains uninterrupted peace. Conversely, one who is not a yogi, who is driven by desires and is attached to the fruits of actions, he becomes bound.
shlokaList.name: By letting go of attachment to the fruits of actions, a yogi attains uninterrupted peace. Conversely, one who is not a yogi, who is driven by desires and is attached to the fruits of actions, he becomes bound.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yuktaḥ karma-phalam tyaktvā śhāntim āpnoti naiṣhṭhikīm
ayuktaḥ kāma-kāreṇa phale sakto nibadhyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.12

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.13 (13-14): The self-controlled embodied being, who has mentally renounced attachment to all results, lives happily within the city of nine gates of the body, neither doing nor being the cause of anything.
shlokaList.name: The self-controlled embodied being, who has mentally renounced attachment to all results, lives happily within the city of nine gates of the body, neither doing nor being the cause of anything.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-karmāṇi manasā sannyasyāste sukham vaśhī
nava-dvāre pure dehī naiva kurvan na kārayan
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.13 (5.13 to 5.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.14 (13-14): The embodied living being, who is the master of this body, is neither the instigator of actions, nor causes others to act, nor does he create results of actions. The qualities of material nature (gunas) alone are responsible for these.
shlokaList.name: The embodied living being, who is the master of this body, is neither the instigator of actions, nor causes others to act, nor does he create results of actions. The qualities of material nature (gunas) alone are responsible for these.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na kartṛitvam na karmāṇi lokasya sṛijati prabhuḥ
na karma-phala-saṅyogam svabhāvas tu pravartate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.14 (5.13 to 5.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.15: The omnipresent Supreme Self neither partakes in the sins nor the merits of anyone. It is the veil of ignorance that covers the real knowledge and bewilders the embodied beings.
shlokaList.name: The omnipresent Supreme Self neither partakes in the sins nor the merits of anyone. It is the veil of ignorance that covers the real knowledge and bewilders the embodied beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nādatte kasyachit pāpam na chaiva sukṛitam vibhuḥ
ajñānenāvṛitam jñānam tena muhyanti jantavaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.16: But when the ignorance of the embodied being is dispelled by divine knowledge, then that knowledge reveals the Supreme, just like the sun illuminates everything.
shlokaList.name: But when the ignorance of the embodied being is dispelled by divine knowledge, then that knowledge reveals the Supreme, just like the sun illuminates everything.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jñānena tu tad ajñānam yeṣhām nāśhitam ātmanaḥ
teṣhām āditya-vaj jñānam prakāśhayati tat param
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.16

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.17: Those whose intelligence, mind and faith are focused on the Supreme and they are dedicated to the Supreme, they get liberated and go to a place of no return, being purified of all impurities through divine knowledge.
shlokaList.name: Those whose intelligence, mind and faith are focused on the Supreme and they are dedicated to the Supreme, they get liberated and go to a place of no return, being purified of all impurities through divine knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tad-buddhayas tad-ātmānas tan-niṣhṭhās tat-parāyaṇāḥ
gachchhantyapunar-āvṛittim jñāna-nirdhūta-kalmaṣhāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.17

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.18: A humble person, fully equipped with divine knowledge, views all beings with an equal vision, whether they be a learned Brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, or an outcaste.
shlokaList.name: A humble person, fully equipped with divine knowledge, views all beings with an equal vision, whether they be a learned Brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, or an outcaste.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini
śhuni chaiva śhva-pāke cha paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśhinaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.18

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.19 (19-20): Those whose minds are established in equanimity, conquer the cycle of birth and death in this life itself. They are flawless like Brahman, and thus they are indeed established in Brahman.
shlokaList.name: Those whose minds are established in equanimity, conquer the cycle of birth and death in this life itself. They are flawless like Brahman, and thus they are indeed established in Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ihaiva tair jitaḥ sargo yeṣhām sāmye sthitam manaḥ
nirdoṣham hi samam brahma tasmād brahmaṇi te sthitāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.19 (5.19 to 5.20)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.20 (19-20): A person who neither rejoices in favourable situations nor grieves in unfavourable ones, one whose intelligence is stable, who is not deluded, one who knows Brahman is considered to be actually situated in Brahman.
shlokaList.name: A person who neither rejoices in favourable situations nor grieves in unfavourable ones, one whose intelligence is stable, who is not deluded, one who knows Brahman is considered to be actually situated in Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na prahṛiṣhyet priyam prāpya nodvijet prāpya chāpriyam
sthira-buddhir asammūḍho brahma-vid brahmaṇi sthitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.20 (5.19 to 5.20)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.21: One who is detached from external sense objects, finding happiness within, united with Brahman through Yoga enjoys unending pleasure.
shlokaList.name: One who is detached from external sense objects, finding happiness within, united with Brahman through Yoga enjoys unending pleasure.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bāhya-sparśheṣhvasaktātmā vindatyātmani yat sukham
sa brahma-yoga-yuktātmā sukham akṣhayam aśhnute
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.21

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.22: O Kaunteya! Pleasures born of contact with external sense objects are sources of suffering. Such pleasures are transient in nature, with a beginning and an end. Thus, the wise do not indulge in them.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! Pleasures born of contact with external sense objects are sources of suffering. Such pleasures are transient in nature, with a beginning and an end. Thus, the wise do not indulge in them.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye hi sansparśha-jā bhogā duḥkha-yonaya eva te
ādyantavantaḥ kaunteya na teṣhu ramate budhaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.22

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.23: Before giving up the body, one who is able to endure the urges arising from desire and anger, is actually a Yogi and is a happy man.
shlokaList.name: Before giving up the body, one who is able to endure the urges arising from desire and anger, is actually a Yogi and is a happy man.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhaknotīhaiva yaḥ soḍhum prāk śharīra-vimokṣhaṇāt
kāma-krodhodbhavam vegam sa yuktaḥ sa sukhī naraḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.23

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.24: One who finds happiness within, who rejoices within, who is illumined within, such a yogi is already liberated and ultimately he attains the Supreme Brahman.
shlokaList.name: One who finds happiness within, who rejoices within, who is illumined within, such a yogi is already liberated and ultimately he attains the Supreme Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yo 'ntaḥ-sukho 'ntar-ārāmas tathāntar-jyotir eva yaḥ
sa yogī brahma-nirvāṇam brahma-bhūto 'dhigachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.24

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.25: The thoughtful sages who are devoid of any sin, whose mental duality is destroyed, who are self-realized and who work for the welfare of all beings, attain liberation in the Supreme.
shlokaList.name: The thoughtful sages who are devoid of any sin, whose mental duality is destroyed, who are self-realized and who work for the welfare of all beings, attain liberation in the Supreme.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
labhante brahma-nirvāṇam ṛiṣhayaḥ kṣhīṇa-kalmaṣhāḥ
chhinna-dvaidhā yatātmānaḥ sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.25

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.26: One who is free from desire and anger, possessing self-knowledge, disciplined in thoughts, attain liberation in the Supreme in the very near future.
shlokaList.name: One who is free from desire and anger, possessing self-knowledge, disciplined in thoughts, attain liberation in the Supreme in the very near future.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kāma-krodha-viyuktānām yatīnām yata-chetasām
abhito brahma-nirvāṇam vartate viditātmanām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.26

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.27 (27-28): By limiting external sense interactions, directing the gaze away from external objects, and focusing it inward between the eyebrows, maintaining a balance of inhalation and exhalation within the nostrils;
shlokaList.name: By limiting external sense interactions, directing the gaze away from external objects, and focusing it inward between the eyebrows, maintaining a balance of inhalation and exhalation within the nostrils;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sparśhān kṛitvā bahir bāhyānśh chakṣhuśh chaivāntare bhruvoḥ
prāṇāpānau samau kṛitvā nāsābhyantara-chāriṇau
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.27 (5.27 to 5.28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.28 (27-28): With disciplined control over the senses, mind and intellect, the sage deeply focused on attaining liberation, becomes liberated from desire, fear and anger. Such a person is always liberated.
shlokaList.name: With disciplined control over the senses, mind and intellect, the sage deeply focused on attaining liberation, becomes liberated from desire, fear and anger. Such a person is always liberated.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yatendriya-mano-buddhir munir mokṣha-parāyaṇaḥ
vigatechchhā-bhaya-krodho yaḥ sadā mukta eva saḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.28 (5.27 to 5.28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 5.29: Knowing Me as the enjoyer of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme controller of all the worlds and the well-wishing friend of all living beings, one attains peace.
shlokaList.name: Knowing Me as the enjoyer of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme controller of all the worlds and the well-wishing friend of all living beings, one attains peace.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bhoktāram yajña-tapasām sarva-loka-maheśhvaram
suhṛidam sarva-bhūtānām jñātvā mām śhāntim ṛichchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 5.29

chapterList.description: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline
chapterList.name: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.1: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - One who performs their duties without desire for the fruits of actions is both a renunciant (sannyasi) and a yogi, and not one who has given up lighting the sacrificial fire as well as performing duties.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - One who performs their duties without desire for the fruits of actions is both a renunciant (sannyasi) and a yogi, and not one who has given up lighting the sacrificial fire as well as performing duties.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
anāśhritaḥ karma-phalam kāryam karma karoti yaḥ
sa sannyāsī cha yogī cha na niragnir na chākriyaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.2: O son of Pandu! Understand that what is referred to as renunciation is the same as yoga. Without renouncing selfish desires of enjoyment, one cannot truly become a yogi.
shlokaList.name: O son of Pandu! Understand that what is referred to as renunciation is the same as yoga. Without renouncing selfish desires of enjoyment, one cannot truly become a yogi.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yam sannyāsam iti prāhur yogam tam viddhi pāṇḍava
na hyasannyasta-saṅkalpo yogī bhavati kaśhchana
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.3 (3-4): For someone beginning on the path of yoga, action is considered the means to progress. However, once one has mastered the science of yoga, stopping all material activities of the senses is emphasized as the pathway to further advancement.
shlokaList.name: For someone beginning on the path of yoga, action is considered the means to progress. However, once one has mastered the science of yoga, stopping all material activities of the senses is emphasized as the pathway to further advancement.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ārurukṣhor muner yogam karma kāraṇam uchyate
yogārūḍhasya tasyaiva śhamaḥ kāraṇam uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.3 (6.3 to 6.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.4 (3-4): One is considered advanced on the path of yoga when having given up all material desires, he doesn’t act for the purpose of sense satisfaction nor for the purpose of enjoying the fruits of one’s work.
shlokaList.name: One is considered advanced on the path of yoga when having given up all material desires, he doesn’t act for the purpose of sense satisfaction nor for the purpose of enjoying the fruits of one’s work.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yadā hi nendriyārtheṣhu na karmasv-anuṣhajjate
sarva-saṅkalpa-sannyāsī yogārūḍhas tadochyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.4 (6.3 to 6.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.5: One should elevate oneself with the help of the mind and avoid degrading oneself. The mind can either be your friend or your foe.
shlokaList.name: One should elevate oneself with the help of the mind and avoid degrading oneself. The mind can either be your friend or your foe.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
uddhared ātmanātmānam nātmānam avasādayet
ātmaiva hyātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.5

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.6: Mind is the best friend of one who has conquered it, but mind is the greatest enemy of one who has failed to conquer it.
shlokaList.name: Mind is the best friend of one who has conquered it, but mind is the greatest enemy of one who has failed to conquer it.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ
anātmanas tu śhatrutve vartetātmaiva śhatru-vat
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.7: One who has mastered the mind and has attained a state of inner peace has realized the Supreme soul in his heart. Such a person remains unaffected by cold and heat, happiness and sorrow, honour and dishonour.
shlokaList.name: One who has mastered the mind and has attained a state of inner peace has realized the Supreme soul in his heart. Such a person remains unaffected by cold and heat, happiness and sorrow, honour and dishonour.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jitātmanaḥ praśhāntasya paramātmā samāhitaḥ
śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.8: One who is content with divine knowledge and wisdom, who is steadfast and has mastered control over all senses, who is self-situated and for whom a piece of clay, stone and gold hold equal value is said to be a yogi.
shlokaList.name: One who is content with divine knowledge and wisdom, who is steadfast and has mastered control over all senses, who is self-situated and for whom a piece of clay, stone and gold hold equal value is said to be a yogi.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jñāna-vijñāna-tṛiptātmā kūṭa-stho vijitendriyaḥ
yukta ityuchyate yogī sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.9: Someone who maintains equanimity towards close friends, acquaintances, adversaries, who remains impartial towards both detractors and supporters and sees equally both virtuous and sinful is truly admirable and to be considered further advanced in yoga.
shlokaList.name: Someone who maintains equanimity towards close friends, acquaintances, adversaries, who remains impartial towards both detractors and supporters and sees equally both virtuous and sinful is truly admirable and to be considered further advanced in yoga.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
suhṛin-mitrāryudāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣhya-bandhuṣhu
sādhuṣhvapi cha pāpeṣhu sama-buddhir viśhiṣhyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.10: A yogi should always engage in the practice of yoga with his body & mind, living alone in seclusion, carefully controlling his mind, free from any distractions and free from any feeling of possessiveness.
shlokaList.name: A yogi should always engage in the practice of yoga with his body & mind, living alone in seclusion, carefully controlling his mind, free from any distractions and free from any feeling of possessiveness.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānam rahasi sthitaḥ
ekākī yata-chittātmā nirāśhīr aparigrahaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.11 (11-12): To practice Yoga, one should prepare a seat in a clean area, choosing a spot that is neither too high nor too low. Layer the seat with Kusa grass, then a deer skin and finally, cover it with a soft cloth. The yogi should then sit firmly on this seat.
shlokaList.name: To practice Yoga, one should prepare a seat in a clean area, choosing a spot that is neither too high nor too low. Layer the seat with Kusa grass, then a deer skin and finally, cover it with a soft cloth. The yogi should then sit firmly on this seat.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhuchau deśhe pratiṣhṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ
nātyuchchhritam nāti-nīcham chailājina-kuśhottaram
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.11 (6.11 to 6.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.12 (11-12):Then sitting firmly on his seat, the yogi should focus the mind on one point. Keeping his mind and senses under full control, he should engage in the practice of yoga for the purification of the self.
shlokaList.name: Then sitting firmly on his seat, the yogi should focus the mind on one point. Keeping his mind and senses under full control, he should engage in the practice of yoga for the purification of the self.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tatraikāgram manaḥ kṛitvā yata-chittendriya-kriyaḥ
upaviśhyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśhuddhaye
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.12 (6.11 to 6.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.13 (13-14): Keeping the body, head and neck aligned and completely still, the practitioner should gaze steadily at the tip of their nose, avoiding distractions by not looking around.
shlokaList.name: Keeping the body, head and neck aligned and completely still, the practitioner should gaze steadily at the tip of their nose, avoiding distractions by not looking around.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
samam kāya-śhiro-grīvam dhārayann achalam sthiraḥ
samprekṣhya nāsikāgram svam diśhaśh chānavalokayan
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.13 (6.13 to 6.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.14 (13-14): With a tranquil mind devoid of fear, adhering to the vow of celibacy and with a subdued mind, a yogi should focus the mind on Me and make Me as his ultimate aspiration.
shlokaList.name: With a tranquil mind devoid of fear, adhering to the vow of celibacy and with a subdued mind, a yogi should focus the mind on Me and make Me as his ultimate aspiration.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
praśhāntātmā vigata-bhīr brahmachāri-vrate sthitaḥ
manaḥ sanyamya mach-chitto yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.14 (6.13 to 6.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.15: In this way, always practicing self-control and with a regulated mind, a yogi becomes free from material bondage, attains supreme peace and ultimately enters My supreme abode.
shlokaList.name: In this way, always practicing self-control and with a regulated mind, a yogi becomes free from material bondage, attains supreme peace and ultimately enters My supreme abode.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yuñjann evam sadātmānam yogī niyata-mānasaḥ
śhāntim nirvāṇa-paramām mat-sansthām adhigachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.16: O Arjuna! Yoga is not for those who overeat or eat very little, nor for those who sleep excessively or sleep inadequately.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna! Yoga is not for those who overeat or eat very little, nor for those who sleep excessively or sleep inadequately.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nātyaśhnatastu yogo ’sti na chaikāntam anaśhnataḥ
na chāti-svapna-śhīlasya jāgrato naiva chārjuna
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.16

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.17: For someone who is regulated in his eating, recreation, endeavors and sleep, the practice of yoga eliminates all material suffering.
shlokaList.name: For someone who is regulated in his eating, recreation, endeavors and sleep, the practice of yoga eliminates all material suffering.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yuktāhāra-vihārasya yukta-cheṣhṭasya karmasu
yukta-svapnāvabodhasya yogo bhavati duḥkha-hā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.17

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.18 (18-19): When the mind, free from all sorts of desires, becomes disciplined and remains firmly established in the self, then one is considered to be perfectly established in yoga.
shlokaList.name: When the mind, free from all sorts of desires, becomes disciplined and remains firmly established in the self, then one is considered to be perfectly established in yoga.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yadā viniyatam chittam ātmanyevāvatiṣhṭhate
niḥspṛihaḥ sarva-kāmebhyo yukta ityuchyate tadā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.18 (6.18 to 6.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.19 (18-19): Just as a lamp does not waver in a windless place, so does a yogi, whose mind is disciplined and fully controlled remains steadily engaged in meditating on the Supreme.
shlokaList.name: Just as a lamp does not waver in a windless place, so does a yogi, whose mind is disciplined and fully controlled remains steadily engaged in meditating on the Supreme.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛitā
yogino yata-chittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.19 (6.18 to 6.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.20 (20-23): When the mind, due to practice of yoga is controlled and thus ceases all material activities, then one can realize the self with the help of the mind and become completely satisfied in the self.
shlokaList.name: When the mind, due to practice of yoga is controlled and thus ceases all material activities, then one can realize the self with the help of the mind and become completely satisfied in the self.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yatroparamate chittam niruddham yoga-sevayā
yatra chaivātmanātmānam paśhyann ātmani tuṣhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.20 (6.20 to 6.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.21 (20-23): The highest level of happiness, which transcends the material senses can be realized through one’s intelligence. Once attained, a yogi remains firmly rooted in this ultimate truth, never straying from it again.
shlokaList.name: The highest level of happiness, which transcends the material senses can be realized through one’s intelligence. Once attained, a yogi remains firmly rooted in this ultimate truth, never straying from it again.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sukham ātyantikam yat tad buddhi-grāhyam atīndriyam
vetti yatra na chaivāyam sthitaśh chalati tattvataḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.21 (6.20 to 6.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.22 (20-23): After achieving this state, one doesn’t consider any other accomplishment greater than this. Remaining steadfast in this realization, one is unaffected even by the greatest sorrows.
shlokaList.name: After achieving this state, one doesn’t consider any other accomplishment greater than this. Remaining steadfast in this realization, one is unaffected even by the greatest sorrows.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yam labdhvā chāparam lābham manyate nādhikam tataḥ
yasmin sthito na duḥkhena guruṇāpi vichālyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.22 (6.20 to 6.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.23 (20-23): You must know that in this state of yoga, one is completely freed from all material suffering.
shlokaList.name: You must know that in this state of yoga, one is completely freed from all material suffering.
shlokaList.sanskritText: tam vidyād duḥkha-sanyoga-viyogam yogasaṅjñitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.23 (6.20 to 6.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.24: Therefore one must practice yoga with determination and a happy mind. By completely abandoning all desires that arise from the mind, one must controlall the senses in all situations using the mind.
shlokaList.name: Therefore one must practice yoga with determination and a happy mind. By completely abandoning all desires that arise from the mind, one must controlall the senses in all situations using the mind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sa niśhchayena yoktavyo yogo ’nirviṇṇa-chetasā
saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāns tyaktvā sarvān aśheṣhataḥ 
manasaivendriya-grāmam viniyamya samantataḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.24

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.25: Gradually the mind attains tranquility with the help of intelligence which is supported by deep conviction. Thus directing the mind towards the self, one should not let it dwell on anything other than the self.
shlokaList.name: Gradually the mind attains tranquility with the help of intelligence which is supported by deep conviction. Thus directing the mind towards the self, one should not let it dwell on anything other than the self.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
hanaiḥ śhanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛiti-gṛihītayā
ātma-sanstham manaḥ kṛitvā na kiñchid api chintayet
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.25

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.26: The mind may wander repeatedly here and there due to its restless, unsteady nature. But a yogi must regulate it and bring it back under the control of the self.
shlokaList.name: The mind may wander repeatedly here and there due to its restless, unsteady nature. But a yogi must regulate it and bring it back under the control of the self.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yato yato niśhcharati manaśh chañchalam asthiram
tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmanyeva vaśham nayet
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.26

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.27 (27-28): The supreme happiness belongs to the yogi whose mind has attained tranquility, whose passion has been subdued who is pure and has attained the stage of brahma-bhuta or liberation in the Supreme.
shlokaList.name: The supreme happiness belongs to the yogi whose mind has attained tranquility, whose passion has been subdued who is pure and has attained the stage of brahma-bhuta or liberation in the Supreme.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
praśhānta-manasam hyenam yoginam sukham uttamam
upaiti śhānta-rajasam brahma-bhūtam akalmaṣham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.27 (6.27 to 6.28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.28 (27-28): By thus always practicing yoga, the yogi is liberated from all impurities and remaining in constant touch with the Supreme, they effortlessly attain the highest happiness.
shlokaList.name: By thus always practicing yoga, the yogi is liberated from all impurities and remaining in constant touch with the Supreme, they effortlessly attain the highest happiness.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yuñjann evam sadātmānam yogī vigata-kalmaṣhaḥ
sukhena brahma-sansparśham atyantam sukham aśhnute
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.28 (6.27 to 6.28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.29: One who is united with the Supreme through yoga, perceives with an equal vision, the Supreme in all living beings and sees all living beings in the Supreme.
shlokaList.name: One who is united with the Supreme through yoga, perceives with an equal vision, the Supreme in all living beings and sees all living beings in the Supreme.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānam sarva-bhūtāni chātmani
īkṣhate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśhanaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.29

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.30: He who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, I never lose him, nor does he ever lose Me.
shlokaList.name: He who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, I never lose him, nor does he ever lose Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yo mām paśhyati sarvatra sarvam cha mayi paśhyati
tasyāham na praṇaśhyāmi sa cha me na praṇaśhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.30 (6.30 to 6.31)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.31: He who has attained the vision of oneness and worships Me, knowing that I reside within every living being, remains united with Me always in all situations.
shlokaList.name: He who has attained the vision of oneness and worships Me, knowing that I reside within every living being, remains united with Me always in all situations.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arva-bhūta-sthitam yo mām bhajatyekatvam āsthitaḥ
sarvathā vartamāno ’pi sa yogī mayi vartate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.31 (6.30 to 6.31)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.32: O Arjuna! In my opinion, the perfect yogi is one who in comparison to oneself sees everyone with an equal vision whether in their happiness or distress.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna! In my opinion, the perfect yogi is one who in comparison to oneself sees everyone with an equal vision whether in their happiness or distress.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ātmaupamyena sarvatra samam paśhyati yo ’rjuna
sukham vā yadi vā duḥkham sa yogī paramo mataḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.32

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.33: Arjuna said - O Madhusudana! The yoga system that you have described appears to be impractical and unrealistic to me because of the restless nature of the mind.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Madhusudana! The yoga system that you have described appears to be impractical and unrealistic to me because of the restless nature of the mind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
yo ’yam yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana
etasyāham na paśhyāmi chañchalatvāt sthitim sthirām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.33

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.34: O Krishna, indeed, the mind is restless, turbulent, strong and obstinate. I find controlling this mind to be more difficult than trying to control the wind.
shlokaList.name: O Krishna, indeed, the mind is restless, turbulent, strong and obstinate. I find controlling this mind to be more difficult than trying to control the wind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
chañchalam hi manaḥ kṛiṣhṇa pramāthi balavad dṛiḍham
tasyāham nigraham manye vāyor iva su-duṣhkaram
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.34

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.35: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O mighty-armed! Undoubtedly, the mind is truly difficult to control due to its flickering nature. However, O Kaunteya, with consistent practice and detachment, it can be controlled.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O mighty-armed! Undoubtedly, the mind is truly difficult to control due to its flickering nature. However, O Kaunteya, with consistent practice and detachment, it can be controlled.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
asanśhayam mahā-bāho mano durnigraham chalam
abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa cha gṛihyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.35

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.36: Yoga is challenging for those whose mind is unrestrained. However, those who have learned to control the mind and who diligently strive through proper means can achieve perfection in yoga. This is My opinion.
shlokaList.name: Yoga is challenging for those whose mind is unrestrained. However, those who have learned to control the mind and who diligently strive through proper means can achieve perfection in yoga. This is My opinion.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
asaṅyatātmanā yogo duṣhprāpa iti me matiḥ
vaśhyātmanā tu yatatā śhakyo ’vāptum upāyataḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.36

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.37: Arjuna said - O Krishna, what is the fate of an unsuccessful yogi who started on this path with faith but who later deviated because of the restless mind, thereby failing to attain the ultimate perfection of yoga?
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Krishna, what is the fate of an unsuccessful yogi who started on this path with faith but who later deviated because of the restless mind, thereby failing to attain the ultimate perfection of yoga?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
ayatiḥ śhraddhayopeto yogāch chalita-mānasaḥ
aprāpya yoga-sansiddhim kāṅ gatim kṛiṣhṇa gachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.37 (6.37 to 6.39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.38: Does not such a person, who deviates from the path of yoga, become deprived of both material and spiritual success, O mighty-armed Krishna! And perish like a torn cloud with no place in either realm?
shlokaList.name: Does not such a person, who deviates from the path of yoga, become deprived of both material and spiritual success, O mighty-armed Krishna! And perish like a torn cloud with no place in either realm?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kachchin nobhaya-vibhraṣhṭaśh chhinnābhram iva naśhyati
apratiṣhṭho mahā-bāho vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ pathi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.38 (6.37 to 6.39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.39: O Krishna, please completely dispel this doubt of mine. There is no one other than you who can remove this doubt.
shlokaList.name: O Krishna, please completely dispel this doubt of mine. There is no one other than you who can remove this doubt.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
etan me sanśhayam kṛiṣhṇa chhettum arhasyaśheṣhataḥ
tvad-anyaḥ sanśhayasyāsya chhettā na hyupapadyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.39 (6.37 to 6.39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.40: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Partha! There is no harm or destruction for such a person neither in this world nor in the next because those who perform good deeds are protected from all evil.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Partha! There is no harm or destruction for such a person neither in this world nor in the next because those who perform good deeds are protected from all evil.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
pārtha naiveha nāmutra vināśhas tasya vidyate
na hi kalyāṇa-kṛit kaśhchid durgatim tāta gachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.40

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.41: Even if someone deviates from the path of Yoga in this lifetime, after death they attain the realms accessible to the pious and reside there for a long duration. And then they are born into the homes of the pious or wealthy people.
shlokaList.name: Even if someone deviates from the path of Yoga in this lifetime, after death they attain the realms accessible to the pious and reside there for a long duration. And then they are born into the homes of the pious or wealthy people.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prāpya puṇya-kṛitām lokān uṣhitvā śhāśhvatīḥ samāḥ
śhuchīnām śhrīmatām gehe yoga-bhraṣhṭo ’bhijāyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.41 (6.41 to 6.42)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.42: Or, he may be born into a family of knowledgeable yogis. However, attaining such a birth is very rare in this world.
shlokaList.name: Or, he may be born into a family of knowledgeable yogis. However, attaining such a birth is very rare in this world.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
atha vā yoginām eva kule bhavati dhīmatām
etad dhi durlabhataram loke janma yad īdṛiśham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.42 (6.41 to 6.42)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.43: O son of the Kurus! In this lifetime, he embarks on his journey towards ultimate perfection, drawing upon the knowledge acquired in his previous lives.
shlokaList.name: O son of the Kurus! In this lifetime, he embarks on his journey towards ultimate perfection, drawing upon the knowledge acquired in his previous lives.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tatra tam buddhi-sanyogam labhate paurva-dehikam
yatate cha tato bhūyaḥ sansiddhau kuru-nandana
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.43 (6.43 to 6.44)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.44: Indeed due to his past practices, he is naturally drawn towards the path of yoga without needing to exert much effort. Such an inquisitive yogi transcends the ritualistic understanding of the scriptures.
shlokaList.name: Indeed due to his past practices, he is naturally drawn towards the path of yoga without needing to exert much effort. Such an inquisitive yogi transcends the ritualistic understanding of the scriptures.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pūrvābhyāsena tenaiva hriyate hyavaśho ’pi saḥ
jijñāsur api yogasya śhabda-brahmātivartate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.44 (6.43 to 6.44)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.45: The yogi who diligently applies themselves to this practice, having purified themselves of all sins, perfect their practice of yoga over many lifetimes and ultimately attains the Supreme destination.
shlokaList.name: The yogi who diligently applies themselves to this practice, having purified themselves of all sins, perfect their practice of yoga over many lifetimes and ultimately attains the Supreme destination.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prayatnād yatamānas tu yogī sanśhuddha-kilbiṣhaḥ
aneka-janma-sansiddhas tato yāti parām gatim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.45

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.46: A yogi is superior to tapasvi or an ascetic, a gyani or a learned person, and even a karmi or a fruitive worker. Therefore, O Arjuna, just become a yogi.
shlokaList.name: A yogi is superior to tapasvi or an ascetic, a gyani or a learned person, and even a karmi or a fruitive worker. Therefore, O Arjuna, just become a yogi.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tapasvibhyo ’dhiko yogī
jñānibhyo ’pi mato ’dhikaḥ
karmibhyaśh chādhiko yogī
tasmād yogī bhavārjuna
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.46

shlokaList.description: Shloka 6.47: Among all the yogis, the one who always thinks of Me within himself and worships Me with unwavering faith, I consider them to be the greatest practitioner of Yoga.
