shlokaList.name: Among all the yogis, the one who always thinks of Me within himself and worships Me with unwavering faith, I consider them to be the greatest practitioner of Yoga.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yoginām api sarveṣhām mad-gatenāntar-ātmanā
śhraddhāvān bhajate yo mām sa me yuktatamo mataḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 6.47

chapterList.description: Chapter 7: Gyana Vigyana Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and Wisdom
chapterList.name: Chapter 7: Gyana Vigyana Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and Wisdom

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.1: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Partha! Listen as to how by having your mind fixed on Me, taking refuge in Me and being established in Yoga you can know Me completely without any doubt.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Partha! Listen as to how by having your mind fixed on Me, taking refuge in Me and being established in Yoga you can know Me completely without any doubt.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
mayyāsakta-manāḥ pārtha yogam yuñjan mad-āśhrayaḥ
asanśhayam samagram mām yathā jñāsyasi tach chhṛiṇu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.2: I shall now explain this knowledge and wisdom to you in full. After knowing this, there does not remain anything further to be known in this world.
shlokaList.name: I shall now explain this knowledge and wisdom to you in full. After knowing this, there does not remain anything further to be known in this world.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jñānam te ’ham sa-vijñānam idam vakṣhyāmyaśheṣhataḥ
yaj jñātvā neha bhūyo ’nyaj jñātavyam-avaśhiṣhyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.3: Among thousands of human beings, hardly one tries for perfection and of those who have achieved perfection, very few understand Me in truth.
shlokaList.name: Among thousands of human beings, hardly one tries for perfection and of those who have achieved perfection, very few understand Me in truth.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
manuṣhyāṇām sahasreṣhu kaśhchid yatati siddhaye
yatatām api siddhānām kaśhchin mām vetti tattvataḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.3

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.4: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intelligence and ego – all these eight elements are part of My separated material energy (prakriti).
shlokaList.name: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intelligence and ego – all these eight elements are part of My separated material energy (prakriti).
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bhūmir-āpo ’nalo vāyuḥ kham mano buddhir eva cha
ahankāra itīyam me bhinnā prakṛitir aṣhṭadhā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.4 (7.4 to 7.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.5: But O mighty-armed Arjuna, understand that besides this inferior nature, there is another superior nature of Mine. This superior nature includes the living beings who sustain the universe.
shlokaList.name: But O mighty-armed Arjuna, understand that besides this inferior nature, there is another superior nature of Mine. This superior nature includes the living beings who sustain the universe.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
apareyam itas tvanyām prakṛitim viddhi me parām
jīva-bhūtām mahā-bāho yayedam dhāryate jagat
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.5 (7.4 to 7.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.6: Know that all living beings come from these two natures. And I am both the origin and end of the entire universe.
shlokaList.name: Know that all living beings come from these two natures. And I am both the origin and end of the entire universe.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇītyupadhāraya
aham kṛitsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.7: O Dhananjaya! There is nothing superior to Me. Everything is held together by Me, just like a thread holds all the gems together.
shlokaList.name: O Dhananjaya! There is nothing superior to Me. Everything is held together by Me, just like a thread holds all the gems together.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mattaḥ parataram nānyat kiñchid asti dhanañjaya
mayi sarvam idam protam sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.8: O Kaunteya! I am the flavor in water, the brightness of the Moon and the Sun, the holy syllable OM in the Vedas,  the sound in ether and the ability in men.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! I am the flavor in water, the brightness of the Moon and the Sun, the holy syllable OM in the Vedas, the sound in ether and the ability in men.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śhaśhi-sūryayoḥ
praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣhu śhabdaḥ khe pauruṣham nṛiṣhu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.8 (7.8 to 7.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.9: I am the pure aroma of the earth and the warmth in fire. I am the life of all the living beings and the penance of the ascetics.
shlokaList.name: I am the pure aroma of the earth and the warmth in fire. I am the life of all the living beings and the penance of the ascetics.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛithivyām cha tejaśh chāsmi vibhāvasau
jīvanam sarva-bhūteṣhu tapaśh chāsmi tapasviṣhu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.9 (7.8 to 7.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.10: O Partha! Know Me to be the eternal seed of all living beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent and the brilliance of the brilliant.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! Know Me to be the eternal seed of all living beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent and the brilliance of the brilliant.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bījam mām sarva-bhūtānām viddhi pārtha sanātanam
buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.10 (7.8 to 7.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.11: O best of the Bharatas! I am the strength of the strong, free from passion and desire. I am the reproductive desire in living beings, which is not against religious principles.
shlokaList.name: O best of the Bharatas! I am the strength of the strong, free from passion and desire. I am the reproductive desire in living beings, which is not against religious principles.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
balam balavatām chāham kāma-rāga-vivarjitam
dharmāviruddho bhūteṣhu kāmo ’smi bharatarṣhabha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.11 (7.8 to 7.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.12: And know that all states of existence, either in the mode of goodness, passion or ignorance, they all come from My energy. I am not in them, rather they are in Me.
shlokaList.name: And know that all states of existence, either in the mode of goodness, passion or ignorance, they all come from My energy. I am not in them, rather they are in Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye chaiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasās tāmasāśh cha ye
matta eveti tān viddhi na tvaham teṣhu te mayi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.12 (7.8 to 7.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.13: The whole world is deluded by the three modes of material nature and thus they do not know Me who is eternal and beyond these modes.
shlokaList.name: The whole world is deluded by the three modes of material nature and thus they do not know Me who is eternal and beyond these modes.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tribhir guṇa-mayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idam jagat
mohitam nābhijānāti māmebhyaḥ param avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.13

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.14: This divine illusory energy of Mine, comprising the three modes (gunas), is very difficult to transcend. But those who surrender to Me, can easily cross over it.
shlokaList.name: This divine illusory energy of Mine, comprising the three modes (gunas), is very difficult to transcend. But those who surrender to Me, can easily cross over it.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
daivī hyeṣhā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayā
mām eva ye prapadyante māyām etām taranti te
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.14

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.15: Those misdirected men who do not surrender to Me are; the foolish, those lowest among men, whose knowledge has been taken away by the illusory energy and those who are of demoniac nature.
shlokaList.name: Those misdirected men who do not surrender to Me are; the foolish, those lowest among men, whose knowledge has been taken away by the illusory energy and those who are of demoniac nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na mām duṣhkṛitino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ
māyayāpahṛita-jñānā āsuram bhāvam āśhritāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.16: O Arjuna, O best of the Bharatas,four types of virtuous men worship Me; the distressed, the curious, one seeking wealth and one who is enlightened.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna, O best of the Bharatas,four types of virtuous men worship Me; the distressed, the curious, one seeking wealth and one who is enlightened.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
chatur-vidhā bhajante mām janāḥ sukṛitino ’rjuna
ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī cha bharatarṣhabha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.16

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.17: Among them, the best are the enlightened ones who are always engaged in exclusive devotion to Me. I am very dear to them and they are also very dear to Me.
shlokaList.name: Among them, the best are the enlightened ones who are always engaged in exclusive devotion to Me. I am very dear to them and they are also very dear to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
teṣhām jñānī nitya-yukta eka-bhaktir viśhiṣhyate
priyo hi jñānino ’tyartham aham sa cha mama priyaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.17

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.18: All of them are certainly noble, but in My opinion, I consider the enlightened ones to be like My own self. Being connected to Me, they will certainly attain Me, the highest destination.
shlokaList.name: All of them are certainly noble, but in My opinion, I consider the enlightened ones to be like My own self. Being connected to Me, they will certainly attain Me, the highest destination.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tvātmaiva me matam
āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamām gatim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.18

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.19: After many lives, the enlightened man surrenders to Me, knowing Me to be everything. Such a great soul is very rare.
shlokaList.name: After many lives, the enlightened man surrenders to Me, knowing Me to be everything. Such a great soul is very rare.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bahūnām janmanām ante jñānavān mām prapadyate
vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā su-durlabhaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.19

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.20: Those whose knowledge has been carried away by selfish desires, surrender to other celestial beings or devatas and they follow various rules and regulations according to their own nature.
shlokaList.name: Those whose knowledge has been carried away by selfish desires, surrender to other celestial beings or devatas and they follow various rules and regulations according to their own nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kāmais tais tair hṛita-jñānāḥ prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥ
tam tam niyamam āsthāya prakṛityā niyatāḥ svayā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.20 (7.20 to 7.22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.21: Whoever desires to faithfully worship a specific form of celestial beings or devatas, I surely make their faith steady in them.
shlokaList.name: Whoever desires to faithfully worship a specific form of celestial beings or devatas, I surely make their faith steady in them.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yo yo yām yām tanum bhaktaḥ śhraddhayārchitum ichchhati
tasya tasyāchalām śhraddhām tām eva vidadhāmyaham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.21 (7.20 to 7.22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.22: With that faith, he desires to worship that particular celestial being and consequently he then receives the desired results. But actually these results are awarded by Me only.
shlokaList.name: With that faith, he desires to worship that particular celestial being and consequently he then receives the desired results. But actually these results are awarded by Me only.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sa tayā śhraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhate
labhate cha tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.22 (7.20 to 7.22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.23: People with little intelligence receive the desired results but these results are temporary in nature. Worshippers of other devatas eventually go to them but My devotees ultimately come to Me.
shlokaList.name: People with little intelligence receive the desired results but these results are temporary in nature. Worshippers of other devatas eventually go to them but My devotees ultimately come to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
antavat tu phalam teṣhām tad bhavatyalpa-medhasām
devān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.23

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.24: Unintelligent men think that I was formless before and have now taken this form. They do not know My highest nature which is changeless and is the best.
shlokaList.name: Unintelligent men think that I was formless before and have now taken this form. They do not know My highest nature which is changeless and is the best.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
avyaktam vyaktim āpannam manyante mām abuddhayaḥ
param bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.24

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.25: Being covered by My divine energy or Yogamaya, I do not disclose Myself to everybody. Therefore, the ignorant people do not know that I am unborn and eternal.
shlokaList.name: Being covered by My divine energy or Yogamaya, I do not disclose Myself to everybody. Therefore, the ignorant people do not know that I am unborn and eternal.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nāham prakāśhaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛitaḥ
mūḍho ’yam nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.25

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.26: O Arjuna, I know about the past, present and future and I also know everything about all living beings, but no one knows Me.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna, I know about the past, present and future and I also know everything about all living beings, but no one knows Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vedāham samatītāni vartamānāni chārjuna
bhaviṣhyāṇi cha bhūtāni mām tu veda na kaśhchana
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.26

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.27: O scion of Bharata! O Parantapa! All the living beings are born deluded, bewildered by dualities arising from desire and hatred.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata! O Parantapa! All the living beings are born deluded, bewildered by dualities arising from desire and hatred.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ichchhā-dveṣha-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhārata
sarva-bhūtāni sammoham sarge yānti parantapa
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.27

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.28: But, by virtuous activities of many lives those whose sins have been completely erased, they are freed from the delusion born of dualities and then they worship Me with determination.
shlokaList.name: But, by virtuous activities of many lives those whose sins have been completely erased, they are freed from the delusion born of dualities and then they worship Me with determination.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yeṣhām tvanta-gatam pāpam janānām puṇya-karmaṇām
te dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante mām dṛiḍha-vratāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.28

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.29: Those who are endeavoring to be liberated from old age and death, take shelter of Me and they fully understand everything about Brahman, Adhyatma and Karma.
shlokaList.name: Those who are endeavoring to be liberated from old age and death, take shelter of Me and they fully understand everything about Brahman, Adhyatma and Karma.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jarā-maraṇa-mokṣhāya mām āśhritya yatanti ye
te brahma tadviduḥ kṛitsnam adhyātmam karma chākhilam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.29 (7.29 to 7.30)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 7.30: And those who understand Me as the guiding principle behind this material creation, the devatas and the sacrifices, can also know Me with their minds absorbed in Me even at the time of death.
shlokaList.name: And those who understand Me as the guiding principle behind this material creation, the devatas and the sacrifices, can also know Me with their minds absorbed in Me even at the time of death.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sādhibhūtādhidaivam mām sādhiyajñam cha ye viduḥ
prayāṇa-kāle ’pi cha mām te vidur yukta-chetasaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 7.30 (7.29 to 7.30)

chapterList.description: Chapter 8: Akshara Brahma Yoga - The Yoga of the Imperishable Brahman
chapterList.name: Chapter 8: Akshara Brahma Yoga - The Yoga of the Imperishable Brahman

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.1: Arjuna said - O Purushottama! What is Brahman? What is Adhyatma and what is Karma? What is said to be Adhibhuta and what is Adhidaiva?
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Purushottama! What is Brahman? What is Adhyatma and what is Karma? What is said to be Adhibhuta and what is Adhidaiva?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
kim tad brahma kim adhyātmam kim karma puruṣhottama
adhibhūtam cha kim proktam adhidaivam kim uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.1 (8.1 to 8.2)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.2: O Madhusudana! Who is Adhiyajna within the body and how does He manifest as the Adhiyajna? How can the self-controlled know You at the time of death?
shlokaList.name: O Madhusudana! Who is Adhiyajna within the body and how does He manifest as the Adhiyajna? How can the self-controlled know You at the time of death?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adhiyajñaḥ katham ko ’tra dehe ’smin madhusūdana
prayāṇa-kāle cha katham jñeyo ’si niyatātmabhiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.2 (8.1 to 8.2)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.3: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - The supreme indestructible transcendental entity is called Brahman; its essential nature is called adhyatma or the self; and actions leading to the creation of material bodies of living entities is called action or karma.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - The supreme indestructible transcendental entity is called Brahman; its essential nature is called adhyatma or the self; and actions leading to the creation of material bodies of living entities is called action or karma.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
akṣharam brahma paramam svabhāvo ’dhyātmam uchyate
bhūta-bhāvodbhava-karo visargaḥ karma-sanjñitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.3 (8.3 to 8.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.4: The physical nature which is constantly changing is called adhibhuta; the universal form of God is called Adhidaiva; and I, who dwells in the heart of every embodied being, am called Adhiyajna.
shlokaList.name: The physical nature which is constantly changing is called adhibhuta; the universal form of God is called Adhidaiva; and I, who dwells in the heart of every embodied being, am called Adhiyajna.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adhibhūtam kṣharo bhāvaḥ puruṣhaśh chādhidaivatam
adhiyajño ’ham evātra dehe deha-bhṛitām vara
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.4 (8.3 to 8.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.5: Whoever, at the time of their demise, remembering Me alone, departs and gives up his mortal body, at once attains My nature; there is not the least doubt about it.
shlokaList.name: Whoever, at the time of their demise, remembering Me alone, departs and gives up his mortal body, at once attains My nature; there is not the least doubt about it.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
anta-kāle cha mām eva smaran muktvā kalevaram
yaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvam yāti nāstyatra sanśhayaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.5

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.6: O Kaunteya! Whatever state of being a person is thinking while giving up his body at the time of death, attains that state, as he is constantly absorbed in that thought.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! Whatever state of being a person is thinking while giving up his body at the time of death, attains that state, as he is constantly absorbed in that thought.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yam yam vāpi smaran bhāvam tyajatyante kalevaram
tam tam evaiti kaunteya sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.7: Therefore, always remember Me and perform your duty of fighting this war. With mind and intelligence fixed on Me, you will undoubtedly come to Me.
shlokaList.name: Therefore, always remember Me and perform your duty of fighting this war. With mind and intelligence fixed on Me, you will undoubtedly come to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu mām anusmara yudhya cha
mayyarpita-mano-buddhir mām evaiṣhyasyasanśhayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.8: O Partha, with practice one who constantly meditates on the Supreme with a focused mind attains that Supreme Divine Being.
shlokaList.name: O Partha, with practice one who constantly meditates on the Supreme with a focused mind attains that Supreme Divine Being.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
abhyāsa-yoga-yuktena chetasā nānya-gāminā
paramam puruṣham divyam yāti pārthānuchintayan
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.9: One should meditate on the Supreme as the omniscient, the oldest, the ruler of all, subtler than the subtle, maintainer of all, one whose form transcends human conception, one who is self-luminous like the sun and is beyond the darkness of ignorance.
shlokaList.name: One should meditate on the Supreme as the omniscient, the oldest, the ruler of all, subtler than the subtle, maintainer of all, one whose form transcends human conception, one who is self-luminous like the sun and is beyond the darkness of ignorance.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kavim purāṇam anuśhāsitāram
aṇor aṇīyānsam anusmared yaḥ
sarvasya dhātāram achintya-rūpam
āditya-varṇam tamasaḥ parastāt
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.10: At the time of death, one who fixes the life air in between the eyebrows and by the power of yoga with a focused mind remembers the Supreme Divine Being with devotion certainly attains Him.
shlokaList.name: At the time of death, one who fixes the life air in between the eyebrows and by the power of yoga with a focused mind remembers the Supreme Divine Being with devotion certainly attains Him.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prayāṇa-kāle manasāchalena
bhaktyā yukto yoga-balena chaiva
bhruvor madhye prāṇam āveśhya samyak
sa tam param puruṣham upaiti divyam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.11: The scholars of the Vedas describe Him as the Imperishable; great ascetics practise celibacy and renounce worldly pleasures to attain Him. Now, I will briefly explain to you the path leading to that goal.
shlokaList.name: The scholars of the Vedas describe Him as the Imperishable; great ascetics practise celibacy and renounce worldly pleasures to attain Him. Now, I will briefly explain to you the path leading to that goal.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yad akṣharam veda-vido vadanti
viśhanti yad yatayo vīta-rāgāḥ
yad ichchhanto brahmacharyam charanti
tat te padam saṅgraheṇa pravakṣhye
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.11 (8.11 to 8.13)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.12: By restraining all the senses of the body, fixing the mind in the region of the heart, and then directing the life-breath to the head, one should establish oneself in unwavering yogic concentration.
shlokaList.name: By restraining all the senses of the body, fixing the mind in the region of the heart, and then directing the life-breath to the head, one should establish oneself in unwavering yogic concentration.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-dvārāṇi sanyamya mano hṛidi nirudhya cha
mūrdhnyādhāyātmanaḥ prāṇam āsthito yoga-dhāraṇām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.12 (8.11 to 8.13)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.13: The one who departs from the body while remembering Me and chanting the sacred syllable OM will attain the supreme goal.
shlokaList.name: The one who departs from the body while remembering Me and chanting the sacred syllable OM will attain the supreme goal.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
om ityekākṣharam brahma vyāharan mām anusmaran
yaḥ prayāti tyajan deham sa yāti paramām gatim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.13 (8.11 to 8.13)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.14: O Partha! I am easily accessible to that yogi who remains deeply absorbed in Me, always remembering Me with a focused and undivided mind.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! I am easily accessible to that yogi who remains deeply absorbed in Me, always remembering Me with a focused and undivided mind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ananya-chetāḥ satatam yo mām smarati nityaśhaḥ
tasyāham sulabhaḥ pārtha nitya-yuktasya yoginaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.14

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.15: Having attained the highest perfection of achieving Me, these great souls do not return to the cycle of rebirth which is characterised by sorrow and impermanence.
shlokaList.name: Having attained the highest perfection of achieving Me, these great souls do not return to the cycle of rebirth which is characterised by sorrow and impermanence.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mām upetya punar janma duḥkhālayam aśhāśhvatam
nāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ sansiddhim paramām gatāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.16: O Arjuna, all worlds, from the realm of Brahma down to the lower plane, are subject to the cycle of rebirth. However, upon attaining Me, O Kaunteya, one does not take birth again.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna, all worlds, from the realm of Brahma down to the lower plane, are subject to the cycle of rebirth. However, upon attaining Me, O Kaunteya, one does not take birth again.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ā-brahma-bhuvanāl lokāḥ punar āvartino ’rjuna
mām upetya tu kaunteya punar janma na vidyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.16

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.17: One day of Brahma, the creator, spans a thousand cycles of the four ages (or mahayuga), and his night also extends for the same duration. Those who understand this truth are wise and grasp the nature of this day and night.
shlokaList.name: One day of Brahma, the creator, spans a thousand cycles of the four ages (or mahayuga), and his night also extends for the same duration. Those who understand this truth are wise and grasp the nature of this day and night.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sahasra-yuga-paryantam ahar yad brahmaṇo viduḥ
rātrim yuga-sahasrāntām te ’ho-rātra-vido janāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.17 (8.17 to 8.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.18: At the start of Brahma’s day, all living beings emerge from the unmanifest source. And at the start of his night, all embodied beings once again merge into that unmanifest state.
shlokaList.name: At the start of Brahma’s day, all living beings emerge from the unmanifest source. And at the start of his night, all embodied beings once again merge into that unmanifest state.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
avyaktād vyaktayaḥ sarvāḥ prabhavantyahar-āgame
rātryāgame pralīyante tatraivāvyakta-sanjñake
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.18 (8.17 to 8.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.19: O Partha! Again and again, multitudes of living beings come into existence at the beginning of Brahma’s day and are annihilated at the onset of his night.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! Again and again, multitudes of living beings come into existence at the beginning of Brahma’s day and are annihilated at the onset of his night.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bhūta-grāmaḥ sa evāyam bhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyate
rātryāgame ’vaśhaḥ pārtha prabhavatyahar-āgame
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.19 (8.17 to 8.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.20: However, beyond this manifest and unmanifest nature, there exists another unmanifest realm which is eternal and even when everything is destroyed in thisuniverse that realm remains unaffected.
shlokaList.name: However, beyond this manifest and unmanifest nature, there exists another unmanifest realm which is eternal and even when everything is destroyed in thisuniverse that realm remains unaffected.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
paras tasmāt tu bhāvo ’nyo ’vyakto ’vyaktāt sanātanaḥ
yaḥ sa sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu naśhyatsu na vinaśhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.20 (8.20 to 8.21)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.21: That unmanifest realm which is described as infallible as the ultimate destination having attained which one never takes birth again, that is My Supreme Abode.
shlokaList.name: That unmanifest realm which is described as infallible as the ultimate destination having attained which one never takes birth again, that is My Supreme Abode.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
avyakto ’kṣhara ityuktas tam āhuḥ paramām gatim
yam prāpya na nivartante tad dhāma paramam mama
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.21 (8.20 to 8.21)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.22: O Partha! The Supreme Being who is present everywhere and in whom everything is contained, is attainable only through undeviating devotion.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! The Supreme Being who is present everywhere and in whom everything is contained, is attainable only through undeviating devotion.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
puruṣhaḥ sa paraḥ pārtha bhaktyā labhyas tvananyayā
yasyāntaḥ-sthāni bhūtāni yena sarvam idam tatam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.22

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.23: I shall now explain to you, O best of the Bharatas, the different times at which a yogi departs from this world – one leading to liberation and the other leading to rebirth.
shlokaList.name: I shall now explain to you, O best of the Bharatas, the different times at which a yogi departs from this world – one leading to liberation and the other leading to rebirth.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yatra kāle tvanāvṛittim āvṛittim chaiva yoginaḥ
prayātā yānti tam kālam vakṣhyāmi bharatarṣhabha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.23 (8.23 to 8.26)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.24: If a yogi passes away during the six months of the sun’s northern course, the waxing phase of the moon, during daytime, and around a fire, he attains that Supreme.
shlokaList.name: If a yogi passes away during the six months of the sun’s northern course, the waxing phase of the moon, during daytime, and around a fire, he attains that Supreme.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
agnir jyotir ahaḥ śhuklaḥ ṣhaṇ-māsā uttarāyaṇam
tatra prayātā gachchhanti brahma brahma-vido janāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.24 (8.23 to 8.26)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.25: The yogis who depart during the six months of the sun’s southern course, the waning phase of the moon, the time of smoke or during the night attain the effulgent moon planet but return again.
shlokaList.name: The yogis who depart during the six months of the sun’s southern course, the waning phase of the moon, the time of smoke or during the night attain the effulgent moon planet but return again.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dhūmo rātris tathā kṛiṣhṇaḥ ṣhaṇ-māsā dakṣhiṇāyanam
tatra chāndramasam jyotir yogī prāpya nivartate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.25 (8.23 to 8.26)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.26: Light and darkness are considered as the eternal paths of leaving this world. By the path of light, a man attains liberation, while the path of darkness leads to rebirth.
shlokaList.name: Light and darkness are considered as the eternal paths of leaving this world. By the path of light, a man attains liberation, while the path of darkness leads to rebirth.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhukla-kṛiṣhṇe gatī hyete jagataḥ śhāśhvate mate
ekayā yātyanāvṛittim anyayāvartate punaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.26 (8.23 to 8.26)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.27: The yogi who understands these two different paths is never bewildered. Therefore, O Partha, always remain steadfast in yoga.
shlokaList.name: The yogi who understands these two different paths is never bewildered. Therefore, O Partha, always remain steadfast in yoga.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
naite sṛitī pārtha jānan yogī muhyati kaśhchana
tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu yoga-yukto bhavārjuna
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.27

shlokaList.description: Shloka 8.28: Understand perfectly that the yogiswho understand this profound truth, attain merit surpassing that gained from the study of Vedas, performance of sacrifices, austerities and charities. Such yogis reach the Supreme Eternal Abode.
shlokaList.name: Understand perfectly that the yogiswho understand this profound truth, attain merit surpassing that gained from the study of Vedas, performance of sacrifices, austerities and charities. Such yogis reach the Supreme Eternal Abode.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vedeṣhu yajñeṣhu tapaḥsu chaiva
dāneṣhu yat puṇya-phalam pradiṣhṭam
atyeti tat sarvam idam viditvā
yogī param sthānam upaiti chādyam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 8.28

chapterList.description: Chapter 9: Raja Vidya Rajaguhya Yoga - The Yoga of Supreme Knowledge and Mystery
chapterList.name: Chapter 9: Raja Vidya Rajaguhya Yoga - The Yoga of Supreme Knowledge and Mystery

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.1: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - I shall now impart to you, O Arjuna, the most profound and confidential knowledge along with its 1practical application, for you are devoid of envy. By understanding this, you will transcend the entanglements of worldly existence.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - I shall now impart to you, O Arjuna, the most profound and confidential knowledge along with its 1practical application, for you are devoid of envy. By understanding this, you will transcend the entanglements of worldly existence.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
idam tu te guhyatamam pravakṣhyāmyanasūyave
jñānam vijñāna-sahitam yaj jñātvā mokṣhyase ’śhubhāt
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.2: This knowledge is the king of all knowledge, it is the highest secret,the holiest of all teachings and the best path to follow. It is directly experiential, virtuous, practiced joyfully and is eternal.
shlokaList.name: This knowledge is the king of all knowledge, it is the highest secret,the holiest of all teachings and the best path to follow. It is directly experiential, virtuous, practiced joyfully and is eternal.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rāja-vidyā rāja-guhyam pavitram idam uttamam
pratyakṣhāvagamam dharmyam su-sukham kartum avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.3: O Parantapa ! People who have no faith in this path of dharma,cannot attain Me and will return to the cycle of life and death in the mortal world.
shlokaList.name: O Parantapa ! People who have no faith in this path of dharma,cannot attain Me and will return to the cycle of life and death in the mortal world.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aśhraddadhānāḥ puruṣhā dharmasyāsya parantapa
aprāpya mām nivartante mṛityu-samsāra-vartmani
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.3

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.4: This entire universe is enveloped by My unmanifested form. All beings exist within Me, yet I am not confined within them.
shlokaList.name: This entire universe is enveloped by My unmanifested form. All beings exist within Me, yet I am not confined within them.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mayā tatam idam sarvam jagad avyakta-mūrtinā
mat-sthāni sarva-bhūtāni na chāham teṣhvavasthitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.4 (9.4 to 9.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.5: Behold the mystery of My divine energy, these beings do not dwell within Me. While I sustain all beings and am the cause of their existence, still I am not a part of this material creation.
shlokaList.name: Behold the mystery of My divine energy, these beings do not dwell within Me. While I sustain all beings and am the cause of their existence, still I am not a part of this material creation.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na cha mat-sthāni bhūtāni paśhya me yogam aiśhwaram
bhūta-bhṛin na cha bhūta-stho mamātmā bhūta-bhāvanaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.5 (9.4 to 9.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.6: Just as the mighty wind, though moving everywhere, always remains confined within the sky, similarly, you should understand that all beings exist within Me.
shlokaList.name: Just as the mighty wind, though moving everywhere, always remains confined within the sky, similarly, you should understand that all beings exist within Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yathākāśha-sthito nityam vāyuḥ sarvatra-go mahān
tathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni mat-sthānītyupadhāraya
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.7: O Kaunteya! At the end of a cosmic cycle, all living beings merge into My Material energy. Then, at the beginning of the next cosmic cycle, I manifest them once again.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! At the end of a cosmic cycle, all living beings merge into My Material energy. Then, at the beginning of the next cosmic cycle, I manifest them once again.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-bhūtāni kaunteya prakṛitim yānti māmikām
kalpa-kṣhaye punas tāni kalpādau visṛijāmyaham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.7 (9.7 to 9.8)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.8: By presiding over My material energy, I repeatedly generate these countless forms according to their inherent natures and tendencies.
shlokaList.name: By presiding over My material energy, I repeatedly generate these countless forms according to their inherent natures and tendencies.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prakṛitim svām avaṣhṭabhya visṛijāmi punaḥ punaḥ
bhūta-grāmam imam kṛitsnam avaśham prakṛiter vaśhāt
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.8 (9.7 to 9.8)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.9: O Dhananjaya! None of these actions bind Me as I remain a neutral observer, completely detached from such actions.
shlokaList.name: O Dhananjaya! None of these actions bind Me as I remain a neutral observer, completely detached from such actions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na cha mām tāni karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañjaya
udāsīna-vad āsīnam asaktam teṣhu karmasu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.10: Under My supervision, this material energy brings forth all moving and non-moving beings, O Kaunteya. Under its influence the material world undergoes the cycles of creation, maintenance and dissolution.
shlokaList.name: Under My supervision, this material energy brings forth all moving and non-moving beings, O Kaunteya. Under its influence the material world undergoes the cycles of creation, maintenance and dissolution.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mayādhyakṣheṇa prakṛitiḥ sūyate sa-charācharam
hetunānena kaunteya jagad viparivartate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.11: Fools are unable to recognise Me when I appear in the human form. They are unable to perceive the transcendental, divine nature of My personality as the Supreme Lord of all beings.
shlokaList.name: Fools are unable to recognise Me when I appear in the human form. They are unable to perceive the transcendental, divine nature of My personality as the Supreme Lord of all beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
avajānanti mām mūḍhā mānuṣhīm tanum āśhritam
param bhāvam ajānanto mama bhūta-maheśhvaram
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.11

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.12: Those foolish individuals, being deluded by my material energy, get attracted to demoniac and atheistic beliefs. In that deluded state their hopes are baffled, their actions prove futile and even their knowledge proves meaningless.
shlokaList.name: Those foolish individuals, being deluded by my material energy, get attracted to demoniac and atheistic beliefs. In that deluded state their hopes are baffled, their actions prove futile and even their knowledge proves meaningless.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
moghāśhā mogha-karmāṇo mogha-jñānā vichetasaḥ
rākṣhasīm āsurīm chaiva prakṛitim mohinīm śhritāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.12

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.13: However, O Partha! The noble souls, taking refuge in My divine spiritual energy, recognise Me as the fundamental cause of all beings and imperishable. And they consistently worship Me with an undivided mind.
shlokaList.name: However, O Partha! The noble souls, taking refuge in My divine spiritual energy, recognise Me as the fundamental cause of all beings and imperishable. And they consistently worship Me with an undivided mind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mahātmānas tu mām pārtha daivīm prakṛitim āśhritāḥ
bhajantyananya-manaso jñātvā bhūtādim avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.13

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.14: Always singing My glories, steadfast in their commitments, humbly bowing down in devotion, they perpetually worship Me with unwavering devotion.
shlokaList.name: Always singing My glories, steadfast in their commitments, humbly bowing down in devotion, they perpetually worship Me with unwavering devotion.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
satatam kīrtayanto mām yatantaśh cha dṛiḍha-vratāḥ
namasyantaśh cha mām bhaktyā nitya-yuktā upāsate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.14

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.15: Others, through the sacrifice of cultivating knowledge, worship Me as one with them or as distinct and separate or in My universal form.
shlokaList.name: Others, through the sacrifice of cultivating knowledge, worship Me as one with them or as distinct and separate or in My universal form.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jñāna-yajñena chāpyanye yajanto mām upāsate
ekatvena pṛithaktvena bahudhā viśhvato-mukham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.16: I am the Vedic ritual, I am the sacrifice, I am the oblation to the departed, I am the medicinal herb, I am the sacred hymn, I am the sacrificial clarified butter, I am the fire and I am the offering.
shlokaList.name: I am the Vedic ritual, I am the sacrifice, I am the oblation to the departed, I am the medicinal herb, I am the sacred hymn, I am the sacrificial clarified butter, I am the fire and I am the offering.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aham kratur aham yajñaḥ svadhāham aham auṣhadham
mantro ’ham aham evājyam aham agnir aham hutam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.16 (9.16 to 9.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.17: I am the father, mother, sustainer and grandfather of this universe. I am the ultimate object of knowledge, the pure essence, the sacred syllable OM and I am also the Rigveda, Yajurveda and Samaveda.
shlokaList.name: I am the father, mother, sustainer and grandfather of this universe. I am the ultimate object of knowledge, the pure essence, the sacred syllable OM and I am also the Rigveda, Yajurveda and Samaveda.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pitāham asya jagato mātā dhātā pitāmahaḥ
vedyam pavitram omkāra ṛik sāma yajur eva cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.17 (9.16 to 9.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.18: I am the goal, the sustainer, the lord, the witness, the abode, the protector, the well -wishing friend, the creator, the destroyer, the dwelling place, the repository and the eternal seed.
shlokaList.name: I am the goal, the sustainer, the lord, the witness, the abode, the protector, the well -wishing friend, the creator, the destroyer, the dwelling place, the repository and the eternal seed.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
gatir bhartā prabhuḥ sākṣhī nivāsaḥ śharaṇam suhṛit
prabhavaḥ pralayaḥ sthānam nidhānam bījam avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.18 (9.16 to 9.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.19: O Arjuna, I radiate heat; I withhold and send forth the rain to earth; I am immortality and I am also death; I am the eternal unmanifested state and the transient manifested universe also.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna, I radiate heat; I withhold and send forth the rain to earth; I am immortality and I am also death; I am the eternal unmanifested state and the transient manifested universe also.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tapāmyaham aham varṣham nigṛihṇāmyutsṛijāmi cha
amṛitam chaiva mṛityuśh cha sad asach chāham arjuna
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.19 (9.16 to 9.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.20: The knowers of the three Vedas, purified from all sins through sacrificial worship and the soma juice, seek the path to heavenly realms. Arriving in the realm of Indra, they enjoy divine pleasures among the celestial gods.
shlokaList.name: The knowers of the three Vedas, purified from all sins through sacrificial worship and the soma juice, seek the path to heavenly realms. Arriving in the realm of Indra, they enjoy divine pleasures among the celestial gods.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
trai-vidyā mām soma-pāḥ pūta-pāpā
yajñair iṣhṭvā svar-gatim prārthayante
te puṇyam āsādya surendra-lokam
aśhnanti divyān divi deva-bhogān
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.20 (9.20 to 9.21)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.21: After having enjoyed the immense pleasures of heaven and depleting their merits, they return to the mortal realm. Thus, those who engage in rituals given in the three Vedas, seeking worldly pleasures, cycle back and forth in this world.
shlokaList.name: After having enjoyed the immense pleasures of heaven and depleting their merits, they return to the mortal realm. Thus, those who engage in rituals given in the three Vedas, seeking worldly pleasures, cycle back and forth in this world.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
te tam bhuktvā swarga-lokam viśhālam
kṣhīṇe puṇye martya-lokam viśhanti
evam trayī-dharmam anuprapannā
gatāgatam kāma-kāmā labhante
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.21 (9.20 to 9.21)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.22: Those who worship Me alone, meditating solely on Me without distraction, to those devoted souls I provide what they lack and protect what they possess.
shlokaList.name: Those who worship Me alone, meditating solely on Me without distraction, to those devoted souls I provide what they lack and protect what they possess.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ananyāśh chintayanto mām ye janāḥ paryupāsate
teṣhām nityābhiyuktānām yoga-kṣhemam vahāmyaham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.22

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.23: O Kaunteya! The devotees who worship other devatas with sincere faith are ultimately worshipping Me, though not in the correct manner.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! The devotees who worship other devatas with sincere faith are ultimately worshipping Me, though not in the correct manner.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye ’pyanya-devatā-bhaktā yajante śhraddhayānvitāḥ
te ’pi mām eva kaunteya yajantyavidhi-pūrvakam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.23

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.24: I am the enjoyer and the lord of all sacrifices. But those who fail to realise Me in My real essence ultimately fall down and are reborn.
shlokaList.name: I am the enjoyer and the lord of all sacrifices. But those who fail to realise Me in My real essence ultimately fall down and are reborn.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aham hi sarva-yajñānām bhoktā cha prabhureva cha
na tu mām abhijānanti tattvenātaśh chyavanti te
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.24

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.25: Those who worship the devatas go to the devatas, those who worship the ancestors go to the ancestors, those who worship the spirits go to the spirits and those who worship Me will reach Me.
shlokaList.name: Those who worship the devatas go to the devatas, those who worship the ancestors go to the ancestors, those who worship the spirits go to the spirits and those who worship Me will reach Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yānti deva-vratā devān pitṝīn yānti pitṛi-vratāḥ
bhūtāni yānti bhūtejyā yānti mad-yājino ’pi mām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.25

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.26: Whoever offers Me a leaf, a flower, a fruit or water with genuine devotion and with pure consciousness, I accept that heartfelt offering.
shlokaList.name: Whoever offers Me a leaf, a flower, a fruit or water with genuine devotion and with pure consciousness, I accept that heartfelt offering.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
patram puṣhpam phalam toyam yo me bhaktyā prayachchhati
tadaham bhaktyupahṛitam aśhnāmi prayatātmanaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.26

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.27: O Kaunteya! Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer in sacrifice or give in charity, whatever austerities you practice, do all of that as an offering to Me.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer in sacrifice or give in charity, whatever austerities you practice, do all of that as an offering to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yat karoṣhi yad aśhnāsi yaj juhoṣhi dadāsi yat
yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣhva mad-arpaṇam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.27

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.28: By surrendering all your actions to Me in this way, you will be liberated from the bondage of those actions and their fruits, whether good or evil. With a mind firmly established in the path of renunciation and liberated from these bonds, you will attain Me alone.
shlokaList.name: By surrendering all your actions to Me in this way, you will be liberated from the bondage of those actions and their fruits, whether good or evil. With a mind firmly established in the path of renunciation and liberated from these bonds, you will attain Me alone.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhubhāśhubha-phalair evam mokṣhyase karma-bandhanaiḥ
sannyāsa-yoga-yuktātmā vimukto mām upaiṣhyasi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.28

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.29: I am impartial towards all living beings. I do not hate anyone nor is anyone dear to Me. However, those who worship Me with sincere devotion, they reside in Me and I also reside in them.
shlokaList.name: I am impartial towards all living beings. I do not hate anyone nor is anyone dear to Me. However, those who worship Me with sincere devotion, they reside in Me and I also reside in them.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
samo ’ham sarva-bhūteṣhu na me dveṣhyo ’sti na priyaḥ
ye bhajanti tu mām bhaktyā mayi te teṣhu chāpyaham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.29

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.30: Even if one indulges in some most sinful behavior, but worships Me with unwavering devotion, he should be considered a saint, for he has made a proper resolve.
shlokaList.name: Even if one indulges in some most sinful behavior, but worships Me with unwavering devotion, he should be considered a saint, for he has made a proper resolve.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
api chet su-durāchāro bhajate mām ananya-bhāk
sādhur eva sa mantavyaḥ samyag vyavasito hi saḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.30

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.31: Very soon such a person becomes virtuous and attains eternal peace. O Kaunteya, declare it that My devotee never perishes.
shlokaList.name: Very soon such a person becomes virtuous and attains eternal peace. O Kaunteya, declare it that My devotee never perishes.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kṣhipram bhavati dharmātmā śhaśhvach-chhāntim nigachchhati
kaunteya pratijānīhi na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.31

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.32: O Partha! Those who take refuge in Me, regardless of their birth, race, gender, or caste, including those whom society may reject, will attain the supreme destination.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! Those who take refuge in Me, regardless of their birth, race, gender, or caste, including those whom society may reject, will attain the supreme destination.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mām hi pārtha vyapāśhritya ye ’pi syuḥ pāpa-yonayaḥ
striyo vaiśhyās tathā śhūdrās te ’pi yānti parām gatim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.32 (9.32 to 9.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.33: Then what to speak of the righteous brahminical people, My devotees and the saintly kings. Therefore being in this impermanent and unhappy world, just worship Me.
shlokaList.name: Then what to speak of the righteous brahminical people, My devotees and the saintly kings. Therefore being in this impermanent and unhappy world, just worship Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kim punar brāhmaṇāḥ puṇyā bhaktā rājarṣhayas tathā
anityam asukham lokam imam prāpya bhajasva mām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.33 (9.32 to 9.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 9.34: Always keep your mind focused on Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your respects to Me. With complete devotional absorption in Me, you will surely attain Me.
shlokaList.name: Always keep your mind focused on Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your respects to Me. With complete devotional absorption in Me, you will surely attain Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī mām namaskuru
mām evaiṣhyasi yuktvaivam ātmānam mat-parāyaṇaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 9.34

chapterList.description: Chapter 10: Vibhuti Yoga - The Yoga of Divine Glories
chapterList.name: Chapter 10: Vibhuti Yoga - The Yoga of Divine Glories

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.1: Bhagawan Sri Krishna Said - Listen again to My supreme word, O mighty-armed Arjuna. You are dear to Me and therefore I shall reveal them to you for your welfare.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna Said - Listen again to My supreme word, O mighty-armed Arjuna. You are dear to Me and therefore I shall reveal them to you for your welfare.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
bhūya eva mahā-bāho śhṛiṇu me paramam vachaḥ
yatte ’ham prīyamāṇāya vakṣhyāmi hita-kāmyayā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.2: Neither the celestial beings nor the great sages know of My origin, for I am the original source from which both the celestial beings and the great sages themselves have arisen in every aspect.
shlokaList.name: Neither the celestial beings nor the great sages know of My origin, for I am the original source from which both the celestial beings and the great sages themselves have arisen in every aspect.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na me viduḥ sura-gaṇāḥ prabhavam na maharṣhayaḥ
aham ādir hi devānām maharṣhīṇām cha sarvaśhaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.3: The person who knows Me as birthless and without origin and as the Supreme Lord of all worlds; he among mortal beings is free from illusions and freed from all sins.
shlokaList.name: The person who knows Me as birthless and without origin and as the Supreme Lord of all worlds; he among mortal beings is free from illusions and freed from all sins.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yo māmajam anādim cha vetti loka-maheśhvaram
asammūḍhaḥ sa martyeṣhu sarva-pāpaiḥ pramuchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.3

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.4: Intellect, knowledge, free of delusion, forgiveness, truthfulness, restraint of the senses, control of the mind, happiness and sorrow, birth and death, fear, fearlessness;
shlokaList.name: Intellect, knowledge, free of delusion, forgiveness, truthfulness, restraint of the senses, control of the mind, happiness and sorrow, birth and death, fear, fearlessness;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
buddhir jñānam asammohaḥ kṣhamā satyam damaḥ śhamaḥ
sukham duḥkham bhavo ’bhāvo bhayam chābhayameva cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.4 (10.4 to 10.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.5: Non-violence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame, infamy: these different states of living beings certainly originate from Me alone.
shlokaList.name: Non-violence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame, infamy these different states of living beings certainly originate from Me alone.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ahinsā samatā tuṣhṭis tapo dānam yaśho ’yaśhaḥ
bhavanti bhāvā bhūtānām matta eva pṛithag-vidhāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.5 (10.4 to 10.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.6: The seven great sages, preceded by four other sages and the Manus, all emanate from Me, born from My mind. From them, all living beings on these planets have descended.
shlokaList.name: The seven great sages, preceded by four other sages and the Manus, all emanate from Me, born from My mind. From them, all living beings on these planets have descended.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
maharṣhayaḥ sapta pūrve chatvāro manavas tathā
mad-bhāvā mānasā jātā yeṣhām loka imāḥ prajāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.7: One who is truly convinced of these glories and divine powers of Mine engages in unwavering devotion. There is no doubt about this.
shlokaList.name: One who is truly convinced of these glories and divine powers of Mine engages in unwavering devotion. There is no doubt about this.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
etām vibhūtim yogam cha mama yo vetti tattvataḥ
so ’vikampena yogena yujyate nātra sanśhayaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.8: I am the cause of everything. Everything in this world evolves from Me, knowing this, the wise being fully devoted, worship Me.
shlokaList.name: I am the cause of everything. Everything in this world evolves from Me, knowing this, the wise being fully devoted, worship Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aham sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvam pravartate
iti matvā bhajante mām budhā bhāva-samanvitāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.9: Those whose minds are centred on Me, whose lives are surrendered to Me, derive great satisfaction and delight by enlightening one another and conversing about Me.
shlokaList.name: Those whose minds are centred on Me, whose lives are surrendered to Me, derive great satisfaction and delight by enlightening one another and conversing about Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mach-chittā mad-gata-prāṇā bodhayantaḥ parasparam
kathayantaśh cha mām nityam tuṣhyanti cha ramanti cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.10: To those who are always devoted to Me and worship Me with love, I give them the knowledge by which they can come to Me.
shlokaList.name: To those who are always devoted to Me and worship Me with love, I give them the knowledge by which they can come to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
teṣhām satata-yuktānām bhajatām prīti-pūrvakam
dadāmi buddhi-yogam tam yena mām upayānti te
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.11: Out of compassion for them, I, residing in their heart, destroy the darkness of ignorance by the shining lamp of knowledge.
shlokaList.name: Out of compassion for them, I, residing in their heart, destroy the darkness of ignorance by the shining lamp of knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
teṣhām evānukampārtham aham ajñāna-jam tamaḥ
nāśhayāmyātma-bhāva-stho jñāna-dīpena bhāsvatā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.11

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.12: Arjuna said - You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Abode and the Supremely Pure. You are the eternal, divine, original, unborn and the greatest person.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Abode and the Supremely Pure. You are the eternal, divine, original, unborn and the greatest person.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
param brahma param dhāma pavitram paramam bhavān
puruṣham śhāśhvatam divyam ādi-devam ajam vibhum
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.12 (10.12 to 10.13)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.13: All the sages, the divine seer Narada as well as Asita, Devala and Vyasa, explain You as such and now You Yourself are explaining it to me.
shlokaList.name: All the sages, the divine seer Narada as well as Asita, Devala and Vyasa, explain You as such and now You Yourself are explaining it to me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
āhus tvām ṛiṣhayaḥ sarve devarṣhir nāradas tathā
asito devalo vyāsaḥ svayam chaiva bravīṣhi me
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.13 (10.12 to 10.13)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.14: O Keshava, I completely accept it as truth what You have told me. O Bhagawan, neither the devatas nor demons can understand Your divine manifestation.
shlokaList.name: O Keshava, I completely accept it as truth what You have told me. O Bhagawan, neither the devatas nor demons can understand Your divine manifestation.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarvam etad ṛitam manye yan mām vadasi keśhava
na hi te bhagavan vyaktim vidur devā na dānavāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.14 (10.14 to 10.15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.15: O the Supreme person, the source of everything, the controller of all beings, the master of all devatas and the master of the universe; You alone can know Yourself by Your divine energies.
shlokaList.name: O the Supreme person, the source of everything, the controller of all beings, the master of all devatas and the master of the universe; You alone can know Yourself by Your divine energies.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
swayam evātmanātmānam vettha tvam puruṣhottama
bhūta-bhāvana bhūteśha deva-deva jagat-pate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.15 (10.14 to 10.15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.16: Please explain to me in detail about Your divine manifestations by which You pervade the entire universe.
shlokaList.name: Please explain to me in detail about Your divine manifestations by which You pervade the entire universe.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vaktum arhasyaśheṣheṇa divyā hyātma-vibhūtayaḥ
yābhir vibhūtibhir lokān imāms tvam vyāpya tiṣhṭhasi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.16 (10.16 to 10.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.17: O Supreme Yogi, O Bhagawan, how can I know You, how can I always think of You In which various forms can I think of You?
shlokaList.name: O Supreme Yogi, O Bhagawan, how can I know You, how can I always think of You In which various forms can I think of You?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
katham vidyām aham yogins tvām sadā parichintayan
keṣhu keṣhu cha bhāveṣhu chintyo ’si bhagavan mayā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.17 (10.16 to 10.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.18: O Janardana! Please once again describe to me in detail about Your marvellous power and divine manifestations. I do not feel sated in hearing Your words of nectar.
shlokaList.name: O Janardana! Please once again describe to me in detail about Your marvellous power and divine manifestations. I do not feel sated in hearing Your words of nectar.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vistareṇātmano yogam vibhūtim cha janārdana
bhūyaḥ kathaya tṛiptir hi śhṛiṇvato nāsti me ’mṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.18 (10.16 to 10.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.19: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Yes, I shall tell you only about My divine manifestations, but only the most important ones, O best of Kurus, because My opulence is limitless.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Yes, I shall tell you only about My divine manifestations, but only the most important ones, O best of Kurus, because My opulence is limitless.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
hanta te kathayiṣhyāmi divyā hyātma-vibhūtayaḥ
prādhānyataḥ kuru-śhreṣhṭha nāstyanto vistarasya me
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.19 (10.19 to 10.20)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.20: O Gudakesha, I am the Supreme Self which is seated in the heart of all living beings. I alone am the beginning, the middle and the end of all living beings.
shlokaList.name: O Gudakesha, I am the Supreme Self which is seated in the heart of all living beings. I alone am the beginning, the middle and the end of all living beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aham ātmā guḍākeśha sarva-bhūtāśhaya-sthitaḥ
aham ādiśh cha madhyam cha bhūtānām anta eva cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.20 (10.19 to 10.20)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.21: I am Lord Vishnu among the Adityas, the radiant sun among the luminaries, I am Marichi among the Maruts and among the stars I am the moon.
shlokaList.name: I am Lord Vishnu among the Adityas, the radiant sun among the luminaries, I am Marichi among the Maruts and among the stars I am the moon.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ādityānām aham viṣhṇur jyotiṣhām ravir anśhumān
marīchir marutām asmi nakṣhatrāṇām aham śhaśhī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.21 (10.21 to 10.24)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.22: Among the Vedas, I am the Samaveda. Among the devatas, I am the heavenly king Indra. Among all the senses, I am the mind; and I am the consciousness in all living beings.
shlokaList.name: Among the Vedas, I am the Samaveda. Among the devatas, I am the heavenly king Indra. Among all the senses, I am the mind; and I am the consciousness in all living beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vedānām sāma-vedo ’smi devānām asmi vāsavaḥ
indriyāṇām manaśh chāsmi bhūtānām asmi chetanā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.22 (10.21 to 10.24)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.23: Among the Rudras, know Me as Shankara, among the Yakshasas and Rakshasas, I am Kuber, the controller of wealth. Among the Vasus, I am Agni and among the mountains I am Meru.
shlokaList.name: Among the Rudras, know Me as Shankara, among the Yakshasas and Rakshasas, I am Kuber, the controller of wealth. Among the Vasus, I am Agni and among the mountains I am Meru.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rudrāṇām śhaṅkaraśh chāsmi vitteśho yakṣha-rakṣhasām
vasūnām pāvakaśh chāsmi meruḥ śhikhariṇām aham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.23 (10.21 to 10.24)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.24: O Partha, among the priests, I am Brihaspati; of the commanders-in-chief, I am Skandah (Kartikeya); and among bodies of water, know Me as the ocean.
shlokaList.name: O Partha, among the priests, I am Brihaspati; of the commanders-in-chief, I am Skandah (Kartikeya); and among bodies of water, know Me as the ocean.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
purodhasām cha mukhyam mām viddhi pārtha bṛihaspatim
senānīnām aham skandaḥ sarasām asmi sāgaraḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.24 (10.21 to 10.24)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.25: Among the great sages, I am Bhrigu; among sounds, I am the syllable OM; among sacrifices, know Me as japa - the chanting of divine names; among immovable things, I am the Himalayas.
shlokaList.name: Among the great sages, I am Bhrigu; among sounds, I am the syllable OM; among sacrifices, know Me as japa - the chanting of divine names; among immovable things, I am the Himalayas.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
maharṣhīṇām bhṛigur aham girām asmyekam akṣharam
yajñānām japa-yajño ’smi sthāvarāṇām himālayaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.25 (10.25 to 10.28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.26: Among trees, I am the Ashvattha tree; among the celestial sages, I am Narada. Among the Gandharvas, I am Chitraratha and among the Siddhas, I am the sage Kapila.
shlokaList.name: Among trees, I am the Ashvattha tree; among the celestial sages, I am Narada. Among the Gandharvas, I am Chitraratha and among the Siddhas, I am the sage Kapila.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aśhvatthaḥ sarva-vṛikṣhāṇām devarṣhīṇām cha nāradaḥ
gandharvāṇām chitrarathaḥ siddhānām kapilo muniḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.26 (10.25 to 10.28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.27: Among the horses, know Me as Ucchaihshrava, born from the churning of the ocean for nectar. I am Airavata among lordly elephants. And among men, I am the King.
shlokaList.name: Among the horses, know Me as Ucchaihshrava, born from the churning of the ocean for nectar. I am Airavata among lordly elephants. And among men, I am the King.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
uchchaiḥśhravasam aśhvānām viddhi mām amṛitodbhavam
airāvatam gajendrāṇām narāṇām cha narādhipam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.27 (10.25 to 10.28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.28: I am the Vajra, the thunderbolt, among weapons and Kamadhenu among the cows. I am Kandarpa, the god of love and the cause of procreation and among serpents, I am Vasuki.
shlokaList.name: I am the Vajra, the thunderbolt, among weapons and Kamadhenu among the cows. I am Kandarpa, the god of love and the cause of procreation and among serpents, I am Vasuki.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
āyudhānām aham vajram dhenūnām asmi kāmadhuk
prajanaśh chāsmi kandarpaḥ sarpāṇām asmi vāsukiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.28 (10.25 to 10.28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.29: Among the Nagas, I am Ananta, among aquatic beings, I am Varuna. Among the ancestors, I am Aryama; among the executors of law, I am Yama, the lord of death.
shlokaList.name: Among the Nagas, I am Ananta, among aquatic beings, I am Varuna. Among the ancestors, I am Aryama; among the executors of law, I am Yama, the lord of death.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
anantaśh chāsmi nāgānām varuṇo yādasām aham
pitṝīṇām aryamā chāsmi yamaḥ sanyamatām aham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.29 (10.29 to 10.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.30: Among the daityas, I am Prahlada. I am kala (time) among the subduers. Among beasts, I am the lion and among the birds, I am Garuda.
shlokaList.name: Among the daityas, I am Prahlada. I am kala (time) among the subduers. Among beasts, I am the lion and among the birds, I am Garuda.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prahlādaśh chāsmi daityānām kālaḥ kalayatām aham
mṛigāṇām cha mṛigendro ’ham vainateyaśh cha pakṣhiṇām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.30 (10.29 to 10.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.31: Among the purifiers, I am the wind. Among the warriors, I am Rama. Among the aquatics, I am Makara (the alligator). Among the rivers, I am Jahnavi (the Ganges).
shlokaList.name: Among the purifiers, I am the wind. Among the warriors, I am Rama. Among the aquatics, I am Makara (the alligator). Among the rivers, I am Jahnavi (the Ganges).
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pavanaḥ pavatām asmi rāmaḥ śhastra-bhṛitām aham
jhaṣhāṇām makaraśh chāsmi srotasām asmi jāhnavī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.31 (10.29 to 10.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.32: O Arjuna! Among all created things, I am the beginning, the end and the middle. I am the science of the self among all sciences and among those skilled in debate, I am the conclusive truth.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna! Among all created things, I am the beginning, the end and the middle. I am the science of the self among all sciences and among those skilled in debate, I am the conclusive truth.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sargāṇām ādir antaśh cha madhyam chaivāham arjuna
adhyātma-vidyā vidyānām vādaḥ pravadatām aham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.32 (10.29 to 10.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.33: Among the letters, I am A (the first letter of the alphabet). Among compound forms (samasas), I am dvandva (the dual compound). I alone am the imperishable time. I am the creator Brahma.
shlokaList.name: Among the letters, I am A (the first letter of the alphabet). Among compound forms (samasas), I am dvandva (the dual compound). I alone am the imperishable time. I am the creator Brahma.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
akṣharāṇām a-kāro ’smi dvandvaḥ sāmāsikasya cha
aham evākṣhayaḥ kālo dhātāham viśhvato-mukhaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.33 (10.29 to 10.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.34: I am the all-consuming death. I am the source of all that is yet to be. Among women, I am fame, prosperity, fine speech, good memory, intelligence, determination and forgiveness.
shlokaList.name: I am the all-consuming death. I am the source of all that is yet to be. Among women, I am fame, prosperity, fine speech, good memory, intelligence, determination and forgiveness.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mṛityuḥ sarva-haraśh chāham udbhavaśh cha bhaviṣhyatām
kīrtiḥ śhrīr vāk cha nārīṇām smṛitir medhā dhṛitiḥ kṣhamā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.34

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.35: Likewise, among the Samaveda hymns, I am the Bṛihatsama. Among the chandas, I am the Gayatri. Among the months, I am Margashirsha and among the seasons, I am the flower-bearing spring.
shlokaList.name: Likewise, among the Samaveda hymns, I am the Bṛihatsama. Among the chandas, I am the Gayatri. Among the months, I am Margashirsha and among the seasons, I am the flower-bearing spring.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम् |
मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकर: || 35||
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.35 (10.35 to 10.39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.36: Among the fraudulent activities, I am the gambling and among the lustrous persons, I am their lustre. I am the victory, I am the endeavour, I am the goodness of virtuous people.
shlokaList.name: Among the fraudulent activities, I am the gambling and among the lustrous persons, I am their lustre. I am the victory, I am the endeavour, I am the goodness of virtuous people.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dyūtam chhalayatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham
jayo ’smi vyavasāyo ’smi sattvam sattvavatām aham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.36 (10.35 to 10.39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.37: I am Vasudeva, among the Vrsni clan. I am Dhananjaya, among the Pandavas. Also, among the sages, I am Vyasa and among the great poets, I am Ushana (Shukracharya)
shlokaList.name: I am Vasudeva, among the Vrsni clan. I am Dhananjaya, among the Pandavas. Also, among the sages, I am Vyasa and among the great poets, I am Ushana (Shukracharya)
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vṛiṣhṇīnām vāsudevo ’smi pāṇḍavānām dhanañjayaḥ
munīnām apyaham vyāsaḥ kavīnām uśhanā kaviḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.37 (10.35 to 10.39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.38: I am punishment among means of controlling lawlessness. I am the morality of those who seek victory. I am the silence among the secrets and I am the wisdom of the wise.
shlokaList.name: I am punishment among means of controlling lawlessness. I am the morality of those who seek victory. I am the silence among the secrets and I am the wisdom of the wise.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
daṇḍo damayatām asmi nītir asmi jigīṣhatām
maunam chaivāsmi guhyānām jñānam jñānavatām aham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.38 (10.35 to 10.39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.39: And also, O Arjuna, I am the seed of all existence. There is nothing, movable or immovable, that can exist without Me.
shlokaList.name: And also, O Arjuna, I am the seed of all existence. There is nothing, movable or immovable, that can exist without Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yach chāpi sarva-bhūtānām bījam tad aham arjuna
na tad asti vinā yat syān mayā bhūtam charācharam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.39 (10.35 to 10.39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.40: O Parantapa! There is no end to My divine manifestations. Whatever has been explained is only a brief account of My endless divine manifestations.
shlokaList.name: O Parantapa! There is no end to My divine manifestations. Whatever has been explained is only a brief account of My endless divine manifestations.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nānto ’sti mama divyānām vibhūtīnām parantapa
eṣha tūddeśhataḥ prokto vibhūter vistaro mayā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.40 (10.40 to 10.41)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.41: Whatever that is endowed with glory, power or beauty, know that it springs from a part of My divine splendour.
shlokaList.name: Whatever that is endowed with glory, power or beauty, know that it springs from a part of My divine splendour.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yad yad vibhūtimat sattvam śhrīmad ūrjitam eva vā
tat tad evāvagachchha tvam mama tejo ’nśha-sambhavam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.41 (10.40 to 10.41)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 10.42: O Arjuna, what benefit will you gain from all this detailed knowledge? Just know that I alone pervade and sustain the entire universe with just one fraction of Myself.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna, what benefit will you gain from all this detailed knowledge? Just know that I alone pervade and sustain the entire universe with just one fraction of Myself.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
atha vā bahunaitena kim jñātena tavārjuna
viṣhṭabhyāham idam kṛitsnam ekānśhena sthito jagat
shlokaList.title: Shloka 10.42

chapterList.description: Chapter 11: Vishwaroopa Darshana Yoga - The Yoga of the Cosmic Vision
chapterList.name: Chapter 11: Vishwaroopa Darshana Yoga - The Yoga of the Cosmic Vision

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.1: Arjuna said - Having received this profoundly confidential spiritual knowledge, that You have revealed out of compassion for me, my ignorance is now dispelled.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - Having received this profoundly confidential spiritual knowledge, that You have revealed out of compassion for me, my ignorance is now dispelled.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
mad-anugrahāya paramam guhyam adhyātma-sanjñitam
yat tvayoktam vachas tena moho ’yam vigato mama
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.1 (11.1 to 11.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.2: O lotus-eyed One! I have heard from You in detail about the creation and dissolution of all living beings and also about Your eternal glories.
shlokaList.name: O lotus-eyed One! I have heard from You in detail about the creation and dissolution of all living beings and also about Your eternal glories.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bhavāpyayau hi bhūtānām śhrutau vistaraśho mayā
tvattaḥ kamala-patrākṣha māhātmyam api chāvyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.2 (11.1 to 11.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.3: O the Supreme Lord, I wish to see that divine form of Yours by which You pervade this entire creation, exactly the way You have described it, O best of all beings!
shlokaList.name: O the Supreme Lord, I wish to see that divine form of Yours by which You pervade this entire creation, exactly the way You have described it, O best of all beings!
shlokaList.sanskritText:
evam etad yathāttha tvam ātmānam parameśhvara
draṣhṭum ichchhāmi te rūpam aiśhwaram puruṣhottama
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.3 (11.1 to 11.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.4: O Lord! If You think that it is possible for me to behold Your divine cosmic form, then O Krishna, O master of all Yogas, please reveal Your boundless form to me.
shlokaList.name: O Lord! If You think that it is possible for me to behold Your divine cosmic form, then O Krishna, O master of all Yogas, please reveal Your boundless form to me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
manyase yadi tach chhakyam mayā draṣhṭum iti prabho
yogeśhvara tato me tvam darśhayātmānam avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.4 (11.1 to 11.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.5: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Partha! Now see hundreds and thousands of variations of My manifold celestial forms, which are of various colours and shapes.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Partha! Now see hundreds and thousands of variations of My manifold celestial forms, which are of various colours and shapes.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
paśhya me pārtha rūpāṇi śhataśho ’tha sahasraśhaḥ
nānā-vidhāni divyāni nānā-varṇākṛitīni cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.5 (11.5 to 11.7)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.6: O scion of Bharata, now see here the Adityas, Vasus, Rudras, Ashvini Kumaras, Maruts, and countless other wonders never revealed before.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata, now see here the Adityas, Vasus, Rudras, Ashvini Kumaras, Maruts, and countless other wonders never revealed before.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
paśhyādityān vasūn rudrān aśhvinau marutas tathā
bahūny adṛiṣhṭa-pūrvāṇi paśhyāśhcharyāṇi bhārata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.6 (11.5 to 11.7)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.7: Now, Arjuna, behold the entire universein one place, with all moving and non-moving entities assembled in My universal form. Whatever else you wish to witness, observe it all within this expansive universal manifestation.
shlokaList.name: Now, Arjuna, behold the entire universein one place, with all moving and non-moving entities assembled in My universal form. Whatever else you wish to witness, observe it all within this expansive universal manifestation.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ihaika-stham jagat kṛitsnam paśhyādya sa-charācharam
mama dehe guḍākeśha yach chānyad draṣhṭum ichchhasi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.7 (11.5 to 11.7)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.8: But you cannot see Me with your physical eyes. Therefore, I will give you divine eyes. Now watch My divine mystic powers.
shlokaList.name: But you cannot see Me with your physical eyes. Therefore, I will give you divine eyes. Now watch My divine mystic powers.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na tu mām śhakyase draṣhṭum anenaiva sva-chakṣhuṣhā
divyam dadāmi te chakṣhuḥ paśhya me yogam aiśhwaram
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.9: Sanjaya said - O King! Having spoken thus, Lord Hari, the Supreme Master of Yoga showed His divine cosmic form to Partha!
shlokaList.name: Sanjaya said - O King! Having spoken thus, Lord Hari, the Supreme Master of Yoga showed His divine cosmic form to Partha!
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sañjaya uvācha
evam uktvā tato rājan mahā-yogeśhvaro hariḥ
darśhayām āsa pārthāya paramam rūpam aiśhwaram
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.9 (11.9 to 11.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.10: Adorned with many mouths and eyes, displaying various extraordinary sights. Decorated with many celestial ornaments and wielding numerous divine weapons;
shlokaList.name: Adorned with many mouths and eyes, displaying various extraordinary sights. Decorated with many celestial ornaments and wielding numerous divine weapons;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aneka-vaktra-nayanam anekādbhuta-darśhanam
aneka-divyābharaṇam divyānekodyatāyudham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.10 (11.9 to 11.14)
