shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.11: That form was adorned with divine garlands and garment sand smeared with divine fragrances. Everything was wonderful, brilliant, infinite and extending in all directions.
shlokaList.name: That form was adorned with divine garlands and garment sand smeared with divine fragrances. Everything was wonderful, brilliant, infinite and extending in all directions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
divya-mālyāmbara-dharam divya-gandhānulepanam
sarvāśhcharya-mayam devam anantam viśhvato-mukham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.11 (11.9 to 11.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.12: If thousands of suns were to rise simultaneously in the sky, their radiance might compare to the effulgence of this form of the Supreme Being. However, those who have given up attachment to the results of their action do not experience any such consequences.
shlokaList.name: If thousands of suns were to rise simultaneously in the sky, their radiance might compare to the effulgence of this form of the Supreme Being. However, those who have given up attachment to the results of their action do not experience any such consequences.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
divi sūrya-sahasrasya bhaved yugapad utthitā
yadi bhāḥ sadṛiśhī sā syād bhāsas tasya mahātmanaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.12 (11.9 to 11.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.13: There at that time, Arjuna could see the entire universe with all its divisions in one place, within the divine cosmic form of the master of all devatas.
shlokaList.name: There at that time, Arjuna could see the entire universe with all its divisions in one place, within the divine cosmic form of the master of all devatas.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tatraika-stham jagat kṛitsnam pravibhaktam anekadhā
apaśhyad deva-devasya śharīre pāṇḍavas tadā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.13 (11.9 to 11.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.14: And then Dhananjaya became overwhelmed with astonishment, with his hair standing on end, he bowed his head to the Supreme Being and with folded hands he spoke thus;
shlokaList.name: And then Dhananjaya became overwhelmed with astonishment, with his hair standing on end, he bowed his head to the Supreme Being and with folded hands he spoke thus;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tataḥ sa vismayāviṣhṭo hṛiṣhṭa-romā dhanañjayaḥ
praṇamya śhirasā devam kṛitāñjalir abhāṣhata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.14 (11.9 to 11.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.15: Arjuna said - O Lord! I see all the devatas and various other living beings gathered within Your divine form. I see Brahma seated on the lotus, Shiva, the great sages and the divine serpents.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Lord! I see all the devatas and various other living beings gathered within Your divine form. I see Brahma seated on the lotus, Shiva, the great sages and the divine serpents.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
paśhyāmi devāns tava deva dehe
sarvāns tathā bhūta-viśheṣha-saṅghān
brahmāṇam īśham kamalāsana-stham
ṛiṣhīnśh cha sarvān uragānśh cha divyān
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.15 (11.15 to 11.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.16: O controller of the Universe! I behold You with many arms, bellies, mouths, and eyes. Your infinite forms are expanded everywhere. O Universal Form! I perceive You without end, without middle and without any beginning.
shlokaList.name: O controller of the Universe! I behold You with many arms, bellies, mouths, and eyes. Your infinite forms are expanded everywhere. O Universal Form! I perceive You without end, without middle and without any beginning.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aneka-bāhūdara-vaktra-netram
paśhyāmi tvām sarvato ’nanta-rūpam
nāntam na madhyam na punas tavādim
paśhyāmi viśhveśhvara viśhva-rūpa
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.16 (11.15 to 11.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.17: I see You wearing a crown and holding a mace and discus, with an immense radiance shining everywhere. Your brilliance is immeasurable, like the blazing fire or the shining sun. Therefore, it is extremely difficult to see this form.
shlokaList.name: I see You wearing a crown and holding a mace and discus, with an immense radiance shining everywhere. Your brilliance is immeasurable, like the blazing fire or the shining sun. Therefore, it is extremely difficult to see this form.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kirīṭinam gadinam chakriṇam cha
tejo-rāśhim sarvato dīptimantam
paśhyāmi tvām durnirīkṣhyam samantād
dīptānalārka-dyutim aprameyam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.17 (11.15 to 11.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.18: You are to be understood as the supreme, imperishable being, You are the ultimate foundation of this world, You are indestructible, You are the upholder of eternal dharma and You are the eternal Supreme Person, this is my opinion.
shlokaList.name: You are to be understood as the supreme, imperishable being, You are the ultimate foundation of this world, You are indestructible, You are the upholder of eternal dharma and You are the eternal Supreme Person, this is my opinion.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tvam akṣharam paramam veditavyam
tvam asya viśhvasya param nidhānam
tvam avyayaḥ śhāśhvata-dharma-goptā
sanātanas tvam puruṣho mato me
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.18 (11.15 to 11.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.19: I see that You have no beginning, middle, or end. You possess immense valour. You have countless arms and the sun and the moon are Your eyes. I also see blazing fire emanating from Your mouth, burning the entire universe with Your own radiance.
shlokaList.name: I see that You have no beginning, middle, or end. You possess immense valour. You have countless arms and the sun and the moon are Your eyes. I also see blazing fire emanating from Your mouth, burning the entire universe with Your own radiance.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
anādi-madhyāntam ananta-vīryam
ananta-bāhum śhaśhi-sūrya-netram
paśhyāmi tvām dīpta-hutāśha-vaktram
sva-tejasā viśhvam idam tapantam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.19 (11.19 to 11.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.20: The space between the earth and sky, and all directions are filled by You alone. O mighty Self! Having seen Your powerful and wondrous form, the three worlds are disturbed.
shlokaList.name: The space between the earth and sky, and all directions are filled by You alone. O mighty Self! Having seen Your powerful and wondrous form, the three worlds are disturbed.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dyāv ā-pṛithivyor idam antaram hi
vyāptam tvayaikena diśhaśh cha sarvāḥ
dṛiṣhṭvādbhutam rūpam ugram tavedam
loka-trayam pravyathitam mahātman
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.20 (11.19 to 11.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.21: The groups of celestial beings are entering into Your cosmic form, some out of fear, are joining their palms and offering prayers. The great sages and perfectly qualified beings are offering auspicious Vedic hymns, entreating You for peace.
shlokaList.name: The groups of celestial beings are entering into Your cosmic form, some out of fear, are joining their palms and offering prayers. The great sages and perfectly qualified beings are offering auspicious Vedic hymns, entreating You for peace.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
amī hi tvām sura-saṅghā viśhanti
kechid bhītāḥ prāñjalayo gṛiṇanti
svastīty uktvā maharṣhi-siddha-saṅghāḥ
stuvanti tvām stutibhiḥ puṣhkalābhiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.21 (11.19 to 11.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.22: The Rudras, the Adityas, the Vasus, the Sadhyas, the Vishvadevas the Ashvini Kumaras, the Maruts, the celestial forefathers, the Gandharvas, the Yaksas, the Asuras and the Siddhas are all beholding You in wonder.
shlokaList.name: The Rudras, the Adityas, the Vasus, the Sadhyas, the Vishvadevas the Ashvini Kumaras, the Maruts, the celestial forefathers, the Gandharvas, the Yaksas, the Asuras and the Siddhas are all beholding You in wonder.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rudrādityā vasavo ye cha sādhyā
viśhve ’śhvinau marutaśh choṣhmapāśh cha
gandharva-yakṣhāsura-siddha-saṅghā
vīkṣhante tvām vismitāśh chaiva sarve
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.22 (11.19 to 11.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.23: O mighty-armed Lord! Beholding this great form of Yours with many faces, eyes, arms, thighs, feet, bellies and many fearsome teeth; all the worlds are disturbed and so am I.
shlokaList.name: O mighty-armed Lord! Beholding this great form of Yours with many faces, eyes, arms, thighs, feet, bellies and many fearsome teeth; all the worlds are disturbed and so am I.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rūpam mahat te bahu-vaktra-netram
mahā-bāho bahu-bāhūru-pādam
bahūdaram bahu-danṣhṭrā-karālam
dṛiṣhṭvā lokāḥ pravyathitās tathāham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.23 (11.19 to 11.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.24: O Lord Vishnu! Beholding Your radiant and multicolored form stretching to the sky, with Your mouth wide open and large glowing eyes, I am disturbed internally, and I am unable to keep my mind steady or calm.
shlokaList.name: O Lord Vishnu! Beholding Your radiant and multicolored form stretching to the sky, with Your mouth wide open and large glowing eyes, I am disturbed internally, and I am unable to keep my mind steady or calm.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nabhaḥ-spṛiśham dīptam aneka-varṇam
vyāttānanam dīpta-viśhāla-netram
dṛiṣhṭvā hi tvām pravyathitāntar-ātmā
dhṛitim na vindāmi śhamam cha viṣhṇo
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.24 (11.24 to 11.30)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.25: Witnessing your terrifying teeth and fiery mouths resembling the fire of destruction, I have lost all sense of direction and I am confused. Therefore, O shelter of the universe! O master of the devatas! Grace me with Your mercy.
shlokaList.name: Witnessing your terrifying teeth and fiery mouths resembling the fire of destruction, I have lost all sense of direction and I am confused. Therefore, O shelter of the universe! O master of the devatas! Grace me with Your mercy.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
danṣhṭrā-karālāni cha te mukhāni
dṛiṣhṭvaiva kālānala-sannibhāni
diśho na jāne na labhe cha śharma
prasīda deveśha jagan-nivāsa
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.25 (11.24 to 11.30)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.26: All the sons of Dhritarashtra, along with the groups of kings, Bhisma, Drona, Karna, and also our prime warriors;
shlokaList.name: All the sons of Dhritarashtra, along with the groups of kings, Bhisma, Drona, Karna, and also our prime warriors;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
amī cha tvām dhṛitarāśhtrasya putrāḥ
sarve sahaivāvani-pāla-saṅghaiḥ
bhīṣhmo droṇaḥ sūta-putras tathāsau
sahāsmadīyair api yodha-mukhyaiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.26 (11.24 to 11.30)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.27: : They are all rushing towards and entering Your fearful mouths with terrible teeth. Some are seen caught in between the teeth, with their heads crushed.
shlokaList.name: They are all rushing towards and entering Your fearful mouths with terrible teeth. Some are seen caught in between the teeth, with their heads crushed.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vaktrāṇi te tvaramāṇā viśhanti
danṣhṭrā-karālāni bhayānakāni
kechid vilagnā daśhanāntareṣhu
sandṛiśhyante chūrṇitair uttamāṅgaiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.27 (11.24 to 11.30)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.28: The doer who acts against the scriptures, is materialistic, stubborn, deceitful, vulgar, lazy, morose and dilatory is known as a Tamasik-Karta.
shlokaList.name: The doer who acts against the scriptures, is materialistic, stubborn, deceitful, vulgar, lazy, morose and dilatory is known as a Tamasik-Karta.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yathā nadīnām bahavo ’mbu-vegāḥ
samudram evābhimukhā dravanti
tathā tavāmī nara-loka-vīrā
viśhanti vaktrāṇy abhivijvalanti
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.28 (11.24 to 11.30)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.29: Just as insects rush swiftly into a blazing fire for their destruction, similarly all men are rushing into Your mouths for their destruction.
shlokaList.name: Just as insects rush swiftly into a blazing fire for their destruction, similarly all men are rushing into Your mouths for their destruction.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yathā pradīptam jvalanam pataṅgā
viśhanti nāśhāya samṛiddha-vegāḥ
tathaiva nāśhāya viśhanti lokās
tavāpi vaktrāṇi samṛiddha-vegāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.29 (11.24 to 11.30)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.30: O Lord Vishnu! You are engulfing and consuming all beings from every direction with Your fiery mouths. Your radiant effulgence is covering the entire universe, scorching it with fierce radiance.
shlokaList.name: O Lord Vishnu! You are engulfing and consuming all beings from every direction with Your fiery mouths. Your radiant effulgence is covering the entire universe, scorching it with fierce radiance.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
lelihyase grasamānaḥ samantāl
lokān samagrān vadanair jvaladbhiḥ
tejobhir āpūrya jagat samagram
bhāsas tavogrāḥ pratapanti viṣhṇo
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.30 (11.24 to 11.30)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.31: Please reveal to me who You are, possessing such a fierce form. O Supreme one amongst the devatas! I bow down to You. Favor me with Your grace. I desire to understand You, the original supreme being, as I am uncertain of Your intentions.
shlokaList.name: Please reveal to me who You are, possessing such a fierce form. O Supreme one amongst the devatas! I bow down to You. Favor me with Your grace. I desire to understand You, the original supreme being, as I am uncertain of Your intentions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ākhyāhi me ko bhavān ugra-rūpo
namo ’stu te deva-vara prasīda
vijñātum ichchhāmi bhavantam ādyam
na hi prajānāmi tava pravṛittim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.31

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.32: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - I am Time, the great Kala, the great destroyer of the worlds, that destroys everyone and everything in this world. Even if you do not fight, the warriors standing on either side will be destroyed.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - I am Time, the great Kala, the great destroyer of the worlds, that destroys everyone and everything in this world. Even if you do not fight, the warriors standing on either side will be destroyed.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
kālo ’smi loka-kṣhaya-kṛit pravṛiddho
lokān samāhartum iha pravṛittaḥ
ṛite ’pi tvām na bhaviṣhyanti sarve
ye ’vasthitāḥ pratyanīkeṣhu yodhāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.32

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.33: Therefore, rise and attain glory, conquer your enemies, and enjoy a prosperous kingdom. All these enemies have already been slain by Me. O Savyasaci! Just become an instrument in this endeavour.
shlokaList.name: Therefore, rise and attain glory, conquer your enemies, and enjoy a prosperous kingdom. All these enemies have already been slain by Me. O Savyasaci! Just become an instrument in this endeavour.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tasmāt tvam uttiṣhṭha yaśho labhasva
jitvā śhatrūn bhuṅkṣhva rājyam samṛiddham
mayaivaite nihatāḥ pūrvam eva
nimitta-mātram bhava savya-sāchin
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.33

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.34: Drona, Bhisma, Jayadratha, Karna and other great fighters as well, are already slain by Me. Destroy them without being disturbed. Just fight and you will defeat all your enemies in the war.
shlokaList.name: Drona, Bhisma, Jayadratha, Karna and other great fighters as well, are already slain by Me. Destroy them without being disturbed. Just fight and you will defeat all your enemies in the war.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
droṇam cha bhīṣhmam cha jayadratham cha
karṇam tathānyān api yodha-vīrān
mayā hatāms tvam jahi mā vyathiṣhṭhā
yudhyasva jetāsi raṇe sapatnān
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.34

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.35: Sanjaya said - After hearing Lord Keshava’s words, a trembling Arjuna bowed down to Sri Krishna with joined palms again and again. And in a choked voice filled with fear, he spoke to Him.
shlokaList.name: Sanjaya said - After hearing Lord Keshava’s words, a trembling Arjuna bowed down to Sri Krishna with joined palms again and again. And in a choked voice filled with fear, he spoke to Him.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sañjaya uvācha
etach chhrutvā vachanam keśhavasya
kṛitāñjalir vepamānaḥ kirīṭī
namaskṛitvā bhūya evāha kṛiṣhṇam
sa-gadgadam bhīta-bhītaḥ praṇamya
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.35 (11.35 to 11.40)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.36: Arjuna said - O Hrishikesh! It is appropriate that the entire universe rejoices in praising You and thus becomes attached to You. The demons flee in all directions out of fear, while the perfected beings offer their respects to You.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Hrishikesh! It is appropriate that the entire universe rejoices in praising You and thus becomes attached to You. The demons flee in all directions out of fear, while the perfected beings offer their respects to You.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
sthāne hṛiṣhīkeśha tava prakīrtyā
jagat prahṛiṣhyaty anurajyate cha
rakṣhānsi bhītāni diśho dravanti
sarve namasyanti cha siddha-saṅghāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.36 (11.35 to 11.40)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.37: O Great One! Why would they not bow down to You? You are even greater than Brahma, You are the original creator. O the limitless One! O master of the devas! O shelter of the universe! You are imperishable and You are beyond both the existing and non-existing realms.
shlokaList.name: O Great One! Why would they not bow down to You? You are even greater than Brahma, You are the original creator. O the limitless One! O master of the devas! O shelter of the universe! You are imperishable and You are beyond both the existing and non-existing realms.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kasmāch cha te na nameran mahātman
garīyase brahmaṇo ’py ādi-kartre
ananta deveśha jagan-nivāsa
tvam akṣharam sad-asat tat param yat
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.37 (11.35 to 11.40)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.38: You are the original Supreme Being, the most ancient personality and the supreme shelter of this universe. You are both the knower and the worthiest of being known. You are the ultimate refuge. O One with infinite forms! By You, the entire universe is pervaded.
shlokaList.name: You are the original Supreme Being, the most ancient personality and the supreme shelter of this universe. You are both the knower and the worthiest of being known. You are the ultimate refuge. O One with infinite forms! By You, the entire universe is pervaded.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tvam ādi-devaḥ puruṣhaḥ purāṇas
tvam asya viśhvasya param nidhānam
vettāsi vedyam cha param cha dhāma
tvayā tatam viśhvam ananta-rūpa
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.38 (11.35 to 11.40)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.39: You are Vayu, Yamaraj, Agni, Varuna, Chandra. You are the Prajapati and the great-grandfather of all living beings. I bow down to You, I bow down a thousand times, I bow down again and again.
shlokaList.name: You are Vayu, Yamaraj, Agni, Varuna, Chandra. You are the Prajapati and the great-grandfather of all living beings. I bow down to You, I bow down a thousand times, I bow down again and again.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vāyur yamo ’gnir varuṇaḥ śhaśhāṅkaḥ
prajāpatis tvam prapitāmahaśh cha
namo namas te ’stu sahasra-kṛitvaḥ
punaśh cha bhūyo ’pi namo namas te
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.39 (11.35 to 11.40)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.40: O Lord of boundless power, I offer my salutations to You from every direction—from the front, from behind and from all sides! You possess infinite valor and strength, permeating everything; indeed, You are everything.
shlokaList.name: O Lord of boundless power, I offer my salutations to You from every direction—from the front, from behind and from all sides! You possess infinite valor and strength, permeating everything; indeed, You are everything.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
namaḥ purastād atha pṛiṣhṭhatas te
namo ’stu te sarvata eva sarva
ananta-vīryāmita-vikramas tvam
sarvam samāpnoṣhi tato ’si sarvaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.40 (11.35 to 11.40)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.41: Thinking You to be my friend, out of ignorance I called you “O Krishna! O Yadav, O my friend!” Not knowing your glories, I said those words due to foolishness or due to affection.
shlokaList.name: Thinking You to be my friend, out of ignorance I called you “O Krishna! O Yadav, O my friend!” Not knowing your glories, I said those words due to foolishness or due to affection.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sakheti matvā prasabham yad uktam
he kṛiṣhṇa he yādava he sakheti
ajānatā mahimānam tavedam
mayā pramādāt praṇayena vāpi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.41 (11.41 to 11.42)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.42: Jokingly, I have disrespected You during our leisure time, while resting, sitting, or eating together, sometimes alone and sometimes in the presence of others. O infallible Lord! I seek forgiveness for these transgressions, O the immeasurable One!
shlokaList.name: Jokingly, I have disrespected You during our leisure time, while resting, sitting, or eating together, sometimes alone and sometimes in the presence of others. O infallible Lord! I seek forgiveness for these transgressions, O the immeasurable One!
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yach chāvahāsārtham asat-kṛito ’si
vihāra-śhayyāsana-bhojaneṣhu
eko ’tha vāpy achyuta tat-samakṣham
tat kṣhāmaye tvām aham aprameyam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.42 (11.41 to 11.42)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.43: You are the father of all the worlds, of both moving and non moving beings. You are worthy of worship and the supreme teacher. O Lord, with unimaginable power! How can anyone in the three worlds be greater than You, when no one even matches Your might?
shlokaList.name: You are the father of all the worlds, of both moving and non moving beings. You are worthy of worship and the supreme teacher. O Lord, with unimaginable power! How can anyone in the three worlds be greater than You, when no one even matches Your might?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pitāsi lokasya charācharasya
tvam asya pūjyaśh cha gurur garīyān
na tvat-samo ’sty abhyadhikaḥ kuto ’nyo
loka-traye ’py apratima-prabhāva
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.43 (11.43 to 11.44)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.44: Therefore, O beloved worshipable Lord, I offer my respects and I prostrate before You. O Lord! I beseech Your grace. Just as a father forgives his child, as a friend forgives another friend and as a lover forgives the beloved, similarly please pardon me for my mistakes.
shlokaList.name: Therefore, O beloved worshipable Lord, I offer my respects and I prostrate before You. O Lord! I beseech Your grace. Just as a father forgives his child, as a friend forgives another friend and as a lover forgives the beloved, similarly please pardon me for my mistakes.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tasmāt praṇamya praṇidhāya kāyam
prasādaye tvām aham īśham īḍyam
piteva putrasya sakheva sakhyuḥ
priyaḥ priyāyārhasi deva soḍhum
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.44 (11.43 to 11.44)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.45: O master of the devatas! Beholding your unprecedented universal form, which was never seen before, enlivens me and at the same time disturbs my mind with fear. Therefore, O refuge of the universe, please be gracious upon me and again reveal Your divine form.
shlokaList.name: O master of the devatas! Beholding your unprecedented universal form, which was never seen before, enlivens me and at the same time disturbs my mind with fear. Therefore, O refuge of the universe, please be gracious upon me and again reveal Your divine form.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adṛiṣhṭa-pūrvam hṛiṣhito ’smi dṛiṣhṭvā
bhayena cha pravyathitam mano me
tad eva me darśhaya deva rūpam
prasīda deveśha jagan-nivāsa
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.45 (11.45 to 11.46)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.46: O the one adorned with a thousand hands! I desire to see You wearing a crown, holding a mace and discus in Your hands. O universal form! Please manifest Yourself in Your four-armed appearance.
shlokaList.name: O the one adorned with a thousand hands! I desire to see You wearing a crown, holding a mace and discus in Your hands. O universal form! Please manifest Yourself in Your four-armed appearance.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kirīṭinam gadinam chakra-hastam
ichchhāmi tvām draṣhṭum aham tathaiva
tenaiva rūpeṇa chatur-bhujena
sahasra-bāho bhava viśhva-mūrte
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.46 (11.45 to 11.46)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.47: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Arjuna, I am pleased with you and through My divine mystic powers, I have revealed to you My supreme universal form which is radiant, all pervading, infinite, and original. This form of Mine has never been seen by anyone else but you.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Arjuna, I am pleased with you and through My divine mystic powers, I have revealed to you My supreme universal form which is radiant, all pervading, infinite, and original. This form of Mine has never been seen by anyone else but you.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
mayā prasannena tavārjunedam
rūpam param darśhitam ātma-yogāt
tejo-mayam viśhvam anantam ādyam
yan me tvad anyena na dṛiṣhṭa-pūrvam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.47 (11.47 to 11.49)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.48: O best of the Kuru warriors! No one in this world except you can see this form even through the study of the Vedas, fire sacrifices, charity, pious works or intense penances.
shlokaList.name: O best of the Kuru warriors! No one in this world except you can see this form even through the study of the Vedas, fire sacrifices, charity, pious works or intense penances.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na veda-yajñādhyayanair na dānair
na cha kriyābhir na tapobhir ugraiḥ
evam-rūpaḥ śhakya aham nṛi-loke
draṣhṭum tvad anyena kuru-pravīra
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.48 (11.47 to 11.49)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.49: Be free of all trouble and confusion that has arisen because of seeing this terrible universal form of Mine. Now free of fear and with a delighted heart, once again see My form that you desire.
shlokaList.name: Be free of all trouble and confusion that has arisen because of seeing this terrible universal form of Mine. Now free of fear and with a delighted heart, once again see My form that you desire.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mā te vyathā mā cha vimūḍha-bhāvo
dṛiṣhṭvā rūpam ghoram īdṛiṅ mamedam
vyapeta-bhīḥ prīta-manāḥ punas tvam
tad eva me rūpam idam prapaśhya
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.49 (11.47 to 11.49)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.50: Sanjaya said - Saying this, Lord Vasudeva once again revealed His original (four-handed) form to Arjuna. And to further reassure the frightened Arjuna, He then appeared in His gentle (two-armed) form.
shlokaList.name: Sanjaya said - Saying this, Lord Vasudeva once again revealed His original (four-handed) form to Arjuna. And to further reassure the frightened Arjuna, He then appeared in His gentle (two-armed) form.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sañjaya uvācha
ity arjunam vāsudevas tathoktvā
svakam rūpam darśhayām āsa bhūyaḥ
āśhvāsayām āsa cha bhītam enam
bhūtvā punaḥ saumya-vapur mahātmā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.50 (11.50 to 11.51)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.51: Arjuna said - O Janardana! Now that I have seen your gentle human-like form, I feel composed in mind and I feel naturally situated.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Janardana! Now that I have seen your gentle human-like form, I feel composed in mind and I feel naturally situated.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
dṛiṣhṭvedam mānuṣham rūpam tava saumyam janārdana
idānīm asmi samvṛittaḥ sa-chetāḥ prakṛitim gataḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.51 (11.50 to 11.51)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.52: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - You have seen that form of mine, which is exceedingly rare to behold. Even the devatas always desire to witness this form.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - You have seen that form of mine, which is exceedingly rare to behold. Even the devatas always desire to witness this form.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
su-durdarśham idam rūpam dṛiṣhṭavān asi yan mama
devā apy asya rūpasya nityam darśhana-kāṅkṣhiṇaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.52 (11.52 to 11.53)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.53: Neither through studying the Vedas, nor through penances, nor by charity, nor by worship can one see Me as you have seen Me.
shlokaList.name: Neither through studying the Vedas, nor through penances, nor by charity, nor by worship can one see Me as you have seen Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nāham vedair na tapasā na dānena na chejyayā
śhakya evam-vidho draṣhṭum dṛiṣhṭavān asi mām yathā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.53 (11.52 to 11.53)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.54: O Parantapa, only through unalloyed devotion it is possible to truly know Me, to see Me as I am standing before you, and to enter into My divine state.
shlokaList.name: O Parantapa, only through unalloyed devotion it is possible to truly know Me, to see Me as I am standing before you, and to enter into My divine state.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bhaktyā tv ananyayā śhakya aham evam-vidho ’rjuna
jñātum draṣhṭum cha tattvena praveṣhṭum cha parantapa
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.54

shlokaList.description: Shloka 11.55: O Arjuna! One who works for Me, considers Meas the supreme, is devoted to Me, is free from evil company and harbors no enmity toward any living being, certainly attains Me.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna! One who works for Me, considers Meas the supreme, is devoted to Me, is free from evil company and harbors no enmity toward any living being, certainly attains Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mat-karma-kṛin mat-paramo mad-bhaktaḥ saṅga-varjitaḥ
nirvairaḥ sarva-bhūteṣhu yaḥ sa mām eti pāṇḍava
shlokaList.title: Shloka 11.55

chapterList.description: Chapter 12: Bhakti Yoga - The Yoga of Devotion
chapterList.name: Chapter 12: Bhakti Yoga - The Yoga of Devotion

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.1: Arjuna said - Among the devotees who worship You with steadfast devotion and those who worship the imperishable, unmanifested aspect of You (the Brahman), which of these two types of devotees are considered superior?
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - Among the devotees who worship You with steadfast devotion and those who worship the imperishable, unmanifested aspect of You (the Brahman), which of these two types of devotees are considered superior?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
evam satata-yuktā ye bhaktās tvām paryupāsate
ye chāpy akṣharam avyaktam teṣhām ke yoga-vittamāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.2: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - According to Me, the devotees who always worship Me with divine faith and fix their minds on Me, are the highest and the most esteemed.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - According to Me, the devotees who always worship Me with divine faith and fix their minds on Me, are the highest and the most esteemed.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
mayy āveśhya mano ye mām nitya-yuktā upāsate
śhraddhayā parayopetās te me yuktatamā matāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.3: But those who worship the imperishable, the indefinable, the unmanifested, the omnipresent, the inconceivable, the unchangeable, the immovable and fixed;
shlokaList.name: But those who worship the imperishable, the indefinable, the unmanifested, the omnipresent, the inconceivable, the unchangeable, the immovable and fixed;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye tv akṣharam anirdeśhyam avyaktam paryupāsate
sarvatra-gam achintyañcha kūṭa-stham achalandhruvam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.3 (12.3 to 12.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.4: By restraining all their senses, being always even-minded, by doing good for the welfare of all beings; they also attain Me ultimately.
shlokaList.name: By restraining all their senses, being always even-minded, by doing good for the welfare of all beings; they also attain Me ultimately.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sanniyamyendriya-grāmam sarvatra sama-buddhayaḥ
te prāpnuvanti mām eva sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.4 (12.3 to 12.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.5: Devotees whose minds are attached to the unmanifest face greater troubles on the path of realization, as attaining the unmanifest is exceedingly difficult for embodied beings.
shlokaList.name: Devotees whose minds are attached to the unmanifest face greater troubles on the path of realization, as attaining the unmanifest is exceedingly difficult for embodied beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kleśho ’dhikataras teṣhām avyaktāsakta-chetasām
avyaktā hi gatir duḥkham dehavadbhir avāpyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.5

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.6: But those who, surrendering all their actions to Me, being completely attached to Me, constantly meditate on Me and worship Me with single-hearted devotion;
shlokaList.name: But those who, surrendering all their actions to Me, being completely attached to Me, constantly meditate on Me and worship Me with single-hearted devotion;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye tu sarvāṇi karmāṇi mayi sannyasya mat-parāḥ
ananyenaiva yogena mām dhyāyanta upāsate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.6 (12.6 to 12.7)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.7: O Partha, I very quickly rescue those who have fixed their minds upon Me, from this ocean of birth and death.
shlokaList.name: O Partha, I very quickly rescue those who have fixed their minds upon Me, from this ocean of birth and death.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
teṣhām aham samuddhartā mṛityu-samsāra-sāgarāt
bhavāmi na chirāt pārtha mayy āveśhita-chetasām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.7 (12.6 to 12.7)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.8: Fix your mind completely on Me and immerse your intellect in Me. By doing so, you will undoubtedly reside solely in Me hereafter.
shlokaList.name: Fix your mind completely on Me and immerse your intellect in Me. By doing so, you will undoubtedly reside solely in Me hereafter.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mayy eva mana ādhatsva mayi buddhim niveśhaya
nivasiṣhyasi mayy eva ata ūrdhvam na sanśhayaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.9: O Dhananjaya! If you find it challenging to keep your mind steadily focused on Me, then sincerely practise the path of Bhakti Yoga and in this way develop a desire to attain Me.
shlokaList.name: O Dhananjaya! If you find it challenging to keep your mind steadily focused on Me, then sincerely practise the path of Bhakti Yoga and in this way develop a desire to attain Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
atha chittam samādhātum na śhaknoṣhi mayi sthiram
abhyāsa-yogena tato mām ichchhāptum dhanañjaya
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.10: If you are unable to practise the principles of Bhakti Yoga, then just try to dedicate yourself to My work, because by performing actions for My sake, you will attain perfection.
shlokaList.name: If you are unable to practise the principles of Bhakti Yoga, then just try to dedicate yourself to My work, because by performing actions for My sake, you will attain perfection.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
abhyāse ’py asamartho ’si mat-karma-paramo bhava
mad-artham api karmāṇi kurvan siddhim avāpsyasi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.11: If you are unable to work for Me with devotion,then try to give up the fruits of your actions and in this way be self-situated.
shlokaList.name: If you are unable to work for Me with devotion,then try to give up the fruits of your actions and in this way be self-situated.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
athaitad apy aśhakto ’si kartum mad-yogam āśhritaḥ
sarva-karma-phala-tyāgam tataḥ kuru yatātmavān
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.11

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.12: Better than mere practice is cultivating knowledge, better than knowledge is meditation and greater than meditation is renouncing the fruits of one’s actions. Such renunciation leads to peace.
shlokaList.name: Better than mere practice is cultivating knowledge, better than knowledge is meditation and greater than meditation is renouncing the fruits of one’s actions. Such renunciation leads to peace.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhreyo hi jñānam abhyāsāj jñānād dhyānam viśhiṣhyate
dhyānāt karma-phala-tyāgas tyāgāch chhāntir anantaram
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.12

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.13: One who harbours no hatred towards any being, is friendly and compassionate to all, devoid of possessive mentality and ego, treats happiness and distress equally and is forgiving;
shlokaList.name: One who harbours no hatred towards any being, is friendly and compassionate to all, devoid of possessive mentality and ego, treats happiness and distress equally and is forgiving;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adveṣhṭā sarva-bhūtānām maitraḥ karuṇa eva cha
nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣhamī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.13 (12.13 to 12.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.14: Always content, self-disciplined, resolute in purpose and having surrendered their mind and intellect to Me, such a devotee of Mine is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.name: Always content, self-disciplined, resolute in purpose and having surrendered their mind and intellect to Me, such a devotee of Mine is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
santuṣhṭaḥ satatam yogī yatātmā dṛiḍha-niśhchayaḥ
mayy arpita-mano-buddhir yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.14 (12.13 to 12.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.15: He who is unperturbed by others and does not provoke others and who remains free from joy, envy, fear and anxiety– such a person is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.name: He who is unperturbed by others and does not provoke others and who remains free from joy, envy, fear and anxiety– such a person is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate cha yaḥ
harṣhāmarṣha-bhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa cha me priyaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.16: One who is free of any expectations, pure, diligent, impartial, free from anxiety and has relinquished attachment to the outcomes of actions - such a devotee is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.name: One who is free of any expectations, pure, diligent, impartial, free from anxiety and has relinquished attachment to the outcomes of actions - such a devotee is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
anapekṣhaḥ śhuchir dakṣha udāsīno gata-vyathaḥ
sarvārambha-parityāgī yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.16

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.17: One who remains unaffected by joy, hatred, grief, or desire and who has transcended notions of good and evil - such a devotee person is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.name: One who remains unaffected by joy, hatred, grief, or desire and who has transcended notions of good and evil - such a devotee person is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yo na hṛiṣhyati na dveṣhṭi na śhochati na kāṅkṣhati
śhubhāśhubha-parityāgī bhaktimān yaḥ sa me priyaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.17

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.18: One who treats friend and foe equally, remains equal in honour and dishonour, heat and cold, pain and pleasure, and who is free from all bad association;
shlokaList.name: One who treats friend and foe equally, remains equal in honour and dishonour, heat and cold, pain and pleasure, and who is free from all bad association;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
samaḥ śhatrau cha mitre cha tathā mānāpamānayoḥ
śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu samaḥ saṅga-vivarjitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.18 (12.18 to 12.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.19: And one who receives praise and blame with equanimity, speaks with restraint, remains content with whatever comes their way, is detached from the pleasures of home and maintains a disciplined mind - such a devotee is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.name: And one who receives praise and blame with equanimity, speaks with restraint, remains content with whatever comes their way, is detached from the pleasures of home and maintains a disciplined mind - such a devotee is very dear to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tulya-nindā-stutir maunī santuṣhṭo yena kenachit
aniketaḥ sthira-matir bhaktimān me priyo naraḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.19 (12.18 to 12.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 12.20: Those who with deep faith and complete dedication, practise this eternal dharma as instructed above and make Me the Supreme goal of life - such devotees are exceedingly dear to Me.
shlokaList.name: Those who with deep faith and complete dedication, practise this eternal dharma as instructed above and make Me the Supreme goal of life - such devotees are exceedingly dear to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ye tu dharmyāmṛitam idam yathoktam paryupāsate
śhraddadhānā mat-paramā bhaktās te ’tīva me priyāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 12.20

chapterList.description: Chapter 13: Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Field and the Knower of the Field
chapterList.name: Chapter 13: Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Field and the Knower of the Field

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.1: Arjuna said - O Keshava, I desire to comprehend the concepts of prakritiand purusha and to understand the meanings of kshetra and kshetrajna. Additionally, I seek to gain insight into what constitutes true knowledge and what is the ultimate purpose of acquiring this knowledge.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Keshava, I desire to comprehend the concepts of prakritiand purusha and to understand the meanings of kshetra and kshetrajna. Additionally, I seek to gain insight into what constitutes true knowledge and what is the ultimate purpose of acquiring this knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
prakṛitim puruṣham chaiva kṣhetram kṣhetra-jñam eva cha
etad veditum ichchhāmi jñānam jñeyam cha keśhava
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.2: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - This body, O son of Kunti, is called the field of activities; and the one who knows this body is called the knower of the field or kshetrajna by the sages who discern the truth about the two.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - This body, O son of Kunti, is called the field of activities; and the one who knows this body is called the knower of the field or kshetrajna by the sages who discern the truth about the two.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
idam śharīram kaunteya kṣhetram ity abhidhīyate
etad yo vetti tam prāhuḥ kṣhetra-jña iti tad-vidaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.2 (13.2 to 13.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.3: O Bharata, know Me to be the knower of the field (kshetrajna) in all bodies (kshetras). In My view, to know the field and its knower(s) is true knowledge.
shlokaList.name: O Bharata, know Me to be the knower of the field (kshetrajna) in all bodies (kshetras). In My view, to know the field and its knower(s) is true knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kṣhetra-jñam chāpi mām viddhi sarva-kṣhetreṣhu bhārata
kṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor jñānam yat taj jñānam matam mama
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.3 (13.2 to 13.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.4: Listen to Me and I’ll explain about what the field (kshetra) is, what its characteristics are, what its modifications are, what effects arise from various causes and also learn about who the knower of the field is and what His powers are.
shlokaList.name: Listen to Me and I’ll explain about what the field (kshetra) is, what its characteristics are, what its modifications are, what effects arise from various causes and also learn about who the knower of the field is and what His powers are.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tat kṣhetram yach cha yādṛik cha yad-vikāri yataśh cha yat
sa cha yo yat-prabhāvaśh cha tat samāsena me śhṛiṇu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.4 (13.2 to 13.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.5: This knowledge of the field and its knower has been expounded by sages in various hymns of the Vedas expressed in different ways. It is especially elucidated in the Brahma-sutras with sound logic and evidence.
shlokaList.name: This knowledge of the field and its knower has been expounded by sages in various hymns of the Vedas expressed in different ways. It is especially elucidated in the Brahma-sutras with sound logic and evidence.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ṛiṣhibhir bahudhā gītam chhandobhir vividhaiḥ pṛithak
brahma-sūtra-padaiśh chaiva hetumadbhir viniśhchitaiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.5 (13.2 to 13.5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.6: The field of activities comprises the five great elements, ego, intellect, unmanifest primordial matter, the eleven senses (five knowledge acquiring senses, five working senses and mind) and the five objects of the senses.
shlokaList.name: The field of activities comprises the five great elements, ego, intellect, unmanifest primordial matter, the eleven senses (five knowledge acquiring senses, five working senses and mind) and the five objects of the senses.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mahā-bhūtāny ahankāro buddhir avyaktam eva cha
indriyāṇi daśhaikam cha pañcha chendriya-gocharāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.6 (13.6 to 13.7)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.7: Desire, hatred, happiness, sorrow, the aggregate of body and senses, consciousness and determination–these aspects of the field along with its modifications, have been briefly described in this manner.
shlokaList.name: Desire, hatred, happiness, sorrow, the aggregate of body and senses, consciousness and determination–these aspects of the field along with its modifications, have been briefly described in this manner.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ichchhā dveṣhaḥ sukham duḥkham saṅghātaśh chetanā dhṛitiḥ
etat kṣhetram samāsena sa-vikāram udāhṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.7 (13.6 to 13.7)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.8: Humility, absence of arrogance, non-violence, tolerance, integrity, respectful service to the teacher, purity of body and mind, steadfastness and self-control;
shlokaList.name: Humility, absence of arrogance, non-violence, tolerance, integrity, respectful service to the teacher, purity of body and mind, steadfastness and self-control;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavam
āchāryopāsanam śhaucham sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.9: Detachment from sense-objects, absence of egoism, awareness of the distress inherent in birth, death, old age and sickness;
shlokaList.name: Detachment from sense-objects, absence of egoism, awareness of the distress inherent in birth, death, old age and sickness;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
indriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva cha
janma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.10: Non-attachment, non-identification of the self with son, wife, home and other possessions and maintaining equanimity toward the desirable and undesirable;
shlokaList.name: Non-attachment, non-identification of the self with son, wife, home and other possessions and maintaining equanimity toward the desirable and undesirable;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
asaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhu
nityam cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.11: Unwavering devotion to Me with single-minded focus, inclination towards solitude and disinterest in the general mass of people;
shlokaList.name: Unwavering devotion to Me with single-minded focus, inclination towards solitude and disinterest in the general mass of people;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇī
vivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.11 (13.11 to 13.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.12: Continuous absorption in the knowledge of the Self, culminating in the realization of ultimate truth is described as Knowledge, while anything not aligned with this is ignorance.
shlokaList.name: Continuous absorption in the knowledge of the Self, culminating in the realization of ultimate truth is described as Knowledge, while anything not aligned with this is ignorance.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adhyātma-jñāna-nityatvam tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanam
etaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānam yad ato ’nyathā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.12 (13.11 to 13.12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.13: I shall reveal to you what should be known, by knowing which one attains immortality.It is the beginningless Brahman, which is subordinate to Me and which transcends both existence and non-existence.
shlokaList.name: I shall reveal to you what should be known, by knowing which one attains immortality.It is the beginningless Brahman, which is subordinate to Me and which transcends both existence and non-existence.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jñeyam yat tat pravakṣhyāmi yaj jñātvāmṛitam aśhnute
anādi mat-param brahma na sat tan nāsad uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.13

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.14: With hands and feet everywhere, with eyes, heads and mouths everywhere, with ears everywhere in the universe–He exists everywhere, pervading everything.
shlokaList.name: With hands and feet everywhere, with eyes, heads and mouths everywhere, with ears everywhere in the universe–He exists everywhere, pervading everything.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarvataḥ pāṇi-pādam tat sarvato ’kṣhi-śhiro-mukham
sarvataḥ śhrutimal loke sarvam āvṛitya tiṣhṭhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.14 (13.14 to 13.15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.15: Illuminating the functions of all the senses, yet without the senses; unattached, yet sustaining all; devoid of gunas, yet the one to experience them all.
shlokaList.name: Illuminating the functions of all the senses, yet without the senses; unattached, yet sustaining all; devoid of gunas, yet the one to experience them all.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarvendriya-guṇābhāsam sarvendriya-vivarjitam
asaktam sarva-bhṛich chaiva nirguṇam guṇa-bhoktṛi cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.15 (13.14 to 13.15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.16: Existing both outside and inside all beings, of both unmoving and moving beings; is incomprehensible due to its subtlety and is far away and also near simultaneously.
shlokaList.name: Existing both outside and inside all beings, of both unmoving and moving beings; is incomprehensible due to its subtlety and is far away and also near simultaneously.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
bahir antaśh cha bhūtānām acharam charam eva cha
sūkṣhmatvāt tad avijñeyam dūra-stham chāntike cha tat
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.16 (13.16 to 13.17)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.17: He is undivided, though appearing divided in beings. He is to be understood as the sustainer, destroyer and creator of all living beings.
shlokaList.name: He is undivided, though appearing divided in beings. He is to be understood as the sustainer, destroyer and creator of all living beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
avibhaktam cha bhūteṣhu vibhaktam iva cha sthitam
bhūta-bhartṛi cha taj jñeyam grasiṣhṇu prabhaviṣhṇu cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.17 (13.16 to 13.17)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.18: He is the source of light in all luminaries. He is beyond darkness. He is the knowledge, the object of knowledge and the goal of knowledge. He dwells in everyone’s hearts.
shlokaList.name: He is the source of light in all luminaries. He is beyond darkness. He is the knowledge, the object of knowledge and the goal of knowledge. He dwells in everyone’s hearts.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jyotiṣhām api taj jyotis tamasaḥ param uchyate
jñānam jñeyam jñāna-gamyam hṛidi sarvasya viṣhṭhitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.18 (13.18 to 13.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.19: Thus, the field (kshetra), knowledge and the object of knowledge have been briefly explained. Upon knowing this, My devotee attains My state.
shlokaList.name: Thus, the field (kshetra), knowledge and the object of knowledge have been briefly explained. Upon knowing this, My devotee attains My state.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
iti kṣhetram tathā jñānam jñeyam choktam samāsataḥ
mad-bhakta etad vijñāya mad-bhāvāyopapadyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.19 (13.18 to 13.19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.20: Understand that both Prakriti (material nature) and Purusha (soul) are beginningless; and know that all of their modifications and qualities of matter (gunas) are born of Prakriti.
shlokaList.name: Understand that both Prakriti (material nature) and Purusha (soul) are beginningless; and know that all of their modifications and qualities of matter (gunas) are born of Prakriti.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prakṛitim puruṣham chaiva viddhy anādī ubhāv api
vikārānśh cha guṇānśh chaiva viddhi prakṛiti-sambhavān
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.20

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.21: In this material creation, Prakriti is said to be the cause for material cause and effect. And regarding the experience of pleasure and pain in this creation, Purusha (soul) is said to be the cause.
shlokaList.name: In this material creation, Prakriti is said to be the cause for material cause and effect. And regarding the experience of pleasure and pain in this creation, Purusha (soul) is said to be the cause.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kārya-kāraṇa-kartṛitve hetuḥ prakṛitir uchyate
puruṣhaḥ sukha-duḥkhānām bhoktṛitve hetur uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.21

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.22: The Purusha situated within Prakriti, experiences the material qualities (gunas) arising from Prakriti. Attachment to these material qualities or gunas is the cause of birth in superior or inferior wombs.
shlokaList.name: The Purusha situated within Prakriti, experiences the material qualities (gunas) arising from Prakriti. Attachment to these material qualities or gunas is the cause of birth in superior or inferior wombs.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
puruṣhaḥ prakṛiti-stho hi bhuṅkte prakṛiti-jān guṇān
kāraṇam guṇa-saṅgo ’sya sad-asad-yoni-janmasu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.22

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.23: Also in this body there is another Purusha called Paramatma, the Supreme Soul. He is the overseer and permission giver, He is the supreme maintainer, enjoyer and controller and is transcendental.
shlokaList.name: Also in this body there is another Purusha called Paramatma, the Supreme Soul. He is the overseer and permission giver, He is the supreme maintainer, enjoyer and controller and is transcendental.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
upadraṣhṭānumantā cha bhartā bhoktā maheśhvaraḥ
paramātmeti chāpy ukto dehe ’smin puruṣhaḥ paraḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.23

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.24: Whoever knows Purusha and Prakriti along with the material qualities (gunas) in this way, regardless of their state in life is never born again.
shlokaList.name: Whoever knows Purusha and Prakriti along with the material qualities (gunas) in this way, regardless of their state in life is never born again.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ya evam vetti puruṣham prakṛitim cha guṇaiḥ saha
sarvathā vartamāno ’pi na sa bhūyo ’bhijāyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.24

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.25: Some realize the Supreme Soul within themselves through meditation, others through developing knowledge (Sankhya Yoga) and still others through the path of action (Karma Yoga).
shlokaList.name: Some realize the Supreme Soul within themselves through meditation, others through developing knowledge (Sankhya Yoga) and still others through the path of action (Karma Yoga).
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dhyānenātmani paśhyanti kechid ātmānam ātmanā
anye sānkhyena yogena karma-yogena chāpare
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.25 (13.25 to 13.26)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.26: There are others who may not be developed in spiritual knowledge. But they can also begin to worship the Supreme by hearing from others. And because of their inclination to hear they also transcend death.
shlokaList.name: There are others who may not be developed in spiritual knowledge. But they can also begin to worship the Supreme by hearing from others. And because of their inclination to hear they also transcend death.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
anye tv evam ajānantaḥ śhrutvānyebhya upāsate
te ’pi chātitaranty eva mṛityum śhruti-parāyaṇāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.26 (13.25 to 13.26)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.27: O best of the Bharatas, know that whatever being is born, whether moving or non-moving, arises from the union of the field (kshetra) and the knower of the field (kshetrajna).
shlokaList.name: O best of the Bharatas, know that whatever being is born, whether moving or non-moving, arises from the union of the field (kshetra) and the knower of the field (kshetrajna).
shlokaList.sanskritText: yāvat sañjāyate kiñchit sattvam sthāvara-jaṅgamam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.27 (13.27 to 13.29)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.28: They alone truly see, who perceive the Paramatma (Supreme Soul) accompanying the atma (individual soul) in all beings and understand both to be imperishable within this perishable body.
shlokaList.name: They alone truly see, who perceive the Paramatma (Supreme Soul) accompanying the atma (individual soul) in all beings and understand both to be imperishable within this perishable body.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
samam sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu tiṣhṭhantam parameśhvaram
vinaśhyatsv avinaśhyantam yaḥ paśhyati sa paśhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.28 (13.27 to 13.29)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.29: Those who see the Lord as the Supreme Soul, equally present everywhere in all living beings, they do not degrade themselves by their mind and thereby attain the supreme state.
shlokaList.name: Those who see the Lord as the Supreme Soul, equally present everywhere in all living beings, they do not degrade themselves by their mind and thereby attain the supreme state.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
samam paśhyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśhvaram
na hinasty ātmanātmānam tato yāti parām gatim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.29 (13.27 to 13.29)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.30: Anyone who understands that all actions are performed by Prakriti alone, while the Self does nothing, actually sees everything as it is.
shlokaList.name: Anyone who understands that all actions are performed by Prakriti alone, while the Self does nothing, actually sees everything as it is.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
prakṛityaiva cha karmāṇi kriyamāṇāni sarvaśhaḥ
yaḥ paśhyati tathātmānam akartāram sa paśhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.30

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.31: When one sees the separate existence of all beings as situated in the One Supreme Brahman and their manifestation arising from that One Supreme Brahman alone, then one attains Brahman.
shlokaList.name: When one sees the separate existence of all beings as situated in the One Supreme Brahman and their manifestation arising from that One Supreme Brahman alone, then one attains Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yadā bhūta-pṛithag-bhāvam eka-stham anupaśhyati
tata eva cha vistāram brahma sampadyate tadā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.31 (13.31 to 13.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.32: O son of Kunti, the Supreme Self is without beginning, beyond the gunas and eternal. Although existing within the body, it neither acts nor gets entangled.
shlokaList.name: O son of Kunti, the Supreme Self is without beginning, beyond the gunas and eternal. Although existing within the body, it neither acts nor gets entangled.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
anāditvān nirguṇatvāt paramātmāyam avyayaḥ
śharīra-stho ’pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.32 (13.31 to 13.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.33: Like the all-pervading space (akasha), which due to its subtlety is unattached to anything, similarly the Self though existing everywhere, does not become involved with the qualities of the body.
shlokaList.name: Like the all-pervading space (akasha), which due to its subtlety is unattached to anything, similarly the Self though existing everywhere, does not become involved with the qualities of the body.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yathā sarva-gatam saukṣhmyād ākāśham nopalipyate
sarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.33 (13.31 to 13.33)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.34: O scion of Bharata, just as the single sun illuminates the entire world, similarly He (soul) who dwells within the kshetra (the field of activities) illuminates the entire kshetra or the body.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata, just as the single sun illuminates the entire world, similarly He (soul) who dwells within the kshetra (the field of activities) illuminates the entire kshetra or the body.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yathā prakāśhayaty ekaḥ kṛitsnam lokam imam raviḥ
kṣhetram kṣhetrī tathā kṛitsnam prakāśhayati bhārata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.34

shlokaList.description: Shloka 13.35: Thus, those who perceive with the eye of knowledge the distinction between the kshetra (the field) and the kshetrajna (the knower of the field) and understand the liberation of living beings from Prakriti, attain the Supreme.
shlokaList.name: Thus, those who perceive with the eye of knowledge the distinction between the kshetra (the field) and the kshetrajna (the knower of the field) and understand the liberation of living beings from Prakriti, attain the Supreme.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor evam antaram jñāna-chakṣhuṣhā
bhūta-prakṛiti-mokṣham cha ye vidur yānti te param
shlokaList.title: Shloka 13.35

chapterList.description: Chapter 14: Guna Traya Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Division of the Three Gunas
chapterList.name: Chapter 14: Guna Traya Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Division of the Three Gunas

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.1: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Once again, I will impart this supreme knowledge to you, the most profound of all wisdom. Through understanding this knowledge all the sages have attained the ultimate perfection of life.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Once again, I will impart this supreme knowledge to you, the most profound of all wisdom. Through understanding this knowledge all the sages have attained the ultimate perfection of life.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
param bhūyaḥ pravakṣhyāmi jñānānām jñānam uttamam
yaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parām siddhim ito gatāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.2: By taking shelter of this knowledge, one attains a state similar to Mine. Having attained that, such individuals do not undergo rebirth at the time of creation nor suffer during dissolution.
shlokaList.name: By taking shelter of this knowledge, one attains a state similar to Mine. Having attained that, such individuals do not undergo rebirth at the time of creation nor suffer during dissolution.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
idam jñānam upāśhritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ
sarge ’pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.3: The great Brahman (Mula-Prakriti) is the womb and within it I place the seed. From that arises the birth of all beings, O scion of Bharata.
shlokaList.name: The great Brahman (Mula-Prakriti) is the womb and within it I place the seed. From that arises the birth of all beings, O scion of Bharata.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbham dadhāmy aham
sambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānām tato bhavati bhārata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.3 (14.3 to 14.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.4: O son of Kunti! For all types of creatures born in any species, the great Prakriti is their mother and I am the father who imparts the seed.
shlokaList.name: O son of Kunti! For all types of creatures born in any species, the great Prakriti is their mother and I am the father who imparts the seed.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-yoniṣhu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ
tāsām brahma mahad yonir aham bīja-pradaḥ pitā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.4 (14.3 to 14.4)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.5: O mighty-armed Arjuna! Sattva, Rajas and Tamas, are gunas or qualities born of Prakriti. They bind the eternal living being to the body.
shlokaList.name: O mighty-armed Arjuna! Sattva, Rajas and Tamas, are gunas or qualities born of Prakriti. They bind the eternal living being to the body.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sattvam rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛiti-sambhavāḥ
nibadhnanti mahā-bāho dehe dehinam avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.5

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.6: Among these, Sattva, being the purest, is illuminating and free from impurities. O sinless one, it binds the living beings through attachment to happiness and knowledge.
shlokaList.name: Among these, Sattva, being the purest, is illuminating and free from impurities. O sinless one, it binds the living beings through attachment to happiness and knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tatra sattvam nirmalatvāt prakāśhakam anāmayam
sukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena chānagha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.7: O son of Kunti! Know Rajas as the nature of passion. It is born of desire and hankering. It tightly binds the embodied living being through attachment to actions and their outcomes.
shlokaList.name: O son of Kunti! Know Rajas as the nature of passion. It is born of desire and hankering. It tightly binds the embodied living being through attachment to actions and their outcomes.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rajo rāgātmakam viddhi tṛiṣhṇā-saṅga-samudbhavam
tan nibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.8: Conversely, understand Tamas as arising from ignorance, leading to delusion in all embodied living beings. O descendant of Bharata, Tamas binds one tightly through misunderstanding, laziness and sleep.
shlokaList.name: Conversely, understand Tamas as arising from ignorance, leading to delusion in all embodied living beings. O descendant of Bharata, Tamas binds one tightly through misunderstanding, laziness and sleep.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tamas tv ajñāna-jam viddhi mohanam sarva-dehinām
pramādālasya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.9: O scion of Bharata! Sattva binds one to happiness, Rajas to action, while Tamas, by veiling knowledge, binds one to misunderstanding.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata! Sattva binds one to happiness, Rajas to action, while Tamas, by veiling knowledge, binds one to misunderstanding.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sattvam sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata
jñānam āvṛitya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.10: Sometimes Sattva arises dominating over Rajas and Tamas, O scion of Bharata! And sometimes, Rajas arises overpowering Sattva and Tamas, and sometimes Tamas arises dominating over Sattva and Rajas.
shlokaList.name: Sometimes Sattva arises dominating over Rajas and Tamas, O scion of Bharata! And sometimes, Rajas arises overpowering Sattva and Tamas, and sometimes Tamas arises dominating over Sattva and Rajas.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rajas tamaśh chābhibhūya sattvam bhavati bhārata
rajaḥ sattvam tamaśh chaiva tamaḥ sattvam rajas tathā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.11: When all the gates of the body are illuminated by knowledge then it is said that Sattva is predominant there.
shlokaList.name: When all the gates of the body are illuminated by knowledge then it is said that Sattva is predominant there.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-dvāreṣhu dehe ’smin prakāśha upajāyate
jñānam yadā tadā vidyād vivṛiddham sattvam ity uta
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.11

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.12: O best among the descendants of Bharata! When greed, fruitive activity, intense endeavor, restlessness and strong desires arise, then it is to be understood that Rajas is predominant there.
shlokaList.name: O best among the descendants of Bharata! When greed, fruitive activity, intense endeavor, restlessness and strong desires arise, then it is to be understood that Rajas is predominant there.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
lobhaḥ pravṛittir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśhamaḥ spṛihā
rajasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe bharatarṣhabha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.12

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.13: O son of Kurus! When darkness, dullness, misunderstanding and illusion arise, then it is to be understood that Tamas is predominant there.
shlokaList.name: O son of Kurus! When darkness, dullness, misunderstanding and illusion arise, then it is to be understood that Tamas is predominant there.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aprakāśho ’pravṛittiśh cha pramādo moha eva cha
tamasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe kuru-nandana
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.13

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.14: When the quality of Sattva is predominant in a person at the time of death, they attain the pure realms attained by those who are greatly knowledgeable.
shlokaList.name: When the quality of Sattva is predominant in a person at the time of death, they attain the pure realms attained by those who are greatly knowledgeable.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yadā sattve pravṛiddhe tu pralayam yāti deha-bhṛit
tadottama-vidām lokān amalān pratipadyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.14 (14.14 to 14.15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.15: If one passes away with predominance of Rajas, then they are reborn among those attached to worldly actions. Similarly, if one passes away with Tamas predominating, they are born in ignorant species.
shlokaList.name: If one passes away with predominance of Rajas, then they are reborn among those attached to worldly actions. Similarly, if one passes away with Tamas predominating, they are born in ignorant species.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rajasi pralayam gatvā karma-saṅgiṣhu jāyate
tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣhu jāyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.15 (14.14 to 14.15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.16: The result of virtuous actions is said to be pure and hence Sattvic in nature. But the result of actions influenced by Rajas is sorrow and ignorance is the result of actions performed in Tamas.
shlokaList.name: The result of virtuous actions is said to be pure and hence Sattvic in nature. But the result of actions influenced by Rajas is sorrow and ignorance is the result of actions performed in Tamas.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
karmaṇaḥ sukṛitasyāhuḥ sāttvikam nirmalam phalam
rajasas tu phalam duḥkham ajñānam tamasaḥ phalam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.16

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.17: From Sattva arises wisdom, from Rajas arises greed and from Tamas arises miscomprehension, delusion and ignorance.
shlokaList.name: From Sattva arises wisdom, from Rajas arises greed and from Tamas arises miscomprehension, delusion and ignorance.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sattvāt sañjāyate jñānam rajaso lobha eva cha
pramāda-mohau tamaso bhavato ’jñānam eva cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.17

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.18: Those who are inclined towards the quality of Sattva progress upwards, those attached to Rajas remain in the middle and those filled with the negative qualities influenced by Tamas descend downwards.
shlokaList.name: Those who are inclined towards the quality of Sattva progress upwards, those attached to Rajas remain in the middle and those filled with the negative qualities influenced by Tamas descend downwards.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ūrdhvam gachchhanti sattva-sthā madhye tiṣhṭhanti rājasāḥ
jaghanya-guṇa-vṛitti-sthā adho gachchhanti tāmasāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.18

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.19: When the seer realizes that no agent exists behind all our actions except for the three gunas and understands the one Supreme who is superior to the gunas, they then attain My divine nature.
shlokaList.name: When the seer realizes that no agent exists behind all our actions except for the three gunas and understands the one Supreme who is superior to the gunas, they then attain My divine nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nānyam guṇebhyaḥ kartāram yadā draṣhṭānupaśhyati
guṇebhyaśh cha param vetti mad-bhāvam so ’dhigachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.19 (14.19 to 14.20)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.20: When the embodied one transcends these three gunas, from which the body arises, he becomes free from birth, death, old age and sorrow and attains immortality.
shlokaList.name: When the embodied one transcends these three gunas, from which the body arises, he becomes free from birth, death, old age and sorrow and attains immortality.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
guṇān etān atītya trīn dehī deha-samudbhavān
janma-mṛityu-jarā-duḥkhair vimukto ’mṛitam aśhnute
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.20 (14.19 to 14.20)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.21: Arjuna said - O Lord, how can one recognise when a person has transcended these three gunas? How does he behave and how does one go beyond these gunas?
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Lord, how can one recognise when a person has transcended these three gunas? How does he behave and how does one go beyond these gunas?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
kair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabho
kim āchāraḥ katham chaitāns trīn guṇān ativartate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.21

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.22: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O son of Pandu, one who has transcended the three gunas neither resents the manifestation of light (resulting from Sattva), nor the activities driven by Rajas, nor the delusion brought about by Tamas, nor does he hankers for them when they are absent.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O son of Pandu, one who has transcended the three gunas neither resents the manifestation of light (resulting from Sattva), nor the activities driven by Rajas, nor the delusion brought about by Tamas, nor does he hankers for them when they are absent.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
hrī-bhagavān uvācha
prakāśham cha pravṛittim cha moham eva cha pāṇḍava
na dveṣhṭi sampravṛittāni na nivṛittāni kāṅkṣhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.22 (14.22 to 14.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.23: Seated with equanimity, undistracted by the gunas, one who understands that the gunas alone are responsible for all actions, remains undisturbed.
shlokaList.name: Seated with equanimity, undistracted by the gunas, one who understands that the gunas alone are responsible for all actions, remains undisturbed.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
udāsīna-vad āsīno guṇair yo na vichālyate
guṇā vartanta ity evam yo ’vatiṣhṭhati neṅgate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.23 (14.22 to 14.23)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.24: One who sees sorrow and happiness as equal, who is firmly established in his own self, for whom a lump of earth, a stone and gold hold equal value, who perceives the pleasant and unpleasant equally, who is patient and regards criticism and praise alike;
shlokaList.name: One who sees sorrow and happiness as equal, who is firmly established in his own self, for whom a lump of earth, a stone and gold hold equal value, who perceives the pleasant and unpleasant equally, who is patient and regards criticism and praise alike;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥ
tulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-sanstutiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.24 (14.24 to 14.25)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.25: The one who remains equanimous in honour and disgrace, who treats friend and enemy alike and who has renounced all fruitive actions is said to have transcended the three gunas.
shlokaList.name: The one who remains equanimous in honour and disgrace, who treats friend and enemy alike and who has renounced all fruitive actions is said to have transcended the three gunas.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāri-pakṣhayoḥ
sarvārambha-parityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.25 (14.24 to 14.25)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.26: One who serves Me with unwavering devotion, transcends the gunas and becomes fit to attain the state of Brahman.
shlokaList.name: One who serves Me with unwavering devotion, transcends the gunas and becomes fit to attain the state of Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mām cha yo ’vyabhichāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevate
sa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.26

shlokaList.description: Shloka 14.27: For I am the foundation of the formless Brahman, which is immortal, imperishable, eternal and the fundamental state of absolute bliss.
shlokaList.name: For I am the foundation of the formless Brahman, which is immortal, imperishable, eternal and the fundamental state of absolute bliss.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
brahmaṇo hi pratiṣhṭhāham amṛitasyāvyayasya cha
śhāśhvatasya cha dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 14.27

chapterList.description: Chapter 15: Purushottama Yoga - The Yoga of the Supreme Person
chapterList.name: Chapter 15: Purushottama Yoga - The Yoga of the Supreme Person

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.1: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - It is said that there is an eternal Ashwattha tree with roots upward and branches downward, where the hymns of the Vedas are its leaves. One who knows this tree is truly a knower of the Vedas.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - It is said that there is an eternal Ashwattha tree with roots upward and branches downward, where the hymns of the Vedas are its leaves. One who knows this tree is truly a knower of the Vedas.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
ūrdhva-mūlam adhaḥ-śhākham aśhvattham prāhur avyayam
chhandānsi yasya parṇāni yas tam veda sa veda-vit
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.2: Its branches, extending below and above, are nourished by the three gunas, while the sense-objects are like blossoms on this tree. Its roots alsoextend downwards and are bound to actions (karma) in the human world.
shlokaList.name: Its branches, extending below and above, are nourished by the three gunas, while the sense-objects are like blossoms on this tree. Its roots alsoextend downwards and are bound to actions (karma) in the human world.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adhaśh chordhvam prasṛitās tasya śhākhā
guṇa-pravṛiddhā viṣhaya-pravālāḥ
adhaśh cha mūlāny anusantatāni
karmānubandhīni manuṣhya-loke
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.3: Its true form cannot be perceived here in this realm, nor can its end, origin, or support be understood. However, this strongly deep-rooted tree must be cut down with determination, using the weapon of detachment.
shlokaList.name: Its true form cannot be perceived here in this realm, nor can its end, origin, or support be understood. However, this strongly deep-rooted tree must be cut down with determination, using the weapon of detachment.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na rūpam asyeha tathopalabhyate
nānto na chādir na cha sampratiṣhṭhā
aśhvattham enam su-virūḍha-mūlam
asaṅga-śhastreṇa dṛiḍhena chhittvā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.3

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.4: Then one should seek out that abode, having attained which one never returns to the cycle of birth and death and there take refuge of the Supreme Being from whom the cosmic activities began and extended since remote time.
shlokaList.name: Then one should seek out that abode, having attained which one never returns to the cycle of birth and death and there take refuge of the Supreme Being from whom the cosmic activities began and extended since remote time.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tataḥ padam tat parimārgitavyam
yasmin gatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥ
tam eva chādyam puruṣham prapadye
yataḥ pravṛittiḥ prasṛitā purāṇī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.4

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.5: One who is free from pride and delusion, having conquered over faulty association, devoted to the spiritual path, has given up all selfish desires, liberated from dualities like pleasure and pain, who are not bewildered, they attain that Eternal State.
