shlokaList.name: One who is free from pride and delusion, having conquered over faulty association, devoted to the spiritual path, has given up all selfish desires, liberated from dualities like pleasure and pain, who are not bewildered, they attain that Eternal State.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
nirmāna-mohā jita-saṅga-doṣhā
adhyātma-nityā vinivṛitta-kāmāḥ
dvandvair vimuktāḥ sukha-duḥkha-sanjñair
gachchhanty amūḍhāḥ padam avyayam tat
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.5

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.6: Neither the sun, nor the moon, nor fire can illumine that supreme abode of Mine; attaining which one does not return to this world.
shlokaList.name: Neither the sun, nor the moon, nor fire can illumine that supreme abode of Mine; attaining which one does not return to this world.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na tad bhāsayate sūryo na śhaśhāṅko na pāvakaḥ
yad gatvā na nivartante tad dhāma paramam mama
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.7: In this world, all the living beings are My eternal fragmental parts. Being situated in Prakriti (material nature), they are struggling with the six senses including the mind.
shlokaList.name: In this world, all the living beings are My eternal fragmental parts. Being situated in Prakriti (material nature), they are struggling with the six senses including the mind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mamaivānśho jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ
manaḥ-ṣhaṣhṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛiti-sthāni karṣhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.8: Just like air carries aromas from one place to another, similarly the soul carries the mind and its conceptions as it transmigrates from one body to another.
shlokaList.name: Just like air carries aromas from one place to another, similarly the soul carries the mind and its conceptions as it transmigrates from one body to another.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śharīram yad avāpnoti yach chāpy utkrāmatīśhvaraḥ
gṛihītvaitāni sanyāti vāyur gandhān ivāśhayāt
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.9: The living being thus experiences various sense-objects through the faculties of hearing, seeing, touch, taste and smell, which are centered around the mind.
shlokaList.name: The living being thus experiences various sense-objects through the faculties of hearing, seeing, touch, taste and smell, which are centered around the mind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrotram chakṣhuḥ sparśhanam cha rasanam ghrāṇam eva cha
adhiṣhṭhāya manaśh chāyam viṣhayān upasevate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.9

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.10: The deluded cannot perceive this movement of the living being fromone body to another. But those with the eye of wisdom can see the Self as it moves from one body to another, remains in one body, and how it enjoys under the influence of the three gunas.
shlokaList.name: The deluded cannot perceive this movement of the living being fromone body to another. But those with the eye of wisdom can see the Self as it moves from one body to another, remains in one body, and how it enjoys under the influence of the three gunas.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
utkrāmantam sthitam vāpi bhuñjānam vā guṇānvitam
vimūḍhā nānupaśhyanti paśhyanti jñāna-chakṣhuṣhaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.10

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.11: The striving yogis can see the soul dwelling within the body but those who are not spiritually evolved or are unintelligent, even though they strive to understand, they cannot perceive the soul.
shlokaList.name: The striving yogis can see the soul dwelling within the body but those who are not spiritually evolved or are unintelligent, even though they strive to understand, they cannot perceive the soul.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yatanto yoginaśh chainam paśhyanty ātmany avasthitam
yatanto ‘py akṛitātmāno nainam paśhyanty achetasaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.11

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.12: Know that the brilliance of the sun that illuminates the entire world; the light that radiates from the moon, and the brightness of fire, all come from Me.
shlokaList.name: Know that the brilliance of the sun that illuminates the entire world; the light that radiates from the moon, and the brightness of fire, all come from Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yad āditya-gatam tejo jagad bhāsayate ’khilam
yach chandramasi yach chāgnau tat tejo viddhi māmakam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.12 (15.12 to 15.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.13: Permeating the earth, I sustain all beings with My energy. As the essence of the moon, I nourish all plants with the nectar of life.
shlokaList.name: Permeating the earth, I sustain all beings with My energy. As the essence of the moon, I nourish all plants with the nectar of life.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
gām āviśhya cha bhūtāni dhārayāmy aham ojasā
puṣhṇāmi chauṣhadhīḥ sarvāḥ somo bhūtvā rasātmakaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.13 (15.12 to 15.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.14: Residing within the bodies of living beings as the fire of digestion (Vaisvanara), I digest four types of food in association with the incoming and outgoing breath (pranas).
shlokaList.name: Residing within the bodies of living beings as the fire of digestion (Vaisvanara), I digest four types of food in association with the incoming and outgoing breath (pranas).
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aham vaiśhvānaro bhūtvā prāṇinām deham āśhritaḥ
prāṇāpāna-samāyuktaḥ pachāmy annam chatur-vidham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.14 (15.12 to 15.14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.15: I am seated within the hearts of all living beings. Memory, knowledge, and forgetfulness originate from Me. I am the sole object of knowledge described in all the Vedas. I am indeed the originator of Vedanta and the knower of the Vedas as well.
shlokaList.name: I am seated within the hearts of all living beings. Memory, knowledge, and forgetfulness originate from Me. I am the sole object of knowledge described in all the Vedas. I am indeed the originator of Vedanta and the knower of the Vedas as well.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarvasya chāham hṛidi sanniviṣhṭo
mattaḥ smṛitir jñānam apohanam cha
vedaiśh cha sarvair aham eva vedyo
vedānta-kṛid veda-vid eva chāham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.16: There are two kinds of purushas (entities) – the perishable and the imperishable. All living beings in the material realm are perishable, while those situated in the spiritual realm are imperishable.
shlokaList.name: There are two kinds of purushas (entities) – the perishable and the imperishable. All living beings in the material realm are perishable, while those situated in the spiritual realm are imperishable.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dvāv imau puruṣhau loke kṣharaśh chākṣhara eva cha
kṣharaḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni kūṭa-stho ’kṣhara uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.16 (15.16 to 15.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.17: Besides the two there is another Supreme Purusha who is spoken of as the Paramatma (the Supreme Soul), the immutable Lord, who enters and sustains all the three worlds.
shlokaList.name: Besides the two there is another Supreme Purusha who is spoken of as the Paramatma (the Supreme Soul), the immutable Lord, who enters and sustains all the three worlds.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
uttamaḥ puruṣhas tv anyaḥ paramātmety udāhṛitaḥ
yo loka-trayam āviśhya bibharty avyaya īśhvaraḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.17 (15.16 to 15.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.18: Because I transcend both the perishable and the imperishable, I am celebrated in the world and in the Vedas as Purushottama, the Highest Purusha.
shlokaList.name: Because I transcend both the perishable and the imperishable, I am celebrated in the world and in the Vedas as Purushottama, the Highest Purusha.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yasmāt kṣharam atīto ’ham akṣharād api chottamaḥ
ato ’smi loke vede cha prathitaḥ puruṣhottamaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.18 (15.16 to 15.18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.19: O scion of Bharata, anyone who knows Me in this way, free from misconceptions, as the Supreme Person, actually knows everything, and he thus worships Me with complete devotion.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata, anyone who knows Me in this way, free from misconceptions, as the Supreme Person, actually knows everything, and he thus worships Me with complete devotion.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yo mām evam asammūḍho jānāti puruṣhottamam
sa sarva-vid bhajati mām sarva-bhāvena bhārata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.19

shlokaList.description: Shloka 15.20: Thus, I have revealed to you, O sinless Arjuna, this profound confidential wisdom from the Vedic scriptures. Understanding this, one attains enlightenment and attains perfection in his endeavors.
shlokaList.name: Thus, I have revealed to you, O sinless Arjuna, this profound confidential wisdom from the Vedic scriptures. Understanding this, one attains enlightenment and attains perfection in his endeavors.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
iti guhyatamam śhāstram idam uktam mayānagha
etad buddhvā buddhimān syāt kṛita-kṛityaśh cha bhārata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 15.20

chapterList.description: Chapter 16: Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Division between the Divine and the Demoniac
chapterList.name: Chapter 16: Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Division between the Divine and the Demoniac

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.1: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Fearlessness, purity of heart, established in spiritual knowledge, generosity, self-control, sacrifice, study of scriptures, austerity and straightforwardness;
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Fearlessness, purity of heart, established in spiritual knowledge, generosity, self-control, sacrifice, study of scriptures, austerity and straightforwardness;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
abhayam sattva-sanśhuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥ
dānam damaśh cha yajñaśh cha svādhyāyas tapa ārjavam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.2: Non-violence, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, inner tranquillity, absence of slander, compassion towards all beings, non covetousness, gentleness, modesty and firmness;
shlokaList.name: Non-violence, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, inner tranquillity, absence of slander, compassion towards all beings, non covetousness, gentleness, modesty and firmness;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ahinsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śhāntir apaiśhunam
dayā bhūteṣhv aloluptvam mārdavam hrīr achāpalam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.3: O scion of Bharata, vitality, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, absence of hatred and lack of excessive pride are the attributes of one born with divine qualities.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata, vitality, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, absence of hatred and lack of excessive pride are the attributes of one born with divine qualities.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ejaḥ kṣhamā dhṛitiḥ śhaucham adroho nāti-mānitā
bhavanti sampadam daivīm abhijātasya bhārata
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.3

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.4: O Partha, pride, arrogance, self-conceit, anger, harshness and ignorance are the attributes of one born with a demoniac nature.
shlokaList.name: O Partha, pride, arrogance, self-conceit, anger, harshness and ignorance are the attributes of one born with a demoniac nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dambho darpo ’bhimānaśh cha krodhaḥ pāruṣhyam eva cha
ajñānam chābhijātasya pārtha sampadam āsurīm
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.4

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.5: The divine nature leads to liberation, while the demoniac nature leads to bondage. Do not grieve, O son of Pandu, for you are born with divine attributes.
shlokaList.name: The divine nature leads to liberation, while the demoniac nature leads to bondage. Do not grieve, O son of Pandu, for you are born with divine attributes.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
daivī sampad vimokṣhāya nibandhāyāsurī matā
mā śhuchaḥ sampadam daivīm abhijāto ’si pāṇḍava
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.5 (5 to 6)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.6: There are two types of beings in this world: those with divine qualities and those with demoniac qualities. The divine qualities have been described in detail. Now, hear from Me, O Partha, about the demoniac qualities.
shlokaList.name: There are two types of beings in this world those with divine qualities and those with demoniac qualities. The divine qualities have been described in detail. Now, hear from Me, O Partha, about the demoniac qualities.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dvau bhūta-sargau loke ’smin daiva āsura eva cha
daivo vistaraśhaḥ prokta āsuram pārtha me śhṛiṇu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.6 (5 to 6)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.7: Those of demoniac nature do not know what to do and what to refrain from. They lack purity, good conduct and truthfulness.
shlokaList.name: Those of demoniac nature do not know what to do and what to refrain from. They lack purity, good conduct and truthfulness.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pravṛittim cha nivṛittim cha janā na vidur āsurāḥ
na śhaucham nāpi chāchāro na satyam teṣhu vidyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.7

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.8: They proclaim, the universe is devoid of truth, morality and divine order. It is generated by the union of beings, driven solely by passion and has no cause other than fulfilment of sensual desires.
shlokaList.name: They proclaim, the universe is devoid of truth, morality and divine order. It is generated by the union of beings, driven solely by passion and has no cause other than fulfilment of sensual desires.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
asatyam apratiṣhṭham te jagad āhur anīśhvaram
aparaspara-sambhūtam kim anyat kāma-haitukam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.8

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.9: With this outlook, these misguided souls of limited understanding, indulge in cruel deeds and emerge as enemies of the world bent on its destruction.
shlokaList.name: With this outlook, these misguided souls of limited understanding, indulge in cruel deeds and emerge as enemies of the world bent on its destruction.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
etām dṛiṣhṭim avaṣhṭabhya naṣhṭātmāno ’lpa-buddhayaḥ
prabhavanty ugra-karmāṇaḥ kṣhayāya jagato ’hitāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.9 (9 to 10)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.10: Driven by insatiable sensory desires, filled with pride, vanity and arrogance, deluded by the temporary things of this world, these individuals adhere to impure resolves.
shlokaList.name: Driven by insatiable sensory desires, filled with pride, vanity and arrogance, deluded by the temporary things of this world, these individuals adhere to impure resolves.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kāmam āśhritya duṣhpūram dambha-māna-madānvitāḥ
mohād gṛihītvāsad-grāhān pravartante ’śhuchi-vratāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.10 (9 to 10)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.11: Burdened by endless anxieties that persist until death, they view the pursuit of sensual pleasures as life’s sole purpose, firmly believing it to be the ultimate goal of life.
shlokaList.name: Burdened by endless anxieties that persist until death, they view the pursuit of sensual pleasures as life’s sole purpose, firmly believing it to be the ultimate goal of life.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
hintām aparimeyām cha pralayāntām upāśhritāḥ
kāmopabhoga-paramā etāvad iti niśhchitāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.11 (11 to 12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.12: Ensnared by numerous desires and absorbed in lust and anger, they seek to amass wealth through unjust means for indulging in sensual pleasures.
shlokaList.name: Ensnared by numerous desires and absorbed in lust and anger, they seek to amass wealth through unjust means for indulging in sensual pleasures.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
āśhā-pāśha-śhatair baddhāḥ kāma-krodha-parāyaṇāḥ
īhante kāma-bhogārtham anyāyenārtha-sañchayān
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.12 (11 to 12)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.13: I have gained this wealth today and I will get more as per my desires. I already have so much and I will acquire even more wealth in the future.
shlokaList.name: I have gained this wealth today and I will get more as per my desires. I already have so much and I will acquire even more wealth in the future.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
idam adya mayā labdham imam prāpsye manoratham
idam astīdam api me bhaviṣhyati punar dhanam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.13 (13 to 15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.14: I have defeated that enemy and I will defeat others too. I am the controller, I am the enjoyer, I am successful, powerful and happy.
shlokaList.name: I have defeated that enemy and I will defeat others too. I am the controller, I am the enjoyer, I am successful, powerful and happy.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
asau mayā hataḥ śhatrur haniṣhye chāparān api
īśhvaro ’ham aham bhogī siddho ’ham balavān sukhī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.14 (13 to 15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.15: I am wealthy and of noble birth. Who can be equal to me? I will perform sacrifices, give generously and enjoy life. Thus they are deluded by ignorance.
shlokaList.name: I am wealthy and of noble birth. Who can be equal to me? I will perform sacrifices, give generously and enjoy life. Thus they are deluded by ignorance.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
āḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛiśho mayā
yakṣhye dāsyāmi modiṣhya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.15 (13 to 15)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.16: Thus entangled by countless thoughts and covered by the nets of delusion, strongly addicted to satisfying their sensual desires, they descend into filthy hell.
shlokaList.name: Thus entangled by countless thoughts and covered by the nets of delusion, strongly addicted to satisfying their sensual desires, they descend into filthy hell.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aneka-chitta-vibhrāntā moha-jāla-samāvṛitāḥ
prasaktāḥ kāma-bhogeṣhu patanti narake ’śhuchau
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.16

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.17: Filled with self-conceit, haughtiness and intoxicated by wealth, they perform ostentatious sacrifices merely for show disregarding the true spirit of sacrifice.
shlokaList.name: Filled with self-conceit, haughtiness and intoxicated by wealth, they perform ostentatious sacrifices merely for show disregarding the true spirit of sacrifice.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ātma-sambhāvitāḥ stabdhā dhana-māna-madānvitāḥ
yajante nāma-yajñais te dambhenāvidhi-pūrvakam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.17 (17 to 18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.18: Possessed by egoism, power, insolence, lust and wrath, these individuals harbour hatred towards Me,who is situated within their own and in others’ bodies.
shlokaList.name: Possessed by egoism, power, insolence, lust and wrath, these individuals harbour hatred towards Me,who is situated within their own and in others’ bodies.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ahankāram balam darpam kāmam krodham cha sanśhritāḥ
mām ātma-para-deheṣhu pradviṣhanto ’bhyasūyakāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.18 (17 to 18)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.19: I constantly hurl these cruel and hateful individuals, who are vile and vicious among humankind, into inauspicious demoniac species birth after birth.
shlokaList.name: I constantly hurl these cruel and hateful individuals, who are vile and vicious among humankind, into inauspicious demoniac species birth after birth.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tān aham dviṣhataḥ krūrān sansāreṣhu narādhamān
kṣhipāmy ajasram aśhubhān āsurīṣhv eva yoniṣhu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.19 (19 to 20)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.20: Entering demoniac species birth after birth, deluded and failing to attain Me, they progressively devolve, O Kaunteya, into even more degraded states.
shlokaList.name: Entering demoniac species birth after birth, deluded and failing to attain Me, they progressively devolve, O Kaunteya, into even more degraded states.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
āsurīm yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmani
mām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamām gatim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.20 (19 to 20)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.21: There are three gateways to this hellish existence which leads to the degradation of the soul: lust, anger and greed. Therefore, one should renounce these three.
shlokaList.name: There are three gateways to this hellish existence which leads to the degradation of the soul: lust, anger and greed. Therefore, one should renounce these three.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tri-vidham narakasyedam dvāram nāśhanam ātmanaḥ
kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayam tyajet
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.21

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.22: O Kaunteya, the person who is freed from these three gates of darkness, practices what is beneficial for himself spiritually and thus attains the supreme goal.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya, the person who is freed from these three gates of darkness, practices what is beneficial for himself spiritually and thus attains the supreme goal.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
etair vimuktaḥ kaunteya tamo-dvārais tribhir naraḥ
ācharaty ātmanaḥ śhreyas tato yāti parām gatim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.22

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.23: He who acts under the impulse of desire, disregarding the injunctions of scriptures, neither attains perfection nor happiness nor the supreme goal.
shlokaList.name: He who acts under the impulse of desire, disregarding the injunctions of scriptures, neither attains perfection nor happiness nor the supreme goal.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yaḥ śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya vartate kāma-kārataḥ
na sa siddhim avāpnoti na sukham na parām gatim
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.23

shlokaList.description: Shloka 16.24: So let the scriptures be your authority in ascertaining what ought to be done and what ought not to be done. Having understood the teachings of the scriptures, perform your duties in this world accordingly.
shlokaList.name: So let the scriptures be your authority in ascertaining what ought to be done and what ought not to be done. Having understood the teachings of the scriptures, perform your duties in this world accordingly.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tasmāch chhāstram pramāṇam te kāryākārya-vyavasthitau
jñātvā śhāstra-vidhānoktam karma kartum ihārhasi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 16.24

chapterList.description: Chapter 17: Shraddha Traya Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Threefold Faith
chapterList.name: Chapter 17: Shraddha Traya Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Threefold Faith

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.1: Arjuna said - O Krishna, what is the state of those who neglect the injunctions of scriptures but continue to worship as per their faith? Is their faith characterised by the mode of goodness (Sattva), passion (Rajas) or ignorance (Tamas)?
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Krishna, what is the state of those who neglect the injunctions of scriptures but continue to worship as per their faith? Is their faith characterised by the mode of goodness (Sattva), passion (Rajas) or ignorance (Tamas)?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
ye śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya yajante śhraddhayānvitāḥ
teṣhām niṣhṭhā tu kā kṛiṣhṇa sattvam āho rajas tamaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.2: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - There are three types of faith in human beings that arise from their inherent nature. Their faith can be either in goodness, passion or ignorance. Now hear from Me about this.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - There are three types of faith in human beings that arise from their inherent nature. Their faith can be either in goodness, passion or ignorance. Now hear from Me about this.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
tri-vidhā bhavati śhraddhā dehinām sā svabhāva-jā
sāttvikī rājasī chaiva tāmasī cheti tām śhṛiṇu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.2 (2 to 3)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.3: O scion of Bharata! Everyone’s faith is shaped according to the existence that they have evolved to. All living beings possess faith and their nature of faith defines who they are.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata! Everyone’s faith is shaped according to the existence that they have evolved to. All living beings possess faith and their nature of faith defines who they are.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sattvānurūpā sarvasya śhraddhā bhavati bhārata
śhraddhā-mayo ‘yam puruṣho yo yach-chhraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.3 (2 to 3)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.4: Those whose nature is Sattvik, characterised by purity, worship the celestial Gods (devatas). Those of Rajasik nature, marked by activity and passion, worship demons. Those of Tamasik nature, associated with darkness, worship ghosts and spirits.
shlokaList.name: Those whose nature is Sattvik, characterised by purity, worship the celestial Gods (devatas). Those of Rajasik nature, marked by activity and passion, worship demons. Those of Tamasik nature, associated with darkness, worship ghosts and spirits.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yajante sāttvikā devān yakṣha-rakṣhānsi rājasāḥ
pretān bhūta-gaṇānśh chānye yajante tāmasā janāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.4

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.5: Those persons who perform severe austerities not sanctioned in the scriptures, doing them out of pride and egoism, driven by the forces of desire and anger;
shlokaList.name: Those persons who perform severe austerities not sanctioned in the scriptures, doing them out of pride and egoism, driven by the forces of desire and anger;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aśhāstra-vihitam ghoram tapyante ye tapo janāḥ
dambhāhankāra-sanyuktāḥ kāma-rāga-balānvitāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.5 (5 to 6)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.6: These foolish individuals torment not only the elements of their own body but also Me, who dwells within. Without a doubt, recognise them as possessing a demoniac nature.
shlokaList.name: These foolish individuals torment not only the elements of their own body but also Me, who dwells within. Without a doubt, recognise them as possessing a demoniac nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
karṣhayantaḥ śharīra-stham bhūta-grāmam achetasaḥ
mām chaivāntaḥ śharīra-stham tān viddhy āsura-niśhchayān
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.6 (5 to 6)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.7: Even people’s preferences for food are of three types, according to the three gunas.The same is true in case of sacrifices, austerities and charitable acts. Now listen, as I explain these distinctions to you.
shlokaList.name: Even people’s preferences for food are of three types, according to the three gunas.The same is true in case of sacrifices, austerities and charitable acts. Now listen, as I explain these distinctions to you.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
āhāras tv api sarvasya tri-vidho bhavati priyaḥ
yajñas tapas tathā dānam teṣhām bhedam imam śhṛiṇu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.7 (7 to 10)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.8: Those of Sattvik nature prefer foods that promote longevity, purity, strength, health, happiness and cheerfulness. They prefer foods that are delicious, nutritious, substantial and pleasing.
shlokaList.name: Those of Sattvik nature prefer foods that promote longevity, purity, strength, health, happiness and cheerfulness. They prefer foods that are delicious, nutritious, substantial and pleasing.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
āyuḥ-sattva-balārogya-sukha-prīti-vivardhanāḥ
rasyāḥ snigdhāḥ sthirā hṛidyā āhārāḥ sāttvika-priyāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.8 (7 to 10)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.9: Those with a Rajasik nature prefer foods that are bitter, sour, salty, very hot, spicy, dry and have a burning taste. Such foods often lead to discomfort, sorrow and illness.
shlokaList.name: Those with a Rajasik nature prefer foods that are bitter, sour, salty, very hot, spicy, dry and have a burning taste. Such foods often lead to discomfort, sorrow and illness.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kaṭv-amla-lavaṇāty-uṣhṇa- tīkṣhṇa-rūkṣha-vidāhinaḥ
āhārā rājasasyeṣhṭā duḥkha-śhokāmaya-pradāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.9 (7 to 10)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.10: Those of a Tamasik nature prefer stale, tasteless, rotten, leftover and impure foods.
shlokaList.name: Those of a Tamasik nature prefer stale, tasteless, rotten, leftover and impure foods.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yāta-yāmam gata-rasam pūti paryuṣhitam cha yat
uchchhiṣhṭam api chāmedhyam bhojanam tāmasa-priyam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.10 (7 to 10)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.11: The sacrifice performed according to scriptural rules, without desiring any personal gain from the sacrifice and seeing it purely as a duty is considered a Sattvik sacrifice.
shlokaList.name: The sacrifice performed according to scriptural rules, without desiring any personal gain from the sacrifice and seeing it purely as a duty is considered a Sattvik sacrifice.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aphalākāṅkṣhibhir yajño vidhi-driṣhṭo ya ijyate
yaṣhṭavyam eveti manaḥ samādhāya sa sāttvikaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.11 (11 to 13)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.12: O best of Bharatas! Sacrifice performed with the desire for personal gain or to enhance their pride is considered a Rajasik sacrifice.
shlokaList.name: O best of Bharatas! Sacrifice performed with the desire for personal gain or to enhance their pride is considered a Rajasik sacrifice.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
abhisandhāya tu phalam dambhārtham api chaiva yat
ijyate bharata-śhreṣhṭha tam yajñam viddhi rājasam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.12 (11 to 13)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.13: Sacrifice that is not based on scriptural guidelines, where no food is distributed, where no mantras are chanted, and no charity is given to the priests and is performed without faith is considered a Tamasik sacrifice.
shlokaList.name: Sacrifice that is not based on scriptural guidelines, where no food is distributed, where no mantras are chanted, and no charity is given to the priests and is performed without faith is considered a Tamasik sacrifice.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vidhi-hīnam asṛiṣhṭānnam mantra-hīnam adakṣhiṇam
śhraddhā-virahitam yajñam tāmasam parichakṣhate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.13 (11 to 13)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.14: Worship of the Supreme Being, brahmanas, spiritual teachers, elders and the wise; cleanliness; straightforwardness; observance of celibacy; and non-violence are considered as austerities of the body.
shlokaList.name: Worship of the Supreme Being, brahmanas, spiritual teachers, elders and the wise; cleanliness; straightforwardness; observance of celibacy; and non-violence are considered as austerities of the body.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
deva-dwija-guru-prājña- pūjanam śhaucham ārjavam
brahmacharyam ahinsā cha śhārīram tapa uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.14

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.15: Austerity of speech involves using words that do not cause agitation in others, are truthful, spoken with kindness and in goodwill; and also in reciting and studying scriptures.
shlokaList.name: Austerity of speech involves using words that do not cause agitation in others, are truthful, spoken with kindness and in goodwill; and also in reciting and studying scriptures.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
anudvega-karam vākyam satyam priya-hitam cha yat
svādhyāyābhyasanam chaiva vāṅ-mayam tapa uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.15

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.16: Tranquillity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-control and purity of heart are known as austerities of the mind.
shlokaList.name: Tranquillity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-control and purity of heart are known as austerities of the mind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
manaḥ-prasādaḥ saumyatvam maunam ātma-vinigrahaḥ
bhāva-sanśhuddhir ity etat tapo mānasam uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.16

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.17: These three types of austerities practiced by those with unwavering faith, free from desires for personal gain, for the sake of the Supreme are considered Sattvik in nature.
shlokaList.name: These three types of austerities practiced by those with unwavering faith, free from desires for personal gain, for the sake of the Supreme are considered Sattvik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhraddhayā parayā taptam tapas tat tri-vidham naraiḥ
aphalākāṅkṣhibhir yuktaiḥ sāttvikam parichakṣhate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.17 (17 to 19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.18: Austerity performed with pride to gain respect, honour and worship is considered Rajasik in nature. It is unstable and impermanent.
shlokaList.name: Austerity performed with pride to gain respect, honour and worship is considered Rajasik in nature. It is unstable and impermanent.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
satkāra-māna-pūjārtham tapo dambhena chaiva yat
kriyate tad iha proktam rājasam chalam adhruvam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.18 (17 to 19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.19: Austerity carried out with incorrect understanding, with self-inflicted suffering and with an intention to harm others is considered to be Tamasik in nature.
shlokaList.name: Austerity carried out with incorrect understanding, with self-inflicted suffering and with an intention to harm others is considered to be Tamasik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mūḍha-grāheṇātmano yat pīḍayā kriyate tapaḥ
parasyotsādanārtham vā tat tāmasam udāhṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.19 (17 to 19)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.20: Charity given without any expectation of return, viewed as a duty, offered at the right place and right time, to a deserving recipient is considered Sattvik in nature.
shlokaList.name: Charity given without any expectation of return, viewed as a duty, offered at the right place and right time, to a deserving recipient is considered Sattvik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dātavyam iti yad dānam dīyate ‘nupakāriṇe
deśhe kāle cha pātre cha tad dānam sāttvikam smṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.20 (20 to 22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.21: Charity done with the expectation of receiving somethingin return, or with a desire for personal gain, or given half-heartedly is considered Rajasik in nature.
shlokaList.name: Charity done with the expectation of receiving somethingin return, or with a desire for personal gain, or given half-heartedly is considered Rajasik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yat tu pratyupakārārtham phalam uddiśhya vā punaḥ
dīyate cha parikliṣhṭam tad dānam rājasam smṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.21 (20 to 22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.22: Charity that is performed at an unsuitable time and place, given to unworthy recipients without respect or honour is considered Tamasik in nature.
shlokaList.name: Charity that is performed at an unsuitable time and place, given to unworthy recipients without respect or honour is considered Tamasik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adeśha-kāle yad dānam apātrebhyaśh cha dīyate
asat-kṛitam avajñātam tat tāmasam udāhṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.22 (20 to 22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.23: ‘Om tat sat’ is considered a threefold symbolic representation of the Brahman. Through it, the Brahmanas, the Vedas and the sacrifices were ordained in the past.
shlokaList.name: ‘Om tat sat’ is considered a threefold symbolic representation of the Brahman. Through it, the Brahmanas, the Vedas and the sacrifices were ordained in the past.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
om tat sad iti nirdeśho brahmaṇas tri-vidhaḥ smṛitaḥ
brāhmaṇās tena vedāśh cha yajñāśh cha vihitāḥ purā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.23 (23 to 27)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.24: Therefore, by the utterance of ‘OM’, the acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity, which are supported by the scriptures are performed by those who know the Supreme Brahman.
shlokaList.name: Therefore, by the utterance of ‘OM’, the acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity, which are supported by the scriptures are performed by those who know the Supreme Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tasmād om ity udāhṛitya yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ
pravartante vidhānoktāḥ satatam brahma-vādinām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.24 (23 to 27)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.25: By the utterance of ‘tat’, various acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity are performed without desiring the fruits of action by those seeking liberation (moksha).
shlokaList.name: By the utterance of ‘tat’, various acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity are performed without desiring the fruits of action by those seeking liberation (moksha).
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tad ity anabhisandhāya phalam yajña-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ
dāna-kriyāśh cha vividhāḥ kriyante mokṣha-kāṅkṣhibhiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.25 (23 to 27)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.26: O Partha, the term ‘sat’ denotes eternal existence and goodness. It is also used to describe commendable or righteous actions.
shlokaList.name: O Partha, the term ‘sat’ denotes eternal existence and goodness. It is also used to describe commendable or righteous actions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve cha sad ity etat prayujyate
praśhaste karmaṇi tathā sach-chhabdaḥ pārtha yujyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.26 (23 to 27)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.27: Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity and charity is also considered ‘sat’ and actions performed to fulfil these duties are also deemed as ‘sat’.
shlokaList.name: Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity and charity is also considered ‘sat’ and actions performed to fulfil these duties are also deemed as ‘sat’.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yajñe tapasi dāne cha sthitiḥ sad iti chochyate
karma chaiva tad-arthīyam sad ity evābhidhīyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.27 (23 to 27)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 17.28: : O Partha! Whatever sacrifice, charity or austerity is performed without faith is deemed ‘asat’ or impermanent. Such actions hold no value in this life or the next.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! Whatever sacrifice, charity or austerity is performed without faith is deemed ‘asat’ or impermanent. Such actions hold no value in this life or the next.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aśhraddhayā hutam dattam tapas taptam kṛitam cha yat
asad ity uchyate pārtha na cha tat pretya no iha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 17.28

chapterList.description: Chapter 18: Moksha Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Liberation and Renunciation
chapterList.name: Chapter 18: Moksha Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Liberation and Renunciation

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.1: Arjuna said - O might-armed Krishna! O Hrishikesh! I wish to learn the true nature of sannyasa (renunciation of actions) and tyaag (relinquishment); and O killer of Kesi! I also seek to understand the distinction between the two.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O might-armed Krishna! O Hrishikesh! I wish to learn the true nature of sannyasa (renunciation of actions) and tyaag (relinquishment); and O killer of Kesi! I also seek to understand the distinction between the two.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
sannyāsasya mahā-bāho tattvam ichchhāmi veditum
tyāgasya cha hṛiṣhīkeśha pṛithak keśhi-niṣhūdana
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.1

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.2: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - The wise understand ‘sannyasa’ (order of life) as the complete renunciation of all actions performed for material rewards. And the experienced know ‘tyaag’ (renunciation) as renouncing the fruits of all actions.
shlokaList.name: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - The wise understand ‘sannyasa’ (order of life) as the complete renunciation of all actions performed for material rewards. And the experienced know ‘tyaag’ (renunciation) as renouncing the fruits of all actions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
kāmyānām karmaṇām nyāsam sannyāsam kavayo viduḥ
sarva-karma-phala-tyāgam prāhus tyāgam vichakṣhaṇāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.2

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.3: Some wise individuals say that all material actions are inherently evil and should be abandoned altogether, while others suggest that acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity should never be forsaken.
shlokaList.name: Some wise individuals say that all material actions are inherently evil and should be abandoned altogether, while others suggest that acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity should never be forsaken.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tyājyam doṣha-vad ity eke karma prāhur manīṣhiṇaḥ
yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyam iti chāpare
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.3 (3 to 5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.4: O best of the Bharatas! Listen to My conclusion about ‘tyaag’ (renunciation). O lion among men, renunciation is declared to be of three kinds.
shlokaList.name: O best of the Bharatas! Listen to My conclusion about ‘tyaag’ (renunciation). O lion among men, renunciation is declared to be of three kinds.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
niśhchayam śhṛiṇu me tatra tyāge bharata-sattama
tyāgo hi puruṣha-vyāghra tri-vidhaḥ samprakīrtitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.4 (3 to 5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.5: Acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity should never be abandoned; rather, they should be performed. In fact, these acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity purify even the greatly learned.
shlokaList.name: Acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity should never be abandoned; rather, they should be performed. In fact, these acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity purify even the greatly learned.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyam kāryam eva tat
yajño dānam tapaśh chaiva pāvanāni manīṣhiṇām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.5 (3 to 5)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.6: O Partha! All these actions should be performed without attachment or expectation of results and as a matter of duty. This is My ultimate opinion.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! All these actions should be performed without attachment or expectation of results and as a matter of duty. This is My ultimate opinion.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
etāny api tu karmāṇi saṅgam tyaktvā phalāni cha
kartavyānīti me pārtha niśhchitam matam uttamam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.6

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.7: However, abandoning prescribed duties out of delusion is considered Tamasik in nature. Prescribed duties should never be abandoned.
shlokaList.name: However, abandoning prescribed duties out of delusion is considered Tamasik in nature. Prescribed duties should never be abandoned.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
niyatasya tu sannyāsaḥ karmaṇo nopapadyate
mohāt tasya parityāgas tāmasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.7 (7 to 9)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.8: Someone who abandons their prescribed duty due to discomfort or fear of bodily distress, that renunciation is Rajasik by nature. He does not get any profit from such renunciation.
shlokaList.name: Someone who abandons their prescribed duty due to discomfort or fear of bodily distress, that renunciation is Rajasik by nature. He does not get any profit from such renunciation.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
duḥkham ity eva yat karma kāya-kleśha-bhayāt tyajet
sa kṛitvā rājasam tyāgam naiva tyāga-phalam labhet
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.8 (7 to 9)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.9: O Arjuna! Obligatory actions performed as a matter of duty, without any attachment and desire for the fruits of action is known as the Sattvik form of renunciation.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna! Obligatory actions performed as a matter of duty, without any attachment and desire for the fruits of action is known as the Sattvik form of renunciation.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kāryam ity eva yat karma niyatam kriyate ‘rjuna
saṅgam tyaktvā phalam chaiva sa tyāgaḥ sāttviko mataḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.9 (7 to 9)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.10: Endowed with the quality of Sattva and free from all doubts, an intelligent renouncer, neither detests disagreeable actions nor becomes attached to agreeable actions.
shlokaList.name: Endowed with the quality of Sattva and free from all doubts, an intelligent renouncer, neither detests disagreeable actions nor becomes attached to agreeable actions.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na dveṣhṭy akuśhalam karma kuśhale nānuṣhajjate
tyāgī sattva-samāviṣhṭo medhāvī chhinna-sanśhayaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.10 (10 to 11)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.11: An embodied person is incapable of renouncing actions entirely. However, one who renounces the fruits of actions is considered to be a true renouncer.
shlokaList.name: An embodied person is incapable of renouncing actions entirely. However, one who renounces the fruits of actions is considered to be a true renouncer.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na hi deha-bhṛitā śhakyam tyaktum karmāṇy aśheṣhataḥ
yas tu karma-phala-tyāgī sa tyāgīty abhidhīyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.11 (10 to 11)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.12: Those who are not renounced, after death they receive the results of their action which are of three types – whether they be good, bad or a mix of both. However, those who have given up attachment to the results of their action do not experience any such consequences.
shlokaList.name: Those who are not renounced, after death they receive the results of their action which are of three types – whether they be good, bad or a mix of both. However, those who have given up attachment to the results of their action do not experience any such consequences.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
aniṣhṭam iṣhṭam miśhram cha tri-vidham karmaṇaḥ phalam
bhavaty atyāginām pretya na tu sannyāsinām kvachit
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.12

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.13: O mighty-armed Arjuna! Understand from Me the five factors essential for the accomplishment of all actions, as described in the Sankhya philosophy.
shlokaList.name: O mighty-armed Arjuna! Understand from Me the five factors essential for the accomplishment of all actions, as described in the Sankhya philosophy.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pañchaitāni mahā-bāho kāraṇāni nibodha me
sānkhye kṛitānte proktāni siddhaye sarva-karmaṇām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.13 (13 to 14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.14: These five factors of action are: the body, the doer, various instruments (organs), diverse types of endeavours and destiny.
shlokaList.name: These five factors of action are the body, the doer, various instruments (organs), diverse types of endeavours and destiny.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adhiṣhṭhānam tathā kartā karaṇam cha pṛithag-vidham
vividhāśh cha pṛithak cheṣhṭā daivam chaivātra pañchamam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.14 (13 to 14)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.15: These five factors are the causes of all types of actions, whether good or bad, performed by a person through body, speeh or mind.
shlokaList.name: These five factors are the causes of all types of actions, whether good or bad, performed by a person through body, speeh or mind.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
harīra-vāṅ-manobhir yat karma prārabhate naraḥ
nyāyyam vā viparītam vā pañchaite tasya hetavaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.15 (15 to 17)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.16: However, those with undeveloped intelligence, perceive oneself as the only doer. Such people are not very intelligent and thus they fail to see the truth.
shlokaList.name: However, those with undeveloped intelligence, perceive oneself as the only doer. Such people are not very intelligent and thus they fail to see the truth.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tatraivam sati kartāram ātmānam kevalam tu yaḥ
paśhyaty akṛita-buddhitvān na sa paśhyati durmatiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.16 (15 to 17)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.17: A person who is free from egoism, whose intellect remains unattached, does not truly kill, even if he were to slay all these people. Nor do the results of such actions bind him.
shlokaList.name: A person who is free from egoism, whose intellect remains unattached, does not truly kill, even if he were to slay all these people. Nor do the results of such actions bind him.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yasya nāhankṛito bhāvo buddhir yasya na lipyate
hatvā ‘pi sa imāñl lokān na hanti na nibadhyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.17 (15 to 17)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.18: Three factors that motivate action are Gyana (knowledge), Gneya (the object of knowledge) and Parijnata (the knower). The three ingredients of action are Karana (the instrument, senses), Karma (work) and Karta (the doer).
shlokaList.name: Three factors that motivate action are Gyana (knowledge), Gneya (the object of knowledge) and Parijnata (the knower). The three ingredients of action are Karana (the instrument, senses), Karma (work) and Karta (the doer).
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jñānam jñeyam parijñātā tri-vidhā karma-chodanā
karaṇam karma karteti tri-vidhaḥ karma-saṅgrahaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.18

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.19: Based on the distinction of the three gunas there are three types of knowledge, action and the doer. Let Me explain them to you.
shlokaList.name: Based on the distinction of the three gunas there are three types of knowledge, action and the doer. Let Me explain them to you.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
jñānam karma cha kartā cha tridhaiva guṇa-bhedataḥ
prochyate guṇa-saṅkhyāne yathāvach chhṛiṇu tāny api
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.19

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.20: Know that knowledge as Sattvik-Gyana through which one perceives the one undivided spiritual existence equally present in all innumerable living beings.
shlokaList.name: Know that knowledge as Sattvik-Gyana through which one perceives the one undivided spiritual existence equally present in all innumerable living beings.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-bhūteṣhu yenaikam bhāvam avyayam īkṣhate
avibhaktam vibhakteṣhu taj jñānam viddhi sāttvikam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.20 (20 to 22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.21: Know that knowledge as Rajasik-Gyana through which one perceives multitudes of living beings in diverse bodies as separate and unconnected from each other.
shlokaList.name: Know that knowledge as Rajasik-Gyana through which one perceives multitudes of living beings in diverse bodies as separate and unconnected from each other.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pṛithaktvena tu yaj jñānam nānā-bhāvān pṛithag-vidhān
vetti sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu taj jñānam viddhi rājasam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.21 (20 to 22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.22: The knowledge that is trivial, not based on logic and binds one to just one type of action considering it to be the all in all, without any knowledge of reality is to be considered Tamasik-Gyana.
shlokaList.name: The knowledge that is trivial, not based on logic and binds one to just one type of action considering it to be the all in all, without any knowledge of reality is to be considered Tamasik-Gyana.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yat tu kṛitsna-vad ekasmin kārye saktam ahaitukam
atattvārtha-vad alpam cha tat tāmasam udāhṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.22 (20 to 22)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.23: The action that is performed as an obligatory duty, without any attachment, devoid of love or hate and without any desire for enjoying the results is considered Sattvik-Karma.
shlokaList.name: The action that is performed as an obligatory duty, without any attachment, devoid of love or hate and without any desire for enjoying the results is considered Sattvik-Karma.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
niyatam saṅga-rahitam arāga-dveṣhataḥ kṛitam
aphala-prepsunā karma yat tat sāttvikam uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.23 (23 to 25)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.24: But action performed with a desire for enjoying the results, involving great effort and driven by egoism is termed as Rajasik-Karma.
shlokaList.name: But action performed with a desire for enjoying the results, involving great effort and driven by egoism is termed as Rajasik-Karma.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yat tu kāmepsunā karma sāhankārena vā punaḥ
kriyate bahulāyāsam tad rājasam udāhṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.24 (23 to 25)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.25: The action that is undertaken out of delusion, without considering the future consequences or potential loss of wealth or life or harm to others or one’s ability to perform it is termed as Tamasik-Karma.
shlokaList.name: The action that is undertaken out of delusion, without considering the future consequences or potential loss of wealth or life or harm to others or one’s ability to perform it is termed as Tamasik-Karma.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
anubandham kṣhayam hinsām anapekṣhya cha pauruṣham
mohād ārabhyate karma yat tat tāmasam uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.25 (23 to 25)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.26: The doer who is free from attachments and egoism, endowed with determination and enthusiasm and remains even-minded in success or failure is known as a Sattvik-Karta.
shlokaList.name: The doer who is free from attachments and egoism, endowed with determination and enthusiasm and remains even-minded in success or failure is known as a Sattvik-Karta.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mukta-saṅgo ‘naham-vādī dhṛity-utsāha-samanvitaḥ
siddhy-asiddhyor nirvikāraḥ kartā sāttvika uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.26 (26 to 28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.27: The doer who is attached to worldly objects, desires the fruits of actions, is greedy, harmful, impure and affected by joy and sorrow is known as a Rajasik-Karta.
shlokaList.name: The doer who is attached to worldly objects, desires the fruits of actions, is greedy, harmful, impure and affected by joy and sorrow is known as a Rajasik-Karta.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rāgī karma-phala-prepsur lubdho hinsātmako ‘śhuchiḥ
harṣha-śhokānvitaḥ kartā rājasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.27 (26 to 28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.28: The doer who acts against the scriptures, is materialistic, stubborn, deceitful, vulgar, lazy, morose and dilatory is known as a Tamasik-Karta.
shlokaList.name: The doer who acts against the scriptures, is materialistic, stubborn, deceitful, vulgar, lazy, morose and dilatory is known as a Tamasik-Karta.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ayuktaḥ prākṛitaḥ stabdhaḥ śhaṭho naiṣhkṛitiko ‘lasaḥ
viṣhādī dīrgha-sūtrī cha kartā tāmasa uchyate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.28 (26 to 28)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.29: Intellect and determination are also categorised into three types based on the gunas. O Dhananjaya, listen to them carefully as I describe them distinctly and in detail.
shlokaList.name: Intellect and determination are also categorised into three types based on the gunas. O Dhananjaya, listen to them carefully as I describe them distinctly and in detail.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
buddher bhedam dhṛiteśh chaiva guṇatas tri-vidham śhṛiṇu
prochyamānam aśheṣheṇa pṛithaktvena dhanañjaya
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.29 (29 to 32)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.30: O Partha! The intellect that properly discerns between desirable action and undesirable action, what should be done and what should not be done, what should be feared and what should not be feared, what causes bondage and what causes freedom is known as Sattvik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! The intellect that properly discerns between desirable action and undesirable action, what should be done and what should not be done, what should be feared and what should not be feared, what causes bondage and what causes freedom is known as Sattvik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
pravṛittim cha nivṛittim cha kāryākārye bhayābhaye
bandham mokṣham cha yā vetti buddhiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.30 (29 to 32)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.31: O Partha! The intellect that fails to properly discern between right and wrong, what should be done and what should not be done, action and inaction is called Rajasik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! The intellect that fails to properly discern between right and wrong, what should be done and what should not be done, action and inaction is called Rajasik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yayā dharmam adharmam cha kāryam chākāryam eva cha
ayathāvat prajānāti buddhiḥ sā pārtha rājasī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.31 (29 to 32)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.32: O Partha! The intellect which is covered by ignorance, considers the wrong as the right and sees everything in a perverted way, that intellect is known as Tamasik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! The intellect which is covered by ignorance, considers the wrong as the right and sees everything in a perverted way, that intellect is known as Tamasik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adharmam dharmam iti yā manyate tamasāvṛitā
sarvārthān viparītānśh cha buddhiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.32 (29 to 32)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.33: The determination that is sustained by constant practice of Yoga and which controls the activities of the mind, vital force and senses, O Partha, that determination is Sattvik in nature.
shlokaList.name: The determination that is sustained by constant practice of Yoga and which controls the activities of the mind, vital force and senses, O Partha, that determination is Sattvik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
dhṛityā yayā dhārayate manaḥ-prāṇendriya-kriyāḥ
yogenāvyabhichāriṇyā dhṛitiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.33 (33 to 35)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.34: O Arjuna! However, the determination by which a person performs his duties, seeks wealth and pleasure with attachment and a desire to enjoy the fruits of his actions, O Partha, that determination is Rajasik in nature.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna! However, the determination by which a person performs his duties, seeks wealth and pleasure with attachment and a desire to enjoy the fruits of his actions, O Partha, that determination is Rajasik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yayā tu dharma-kāmārthān dhṛityā dhārayate ‘rjuna
prasaṅgena phalākāṅkṣhī dhṛitiḥ sā pārtha rājasī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.34 (33 to 35)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.35: O Partha! The determination due to which an ignorant person is unable to give up dreaming, fearfulness, grief, despondency and illusion, that determination is Tamasik in nature.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! The determination due to which an ignorant person is unable to give up dreaming, fearfulness, grief, despondency and illusion, that determination is Tamasik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yayā svapnam bhayam śhokam viṣhādam madam eva cha
na vimuñchati durmedhā dhṛitiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.35 (33 to 35)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.36: O best of the Bharatas! Now hear from Me about three kinds of happiness, practicing which a living being enjoys and can even end his distress.
shlokaList.name: O best of the Bharatas! Now hear from Me about three kinds of happiness, practicing which a living being enjoys and can even end his distress.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sukham tv idānīm tri-vidham śhṛiṇu me bharatarṣhabha
abhyāsād ramate yatra duḥkhāntam cha nigachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.36 (36 to 39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.37: O Partha! The intellect that fails to properly discern between right and wrong, what should be done and what should not be done, action and inaction is called Rajasik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! The intellect that fails to properly discern between right and wrong, what should be done and what should not be done, action and inaction is called Rajasik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yat tad agre viṣham iva pariṇāme ‘mṛitopamam
tat sukham sāttvikam proktam ātma-buddhi-prasāda-jam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.37 (36 to 39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.38: O Partha! The intellect which is covered by ignorance, considers the wrong as the right and sees everything in a perverted way, that intellect is known as Tamasik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.name: O Partha! The intellect which is covered by ignorance, considers the wrong as the right and sees everything in a perverted way, that intellect is known as Tamasik-Buddhi.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
viṣhayendriya-sanyogād yat tad agre ’mṛitopamam
pariṇāme viṣham iva tat sukham rājasam smṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.38 (36 to 39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.39: And the happiness that deludes one from beginning till the end, arising from sleep, laziness and negligence–that happiness is Tamasik in nature.
shlokaList.name: And the happiness that deludes one from beginning till the end, arising from sleep, laziness and negligence–that happiness is Tamasik in nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yad agre chānubandhe cha sukham mohanam ātmanaḥ
nidrālasya-pramādottham tat tāmasam udāhṛitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.39 (36 to 39)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.40: No being on earth, in heaven or among the celestial beings is free from the influence of the three gunas (qualities) that arise from Prakriti (material nature).
shlokaList.name: No being on earth, in heaven or among the celestial beings is free from the influence of the three gunas (qualities) that arise from Prakriti (material nature).
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na tad asti pṛithivyām vā divi deveṣhu vā punaḥ
sattvam prakṛiti-jair muktam yad ebhiḥ syāt tribhir guṇaiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.40

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.41: O Parantapa! The duties of Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas as well as Sudras have been divided according to their natural qualities acquired according to the gunas they are influenced by.
shlokaList.name: O Parantapa! The duties of Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas as well as Sudras have been divided according to their natural qualities acquired according to the gunas they are influenced by.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
brāhmaṇa-kṣhatriya-viśhām śhūdrāṇām cha parantapa
karmāṇi pravibhaktāni svabhāva-prabhavair guṇaiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.41

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.42: Restraint of mind, control of senses, austerity, purity, forbearance, simplicity, knowledge, wisdom, and theism–these are the natural qualities of Brahmanas by which they work.
shlokaList.name: Restraint of mind, control of senses, austerity, purity, forbearance, simplicity, knowledge, wisdom, and theism–these are the natural qualities of Brahmanas by which they work.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhamo damas tapaḥ śhaucham kṣhāntir ārjavam eva cha
jñānam vijñānam āstikyam brahma-karma svabhāva-jam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.42

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.43: Valour, vigour, determination, skill, not turning away from battle, charity and leadership: these are the natural qualities of Kshatriya by which they work.
shlokaList.name: Valour, vigour, determination, skill, not turning away from battle, charity and leadership, these are the natural qualities of Kshatriya by which they work.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhauryam tejo dhṛitir dākṣhyam yuddhe chāpy apalāyanam
dānam īśhvara-bhāvaśh cha kṣhātram karma svabhāva-jam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.43

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.44: Agriculture, cattle-rearing and trade are the natural qualities of Vaishyas by which they work. The act of giving various kinds of service to others is the natural quality of Sudras by which they work.
shlokaList.name: Agriculture, cattle-rearing and trade are the natural qualities of Vaishyas by which they work. The act of giving various kinds of service to others is the natural quality of Sudras by which they work.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kṛiṣhi-gau-rakṣhya-vāṇijyam vaiśhya-karma svabhāva-jam
paricharyātmakam karma śhūdrasyāpi svabhāva-jam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.44

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.45: One who is deeply devoted to their own duty achieves perfection. Now, let Me explain how one attains perfection through steadfast dedication to their duty.
shlokaList.name: One who is deeply devoted to their own duty achieves perfection. Now, let Me explain how one attains perfection through steadfast dedication to their duty.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sve sve karmaṇy abhirataḥ sansiddhim labhate naraḥ
sva-karma-nirataḥ siddhim yathā vindati tach chhṛiṇu
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.45 (45 to 47)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.46: A person achieves perfection by worshipping the Supreme Being, from whom all living beings arise and by whom this entire universe is pervaded, through the performance of one’s natural duties.
shlokaList.name: A person achieves perfection by worshipping the Supreme Being, from whom all living beings arise and by whom this entire universe is pervaded, through the performance of one’s natural duties.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yataḥ pravṛittir bhūtānām yena sarvam idam tatam
sva-karmaṇā tam abhyarchya siddhim vindati mānavaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.46 (45 to 47)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.47: One’s own duty, even if imperfectly executed, is better than perfectly executing another’s duty. A man does not incur sin when he performs duty according to his own nature.
shlokaList.name: One’s own duty, even if imperfectly executed, is better than perfectly executing another’s duty. A man does not incur sin when he performs duty according to his own nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhreyān swa-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣhṭhitāt
svabhāva-niyatam karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣham
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.47 (45 to 47)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.48: O Kaunteya! One should not abandon their inherent duty, even if it is imperfect, because all actions are shrouded in some imperfections, just as fire is enveloped by smoke.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! One should not abandon their inherent duty, even if it is imperfect, because all actions are shrouded in some imperfections, just as fire is enveloped by smoke.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
saha-jam karma kaunteya sa-doṣham api na tyajet
sarvārambhā hi doṣheṇa dhūmenāgnir ivāvṛitāḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.48

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.49: One who possesses an intellect completely detached, is self-controlled and free from desires, achieves the supreme perfection of inaction by practicing renunciation.
shlokaList.name: One who possesses an intellect completely detached, is self-controlled and free from desires, achieves the supreme perfection of inaction by practicing renunciation.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
asakta-buddhiḥ sarvatra jitātmā vigata-spṛihaḥ
naiṣhkarmya-siddhim paramām sannyāsenādhigachchhati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.49 (49 to 50)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.50: O Kaunteya! Listen to Me as I briefly explain how a person who has attained perfection of inaction can also attain Brahman, which is the ultimate stage of transcendental knowledge.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! Listen to Me as I briefly explain how a person who has attained perfection of inaction can also attain Brahman, which is the ultimate stage of transcendental knowledge.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
siddhim prāpto yathā brahma tathāpnoti nibodha me
samāsenaiva kaunteya niṣhṭhā jñānasya yā parā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.50 (49 to 50)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.51: One who remains endowed with pure intellect, controls his senses through determined practice, rejects sound and other sense-objects, gives up infatuation and hatred;
shlokaList.name: One who remains endowed with pure intellect, controls his senses through determined practice, rejects sound and other sense-objects, gives up infatuation and hatred;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
buddhyā viśhuddhayā yukto dhṛityātmānam niyamya cha
śhabdādīn viṣhayāns tyaktvā rāga-dveṣhau vyudasya cha
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.51 (51 to 53)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.52: (Who) lives in a solitary place, eats little, restrains his speech, body and mind, remains ever-engrossed in meditation and who has taken shelter of detachment;
shlokaList.name: (Who) lives in a solitary place, eats little, restrains his speech, body and mind, remains ever-engrossed in meditation and who has taken shelter of detachment;
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vivikta-sevī laghv-āśhī yata-vāk-kāya-mānasaḥ
dhyāna-yoga-paro nityam vairāgyam samupāśhritaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.52 (51 to 53)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.53: (Who) gives up vanity, show of strength, arrogance, desire, anger and possessions, and remains free from the sense of possessiveness and ever tranquil in mind, such a person attains to the stage of Brahman.
shlokaList.name: (Who) gives up vanity, show of strength, arrogance, desire, anger and possessions, and remains free from the sense of possessiveness and ever tranquil in mind, such a person attains to the stage of Brahman.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ahankāram balam darpam kāmam krodham parigraham
vimuchya nirmamaḥ śhānto brahma-bhūyāya kalpate
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.53 (51 to 53)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.54: One who attains the stage of Brahman, remains tranquil in mind, neither grieving nor desiring, seeing all beings equally, such a person attains supreme devotion to Me.
shlokaList.name: One who attains the stage of Brahman, remains tranquil in mind, neither grieving nor desiring, seeing all beings equally, such a person attains supreme devotion to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
brahma-bhūtaḥ prasannātmā na śhochati na kāṅkṣhati
samaḥ sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu mad-bhaktim labhate parām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.54

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.55: One can understand Me as I am in truth only by devotion. Then knowing Me in truth, one can come to Me.
shlokaList.name: One can understand Me as I am in truth only by devotion. Then knowing Me in truth, one can come to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वत: |
ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् || 55||
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.55

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.56: Even though continuously performing all duties, one taking refuge in Me, attains the eternal,imperishable abode by My grace.
shlokaList.name: Even though continuously performing all duties, one taking refuge in Me, attains the eternal,imperishable abode by My grace.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-karmāṇy api sadā kurvāṇo mad-vyapāśhrayaḥ
mat-prasādād avāpnoti śhāśhvatam padam avyayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.56

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.57: By using your intelligence, surrender all your actions to Me and be wholly devoted to Me. And by taking to the path of buddhi yoga, keep your mind focused solely on Me at all times.
shlokaList.name: By using your intelligence, surrender all your actions to Me and be wholly devoted to Me. And by taking to the path of buddhi yoga, keep your mind focused solely on Me at all times.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
chetasā sarva-karmāṇi mayi sannyasya mat-paraḥ
buddhi-yogam upāśhritya mach-chittaḥ satatam bhava
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.57

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.58: If your mind remains completely focused on Me, you will overcome all difficulties through My grace. But if you act out of pride and do not heed My words, you will perish.
shlokaList.name: If your mind remains completely focused on Me, you will overcome all difficulties through My grace. But if you act out of pride and do not heed My words, you will perish.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
mach-chittaḥ sarva-durgāṇi mat-prasādāt tariṣhyasi
atha chet tvam ahankārān na śhroṣhyasi vinaṅkṣhyasi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.58

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.59: If, indulging in pride, you think “I will not fight”, then your resolve is futile. Because your nature as a kshatriya will drive you to fight.
shlokaList.name: If, indulging in pride, you think “I will not fight”, then your resolve is futile. Because your nature as a kshatriya will drive you to fight.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yad ahankāram āśhritya na yotsya iti manyase
mithyaiṣha vyavasāyas te prakṛitis tvām niyokṣhyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.59

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.60: O Kaunteya! Due to delusion you do not wish to perform actions which are inherent to your nature. But, you will inevitably do them being driven by your own inherent nature.
shlokaList.name: O Kaunteya! Due to delusion you do not wish to perform actions which are inherent to your nature. But, you will inevitably do them being driven by your own inherent nature.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
swbhāva-jena kaunteya nibaddhaḥ svena karmaṇā
kartum nechchhasi yan mohāt kariṣhyasy avaśho ’pi tat
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.60

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.61: O Arjuna! The Supreme controller dwelling within the hearts of all living beings, directs their movement, who are as if seated on a machine made of the material energy.
shlokaList.name: O Arjuna! The Supreme controller dwelling within the hearts of all living beings, directs their movement, who are as if seated on a machine made of the material energy.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
īśhvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānām hṛid-deśhe ‘rjuna tiṣhṭhati
bhrāmayan sarva-bhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.61

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.62: O scion of Bharata! Completely take refuge in Him; through His grace, you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata! Completely take refuge in Him; through His grace, you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tam eva śharaṇam gachchha sarva-bhāvena bhārata
tat-prasādāt parām śhāntim sthānam prāpsyasi śhāśhvatam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.62

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.63: I have revealed to you this wisdom, which is more secret than all secrets. Reflect on it carefully and decide for yourself what you think is best to do.
shlokaList.name: I have revealed to you this wisdom, which is more secret than all secrets. Reflect on it carefully and decide for yourself what you think is best to do.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
iti te jñānam ākhyātam guhyād guhyataram mayā
vimṛiśhyaitad aśheṣheṇa yathechchhasi tathā kuru
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.63

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.64: Listen once more to My supreme word, which is supremely confidential. Because you are very dear to Me, I will speak about this for your benefit.
shlokaList.name: Listen once more to My supreme word, which is supremely confidential. Because you are very dear to Me, I will speak about this for your benefit.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-guhyatamam bhūyaḥ śhṛiṇu me paramam vachaḥ
iṣhṭo ‘si me dṛiḍham iti tato vakṣhyāmi te hitam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.64

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.65: Absorb your mind in Me, become My devotee, worship Me and pay homage to Me. I promise that in this way, you will surely reach Me, for you are very dear to Me.
shlokaList.name: Absorb your mind in Me, become My devotee, worship Me and pay homage to Me. I promise that in this way, you will surely reach Me, for you are very dear to Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī mām namaskuru
mām evaiṣhyasi satyam te pratijāne priyo ‘si me
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.65

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.66: Abandoning all other duties, seek refuge in Me alone. I will liberate you from all sinful reactions. Do not worry.
shlokaList.name: Abandoning all other duties, seek refuge in Me alone. I will liberate you from all sinful reactions. Do not worry.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekam śharaṇam vraja
aham tvām sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.66

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.67: This wisdom should never be revealed to someone who does not perform austerity, who is not My devotee, who does not wish to hear and who bears malice towards Me.
shlokaList.name: This wisdom should never be revealed to someone who does not perform austerity, who is not My devotee, who does not wish to hear and who bears malice towards Me.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
idam te nātapaskāya nābhaktāya kadāchana
na chāśhuśhrūṣhave vāchyam na cha mām yo ‘bhyasūyati
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.67

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.68: O scion of Bharata! Completely take refuge in Him; through His grace, you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.
shlokaList.name: O scion of Bharata! Completely take refuge in Him; through His grace, you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
ya idam paramam guhyam mad-bhakteṣhv abhidhāsyati
bhaktim mayi parām kṛitvā mām evaiṣhyaty asanśhayaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.68 (68 to 69)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.69: There is none among men who is more dear to Me in this world nor shall anyone else be more dearer to Me than one who shares this knowledge with others.
shlokaList.name: There is none among men who is more dear to Me in this world nor shall anyone else be more dearer to Me than one who shares this knowledge with others.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
na cha tasmān manuṣhyeṣhu kaśhchin me priya-kṛittamaḥ
bhavitā na cha me tasmād anyaḥ priyataro bhuvi
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.69 (68 to 69)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.70: I declare that those who study this sacred dialogue between us will worship Me with their intellect, through the sacrifice of knowledge; this is My conclusive opinion.
shlokaList.name: I declare that those who study this sacred dialogue between us will worship Me with their intellect, through the sacrifice of knowledge; this is My conclusive opinion.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
adhyeṣhyate cha ya imam dharmyam samvādam āvayoḥ
jñāna-yajñena tenāham iṣhṭaḥ syām iti me matiḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.70 (70 to 71)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.71: And the person who listens with faith and without malice will also be liberated from sins and attain the joyful realms of the righteous.
shlokaList.name: And the person who listens with faith and without malice will also be liberated from sins and attain the joyful realms of the righteous.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
śhraddhāvān anasūyaśh cha śhṛiṇuyād api yo naraḥ
so ‘pi muktaḥ śhubhāñl lokān prāpnuyāt puṇya-karmaṇām
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.71 (70 to 71)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.72: O Partha! Have you heard My teachings with complete concentration? O Dhananjaya, has your delusion caused by ignorance been destroyed?
shlokaList.name: O Partha! Have you heard My teachings with complete concentration? O Dhananjaya, has your delusion caused by ignorance been destroyed?
shlokaList.sanskritText:
kachchid etach chhrutam pārtha tvayaikāgreṇa chetasā
kachchid ajñāna-sammohaḥ pranaṣhṭas te dhanañjaya
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.72

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.73: Arjuna said - O Acyuta (the infallible one)! By your grace, my delusion has been dispelled and I have gained the knowledge to discern right from wrong. I am now steady and free from doubts. I will act in accordance with your guidance.
shlokaList.name: Arjuna said - O Acyuta (the infallible one)! By your grace, my delusion has been dispelled and I have gained the knowledge to discern right from wrong. I am now steady and free from doubts. I will act in accordance with your guidance.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
naṣhṭo mohaḥ smṛitir labdhā tvat-prasādān mayāchyuta
sthito ‘smi gata-sandehaḥ kariṣhye vachanam tava
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.73

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.74: Sanjaya said - Thus, I have listened to this marvellous and profound dialogue between great personalities - Vasudeva and Partha. Hearing this wonderful dialogue has made my hair stand on end.
shlokaList.name: Sanjaya said - Thus, I have listened to this marvellous and profound dialogue between great personalities - Vasudeva and Partha. Hearing this wonderful dialogue has made my hair stand on end.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
sañjaya uvācha
ity aham vāsudevasya pārthasya cha mahātmanaḥ
samvādam imam aśhrauṣham adbhutam roma-harṣhaṇam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.74 (74 to 77)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.75: By the grace of Vyasa, I have received this supreme and most profound knowledge of yoga directly from the Lord of Yoga, Sri Krishna.
shlokaList.name: By the grace of Vyasa, I have received this supreme and most profound knowledge of yoga directly from the Lord of Yoga, Sri Krishna.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
vyāsa-prasādāch chhrutavān etad guhyam aham param
yogam yogeśhvarāt kṛiṣhṇāt sākṣhāt kathayataḥ svayam
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.75 (74 to 77)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.76: O King! Repeatedly recalling this sacred and marvellous conversation between Keshava and Arjuna fills me with joy anew each time.
shlokaList.name: O King! Repeatedly recalling this sacred and marvellous conversation between Keshava and Arjuna fills me with joy anew each time.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
rājan sansmṛitya sansmṛitya samvādam imam adbhutam
keśhavārjunayoḥ puṇyam hṛiṣhyāmi cha muhur muhuḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.76 (74 to 77)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.77: O King! Reflecting repeatedly on the wonderful form of Lord Krishna, is making me astonished and fills me with joy each time.
shlokaList.name: O King! Reflecting repeatedly on the wonderful form of Lord Krishna, is making me astonished and fills me with joy each time.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
tach cha sansmṛitya sansmṛitya rūpam aty-adbhutam hareḥ
vismayo ye mahān rājan hṛiṣhyāmi cha punaḥ punaḥ
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.77 (74 to 77)

shlokaList.description: Shloka 18.78: Wherever Lord Krishna, the Lord of Yoga and the great archer Arjuna are present, there will certainly be fortune, victory, glory and righteousness. This is my conclusive opinion.
shlokaList.name: Wherever Lord Krishna, the Lord of Yoga and the great archer Arjuna are present, there will certainly be fortune, victory, glory and righteousness. This is my conclusive opinion.
shlokaList.sanskritText:
yatra yogeśhvaraḥ kṛiṣhṇo yatra pārtho dhanur-dharaḥ
tatra śhrīr vijayo bhūtir dhruvā nītir matir mama
shlokaList.title: Shloka 18.78
